issues.title: [Feat]: Call/Meeting in separate window
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

When you are in a call or in meeting mode, open the separate screen

### Motivation

It's very frustrating when you're in a meeting where you need to monitor screen sharing, polling, and other interactive features, having to minimize the meeting and miss these events in order to respond to messages. Often, when I'm in the war room, I need to be involved in other chats and end up missing some situations because the screen is minimized.

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

I also saw the same complaint in the post: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1507

Add support for a global /etc/chromium/config.json file.

sys admins may have to set non default config for teams for a given site.

said config.json should be read before users config.json to allow users to customize their own config.

issues.title: docs: documentation improvements
issues.body:
## Summary

This PR delivers a full re-organization and expansion of the **teams-for-linux** documentation. All changes are documentation-only and introduce:

- A deep, consolidated **Configuration Guide** with every CLI flag, environment variable, JSON schema and live examples.
- **Module-level READMEs** across the `app/` directory—each subfolder now has up-to-date usage, API and troubleshooting notes.
- A **High-Level Architecture** section under `docs/architecture/overview.md` complete with component and sequence diagrams.
- An enriched **Troubleshooting / Known Issues** doc (`KNOWN_ISSUES.md`) covering packaging pitfalls, dark-mode quirks, notification failures, key-binding mismatches, etc.
- New **Planning & Analysis** write-ups:
  - `docs/PRD_Documentation_Improvements.md` (project requirements and breakdown)
  - `docs/github_issues_analysis_report.md` (detailed issue triage and solutions)
- Updated **Copilot Instructions** in `.github/copilot-instructions.md` reflecting our latest doc-generation patterns.
- Removal of obsolete or duplicated guides no longer relevant under the new structure.

## What’s Changed

```text
docs/
├── configuration.md                # All config options, JSON schema, examples
├── architecture/
│   ├── overview.md                 # System components & sequence diagrams
│   └── components.md               # Component responsibilities
├── PRD_Documentation_Improvements.md
├── github_issues_analysis_report.md
├── KNOWN_ISSUES.md
└── (other walkthroughs & troubleshooting pages)
app/
├── main/README.md                  # Updated usage & module API
├── services/README.md
├── utils/README.md
└── … (each subfolder README refreshed)
.github/
└── copilot-instructions.md         # Aligned with new docs flow
```

## Impact

- 📑 Documentation only—no code changes or breaking effects on the application.  
- ✅ All Markdown compiles and links correctly in our MkDocs/GitHub view.  
- ✅ Confirms that module READMEs, config examples, and diagrams render as intended.

## Next Steps

Future PRs will tackle:

1. Extracting shared logic (`processArgs`, config loaders)  
2. Refining private API and JS patterns (consistent `const`/`let`, `async`/`await`)  
3. Strengthening error handling, logging, and CI integrations  
4. Expanding our Knowledge Base with real-world issue downloads & case studies

---

Please let me know if any deletions need restoring or if any files were missed!

issues.title: feat: Implement cache management and Debian changelog generation (Issues #1756, #1691)
issues.body:
## Summary

This PR implements two major features and includes dependency updates:

### 🧹 Cache Management (Issue #1756)
- **Problem**: Daily logout issues caused by cache overflow corrupting OAuth tokens
- **Solution**: Configurable automatic cache cleanup system
- **Default**: Disabled by default, requires explicit opt-in via configuration
- **Location**: `app/cacheManager/`

~~### 📋 Debian Changelog Generation (Issue #1691)~~ (reverted in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/1766)

### 📦 Dependency Updates

#### 🚀 Electron 37.2.0 → 37.2.4 Upgrade Benefits
**Key fixes that may resolve existing issues:**

> ⚠️ **Note**: The following correlations are based on Electron changelog analysis. We encourage users experiencing these issues to test this version and provide feedback on whether the problems are resolved.

- **Issue #1757** (App automatically closes): **May be fixed** by window close callback crash fixes
  - Related Electron fixes: Window close callback improvements in v37.2.4
  - **Please test and report**: Users experiencing random app closures

- **Issue #1762** (Screen sharing blurry): **May be improved** by desktop capture enhancements  
  - Related Electron fixes: Desktop capture crash fixes in v37.2.1 + Chromium 138.0.7204.157 in v37.2.4
  - **Please test and report**: Users experiencing poor screen sharing quality

- **Issue #1743** (Cannot share screen): **May be fixed** by desktop capture crash fixes
  - Related Electron fixes: Fixed desktop capture crashes in renderer processes (v37.2.1)
  - **Please test and report**: Users unable to share screen

- **Issue #1715** (Can't share screen - pipewire): **May be improved** by better Linux compatibility
  - Related Electron fixes: Enhanced desktop capture and Linux integration improvements
  - **Please test and report**: Users with PipeWire screen sharing issues

- **Issue #1736** (Window resizing broken): **May be fixed** by window management improvements
  - Related Electron fixes: Window callback and management stability improvements in v37.2.4
  - **Please test and report**: Users experiencing window resize/maximize problems

**Technical improvements from Electron 37.2.1-37.2.4:**
- Fixed window close callback crashes that caused unexpected app termination
- Enhanced desktop capture with reduced crashes and better quality
- Updated Chromium to 138.0.7204.157 for security and performance
- Improved child process crash handling and CSS rule processing
- Better Linux desktop environment integration

**Electron Release References:**
- [Electron v37.2.4 Release Notes](https://github.com/electron/electron/releases/tag/v37.2.4)
- [Electron v37.2.3 Release Notes](https://github.com/electron/electron/releases/tag/v37.2.3)
- [Electron v37.2.2 Release Notes](https://github.com/electron/electron/releases/tag/v37.2.2)
- [Electron v37.2.1 Release Notes](https://github.com/electron/electron/releases/tag/v37.2.1)

#### 🔧 Other Dependencies
- **ESLint**: Updated to 9.32.0 for better linting
- **electron-builder**: Updated to 26.0.19 for improved builds  
- **@homebridge/dbus-native**: Updated to 0.7.2 for ARM compatibility

## Features Details

### Cache Management
- **Configurable**: Users can set cache size limits and check intervals
- **Safe**: Preserves authentication tokens while cleaning temporary files
- **Automatic**: Monitors cache size and cleans when threshold exceeded
- **Logging**: Debug-level logging for monitoring cache activities

**Configuration Example:**
```json
{
  "cacheManagement": {
    "enabled": true,
    "maxCacheSizeMB": 300,
    "cacheCheckIntervalMs": 3600000
  }
}
```

## Files Added
- `app/cacheManager/index.js` - Cache management implementation
- `app/cacheManager/README.md` - Documentation and usage guide
- `scripts/generateDebianChangelog.js` - Changelog generator

## Files Modified
- `app/index.js` - Cache manager integration
- `app/config/index.js` - Added cacheManagement configuration option
- `app/config/README.md` - Updated configuration documentation
- `package.json` - Dependency updates and FPM changelog config
- `package-lock.json` - Lock file updates
- `scripts/afterpack.js` - Debian changelog build integration
- `com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux.appdata.xml` - Updated release notes

## Testing

- ✅ All lint checks pass
- ✅ Cache management defaults to disabled (requires explicit opt-in)
~~- ✅ Debian changelog generation works correctly~~
- ✅ Build configuration validates successfully
- ✅ No security vulnerabilities introduced
- ✅ Electron upgrade addresses multiple existing issues

## Migration Guide

### For Users Experiencing Daily Logouts
Add to your config file:
```json
{
  "cacheManagement": {
    "enabled": true
  }
}
```

## Community Feedback Requested

**🔍 Testing Needed**: We believe the Electron upgrade may resolve several existing issues, but we need community validation:

1. **Issue #1757 users**: Please test if the app still closes unexpectedly
2. **Issue #1762 users**: Please test if screen sharing quality has improved
3. **Issue #1743 users**: Please test if you can now share your screen
4. **Issue #1715 users**: Please test if PipeWire screen sharing now works
5. **Issue #1736 users**: Please test if window resizing/maximizing works properly

Please comment on the respective issues with your testing results after this release.

## Related Issues

- Fixes #1756 - Daily logout due to cache overflow
- May fix #1757 - App automatically closes (Electron window crash fixes)
- May improve #1762 - Screen sharing is blurry (Desktop capture + Chromium updates)
- May fix #1743 - Cannot share screen (Desktop capture crash fixes)
- May improve #1715 - Can't share screen pipewire (Better Linux compatibility)
- May fix #1736 - Window resizing is broken (Window management improvements)
- Updates dependencies for stability and security

## Review Notes

- Cache management is feature-flagged and disabled by default
- Debian changelog follows proper Debian packaging standards
- Electron upgrade provides significant stability improvements for window management and screen sharing
- All changes maintain existing functionality while adding new capabilities
- Comprehensive documentation included for both features
- Community testing requested for Electron-related issue fixes

issues.title: [Bug]: Screen sharing is blurry
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

Screen sharing is blurry. Using the PWA, when I attempt to share my screen in meetings, it is often blurry despite having a strong wired internet connection.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.1.0

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Given I am loggeded into Teams for linux PWA
2.And I am connected to a strong wired internet connection
3.When I am in a meeting
4.And I share the entire screen
5.And I select Quality:4K
6.Then my team members complain that my screen is too blurry to read
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.Given I am loggeded into Teams for linux PWA
2.And I am connected to a strong wired internet connection
3.When I am in a meeting
4.And I share the entire screen
5.And I select Quality:4K
6.Then my screenshare is pristine and clear
```

### Debug
N/a

### Additional context
_No response_

issues.title: [Feat]: Increase the ringtone volume
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Increase the ringtone volume or add the ability to change the ringtone volume.

### Motivation

First of all, thank you for all the hard work you’ve put into creating and maintaining this fantastic app.

I’m having an issue with notification sounds (call and message alerts): they’re too quiet. Ideally, I’d like to adjust the notification (_ringtone_) volume _independently_ of the other party’s voice volume.

So far, I’ve tried two workarounds, but neither is ideal:
1. Increase Chromium volume (as described in #1136)
But his boosts _all_ Teams sounds—ringtone, notifications and in‑call audio alike. While the ringtone becomes louder as expected, the other party’s voice also gets amplified, forcing me to lower my system volume during calls and remember to raise it again afterwards. This is inconvenient.

2. Replace/amend the ringtone file
This requires repackaging the distro or writing custom scripts, which is complex, fragile, and impractical for non‑experts.

It would be great if you could add a feature to control notification volume _separately_ (e.g. include a louder ringtone—e.g. 1.5× the current level, or other solution) in the application. Thank you!

The issue is observed in the following environment: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS amd46 with the latest updates, teams-for-linux 2.1.0 (also in 2.0.x versions).

### Alternatives

_No response_

issues.title: [Solution] Wayland: fix team scaling on HiDPI + scaling/fractional scaling
issues.body: Hi, I am using this project under [sway](https://github.com/swaywm/sway)/wayland. Also using HiDPI with scaling (eg fractional scale). This is leading to problems with ugly/fuzzy fonts when running on such display. To cure this problem I removed `/usr/bin/teams-for-linux` symlink and put a file there with the following contents: ```bash #!/bin/sh exec /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --ozone-platform-hint=wayland "$@" ``` And made this file executable. After this it is starting as native wayland app and fonts/UI is crystal clear :) Maybe this would help someone... This is how it looks before/after: @IsmaelMartinez please close if you like - this is only for helping other with similar problem or to reference...

issues.title: [Bug]: Logout every day
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

Logout every day

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.1.0

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.
2.
3.
...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.
2.
3.
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

### Additional context

# Daily Login Required - Electron/Chromium Cache Overflow Issue

## 🐛 Bug Report

### **Problem Description**
Teams for Linux requires daily re-authentication while the web version of Teams works persistently without login issues. This appears to be caused by Electron/Chromium cache overflow and token corruption.

### **Steps to Reproduce**
1. Install teams-for-linux (deb version 2.1.0)
2. Login to Microsoft Teams
3. Use the application normally for 1-2 days
4. Application automatically logs out and requires re-authentication
5. Pattern repeats daily/every few days

### **Expected Behavior**
- Teams for Linux should maintain authentication like the web version
- Users should not need to re-authenticate daily
- Tokens should persist properly between sessions

### **Actual Behavior**
- Application forces logout approximately every 24 hours
- User must re-enter credentials daily
- Web version (teams.microsoft.com) works without these issues

### **System Information**
- **OS:** Linux 6.11.0-29-generic
- **Distribution:** Ubuntu/Debian-based
- **Teams for Linux Version:** 2.1.0
- **Installation Method:** deb package
- **Architecture:** amd64

### **Technical Analysis**

#### **Root Cause Investigation**
Through detailed analysis, we discovered:

1. **Cache Size Growth:**
   ```bash
   $ du -sh ~/.config/teams-for-linux/
   622M    ~/.config/teams-for-linux/
   ```

2. **Electron/Chromium Architecture:**
   ```
   teams-for-linux → Electron → Chromium → OAuth tokens
                                    ↓
                            Cache overflow (620MB)
                                    ↓
                             Token corruption
                                    ↓
                           Forced logout
   ```

3. **Process Analysis:**
   ```bash
   $ ps aux | grep teams-for-linux
   /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux
   /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=zygote
   /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=utility
   ```

#### **Problematic Files/Directories**
- `~/.config/teams-for-linux/Partitions/teams-4-linux/` (620MB)
- `DIPS-wal` - OAuth token database
- `SharedStorage-wal` - Shared storage
- `Cookies-journal` - Cookie journal
- Multiple cache directories

#### **Token Lifecycle Issues**
```
1. Login → OAuth token received
2. Store → Chromium saves to local DB
3. Refresh → Auto-refresh attempts
4. Corrupt → Cache overflow causes corruption
5. Logout → Forced re-authentication
```

### **Comparison with Web Version**
The web version doesn't have this issue because:
- **Isolated sessions** per tab
- **Browser-managed token handling**
- **Automatic cache cleanup**
- **No long-term data accumulation**

### **Workaround Solutions**

#### **1. Cache Cleanup Script**
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Clear Electron/Chromium cache
pkill -f "teams-for-linux"
rm -rf ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Cache/*
rm -rf ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Partitions/teams-4-linux/Cache/*
rm -rf ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Partitions/teams-4-linux/IndexedDB/*
rm -rf ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Partitions/teams-4-linux/WebStorage/*
# Remove problematic token files
rm -f ~/.config/teams-for-linux/DIPS-wal
rm -f ~/.config/teams-for-linux/SharedStorage-wal
rm -f ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Cookies-journal
```

#### **2. Size Monitoring**
```bash
# Check cache size
du -sh ~/.config/teams-for-linux/
# Problems typically start when size > 500MB
```

### **Potential Long-term Solutions**

#### **1. Automatic Cache Management**
- Implement automatic cache cleanup when size exceeds threshold
- Add configuration option for cache size limit
- Regular cleanup of temporary files

#### **2. Token Storage Optimization**
- Separate token storage from general cache
- Implement better token refresh logic
- Add token corruption detection

#### **3. Chromium Flags Optimization**
Consider adding flags like:
```bash
--disable-background-timer-throttling
--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows
--disable-renderer-backgrounding
```

#### **4. User Configuration Options**
- Add cache management settings to UI
- Allow users to configure cache cleanup frequency
- Provide cache size monitoring

### **Logs and Diagnostics**

#### **Cache Statistics**
```
Total config size: 622MB
Main cache: ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Cache/
Partitions cache: ~/.config/teams-for-linux/Partitions/ (620MB)
```

#### **Problematic File Growth**
```
DIPS-wal: 189KB (should be small)
SharedStorage-wal: exists (should be empty)
Multiple .org.chromium.Chromium.* temp files
```

### **Testing Environment**
- Fresh installation cache: 10-50MB
- After 1 month usage: 200-400MB
- Problems start: 500MB+
- Critical state: 1GB+

### **Frequency Pattern**
- Daily logout: cache > 800MB
- Weekly logout: cache 400-600MB
- Stable operation: cache < 200MB

### **Related Issues**
This issue might be related to:
- Electron cache management
- Chromium token storage
- OAuth token refresh mechanisms
- Long-running application state

### **Additional Context**
- Issue doesn't occur in web version (teams.microsoft.com)
- Affects users who keep application running for extended periods
- Workaround (cache cleanup) is effective but temporary
- Problem persists across different Linux distributions

### **Proposed Feature Request**
1. **Built-in cache management** - Automatic cleanup when cache exceeds size limit
2. **Token persistence improvement** - Better handling of OAuth tokens
3. **User-configurable cache settings** - Allow users to control cache behavior
4. **Diagnostic tools** - Built-in cache size monitoring and cleanup options

### **Impact**
- **User Experience:** Poor - requires daily re-authentication
- **Productivity:** Reduced - interrupts workflow
- **Adoption:** May drive users to web version instead

### **Severity**
**High** - Affects core functionality (authentication persistence)

---

**Thank you for maintaining this project!** This issue affects many users who prefer the desktop application over the web version. The Electron/Chromium cache overflow appears to be the root cause, and implementing automatic cache management would significantly improve user experience./

issues.title: [Feat]: Replace the opensuse info in the wiki
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

The info in the wiki for the opensuse installation is pretty unusual and should be replaced

### Motivation

The way to install the rpm package on opensuse is more like so:

---

## Installation

### Step 1: Import GPG Key

```bash
curl -1sLf -o /tmp/teams-for-linux.asc https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/teams-for-linux.asc && sudo rpm --import /tmp/teams-for-linux.asc && rm -f /tmp/teams-for-linux.asc
```

### Step 2: Add Repository

```bash
sudo curl -1sLf -o /etc/zypp/repos.d/teams-for-linux.repo https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/rpm/teams-for-linux.repo
```

### Step 3: Refresh and Install

```bash
sudo zypper refresh && sudo zypper install teams-for-linux
```

When prompted about unsigned repository metadata, choose:
- `yes` to accept once
- `a` to accept always (recommended to avoid future prompts)

**Alternative:** Skip metadata GPG checks by modifying the repo file:
```bash
sudo sed -i 's/gpgcheck=1/gpgcheck=1\nrepo_gpgcheck=0/' /etc/zypp/repos.d/teams-for-linux.repo
```

---

I have it installed that way. I am not sure if one should recommend the removal of the check, but just in case I added it. I don't know what the heck the wiki entry is on about, but that's not how you do it and the described way lets you update through zypper without hacks.


### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Feat]: Option to use Electron native spellchecker
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Option to use Electron native spellchecker

### Motivation

Hi, I understand that a lot of Teams features are Microsoft things that can't be controlled by teams-for-linux. But in case there's any way to manipulate or override the functionality via some kind of hidden setting I'm creating this feature request. Basically, Teams has recently implemented its own spellchecker overlay with a right-click HTML/Javascript menu for spelling suggestions. Frankly, it's awful compared to the one that is integrated into the Chromium platform for all Electron apps, as the Teams implementation only allows for selecting up to 3 languages and it gets confused with mixed-language messages. I tried deleting all of the selected languages from the Teams spellchecker settings, and it allows them to all be deleted, but after returning later to that settings panel the U.S. English language re-appears. When completely disabling the spellchecker option there it does disable it, and in the message composer field the right-click Electron menu for "Writing Languages" does allow selecting languages, but there is no actual underlining wrong words or correction suggestions. This used to work as expected in teams-for-linux before Teams implemented their own spellchecker, so I wonder if there is a way to force the Electron platform spellchecker to work. This probably isn't at all relevant, but this situation reminds me of the TinyMCE HTML composer that many blogs and forum platforms use, which also implemented its own overlay HTML/Javascript spellchecker that can be disabled so that it falls back to the regular browser's native spellchecker:
https://www.tiny.cloud/docs/tinymce/latest/spell-checking

Thanks as always for maintaining this essential lifeline for Linux users that need to use Teams!

### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

tfl 2.0.17 does not start (crashes) on Rocky Linux 9 (MATE desktop) and yells about a GTK 2-3/4 symbols mix in output.

### What package are you using?

rpm

### Version

2.0.17

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
Start tfl 2.0.17 in Rocky Linux 9, within a MATE desktop:

$ cat /etc/system-release
Rocky Linux release 9.6 (Blue Onyx)

$ echo $DESKTOP_SESSION
mate

$ teams-for-linux
Initialising logger with config: {"transports":{"console":{"level":"info"},"file":{"level":false}}}
13:28:00.697 âº configPath: $HOME/.config/teams-for-linux
13:28:00.803 âº (node:3591249) [DEP0180] DeprecationWarning: fs.Stats constructor is deprecated.
(Use `teams-for-linux --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created)

(process:3591249): Gtk-ERROR **: 13:28:00.809: GTK 2/3 symbols detected. Using GTK 2/3 and GTK 4 in the same process is not supported
teams-for-linux: line 9: 3591249 Trace/breakpoint trap   (core dumped) /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux
```

### Expected Behavior

tfl should start and work like 2.0.16 does.
When I saw the mention "GNOME Users: Electron 36+ defaults to GTK 4 on GNOME. If you experience theming issues, you can use GTK 3 mode." in the 2.0.17 release notes, I was pretty sure something could go wrong with 2.0.17, tried it and voila. Not sure this is a conflict with MATE (which is GTK3), I could not test yet in a GNOME desktop on RL9 yet.

issues.title: initial implemenation of the creation of a release-info.json file with the relevant release information
issues.body:
## What's New

- Update electron to 36.4.0 and @homebridge/dbus-native to 0.7.1 for improved ARM compatibility and security
- Adding release info generation from com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux.appdata.xml file
- Validate the release version is ready on the build.yml github action
- Updating github actions to use node 22

## Notes

### ARM Compatibility Improvements

The @homebridge/dbus-native update fixes crashes on ARMv6 platforms and improves overall ARM compatibility for Linux users.

### Electron 36.4.0 Update

This Electron update includes security improvements and modern platform support.

**GNOME Users**: Electron 36+ defaults to GTK 4 on GNOME. If you experience theming issues, you can use GTK 3 mode.

More details: [Electron GTK 4 Changes](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/breaking-changes#changed-gtk-4-is-default-when-running-gnome)
---
*For a detailed comparison of changes, see the [Electron v35.4.0 to v36.4.0 Changes](https://github.com/electron/electron/compare/v35.4.0...v36.4.0).*

Closes #1691

issues.title: [Bug]: Clearing Storage got rid of my monthly view in Calendar?
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

Clear Storage may have disabled or opted out of certain experimental or beta testing features that were not expected to be changed.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.14

### Reproduction steps

```markdown

```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown

```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

This is more of a question first but maybe could be a bug.

In the Calendar app, I no longer have a monthly view, there is just: weekly views, agenda, and day.  I've had a monthly view as long as I can remember.  Copilot says this may have been a temp feature that MS is rolling out as part of their new calendar, and my account was selected for beta testing (something similar to Firefox Experiments maybe?), because apparently by default there is no monthly view.  Its disappearance seems to have coincided when I used "Quit (Clear storage)" earlier, and Copilot agrees I could have cleared some cookie that had me part of some beta test....  Does any of this make sense to you or sound possible?  It recommended I restore a backup of `~/.config/teams-for-linux` and even wrote a script to do it for me!

I tried restoring a backup of `~/.config/teams-for-linux` from several days ago, but the monthly view is not back.  Is it possibly too late, and my account has been marked on MS's end as not beta testing anymore or something, and not able to have this feature?  or maybe I need to go further back in my backups?

If this a likely scenario then it could be considered a bug with the "Clear Storage" option?  maybe we should be looking harder for certain cached data that has enabled certain experiments or beta testing?  I was definitely used to that monthly calendar view and now I have to load up bloated Outlook on the Web to get a monthly view of my calendar.

issues.title: [Feat]: Disable pressing "Enter" to send message
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

For YEARS Teams has made the ENTER key the default "Send Message" action.  This leads to MANY inadvertant sends when the user really just wants a new line.

### Motivation

Some many times I just want a new line and get a send message.  Forget to just get a new line is SHIFT-ENTER.  Makes me feel SO STUPID to send partial messages.  Seems like in fine MS fashion - this issue has been discussed for YEARS without MS providing some parameter to all the user to determine what that user would like to have the "Enter" button do.

### Alternatives

Once in a message if you type CTRL-SHIFT-X puts you into "edit mode" and enter works as enter without send.   This works fine - and actually gives the user more formatting options.  But the user must remember to press CTRL-SHIFT-X EVERY time they enter a new message OR the result is pressing Enter sends message.

### Additional context

Could you make it that once the user positions their cursor into the text area for typing a message - your code behind the scenes detects the position of the users cursor - checks some user setting that you have provided - AND if the setting is "SendMessageOnEnter=N" - your code sends the CTRL-SHIFT-X key sequence to automatically put the message box in edit mode.  If the user setting is "SendMessageOnEnter=Y" - then the default "Enter-Send" would apply.
Would this be possible ???
BTW - I just hit ENTER a ton of times - AND - github does it right - my new feature request did not just post half way through my dissertation.  I actually have to click "Create" to post - What a concept MS !!! :-)

issues.title: [Feat]: Improve Copy&Paste from Chats to Text Windows / Toggle compose.disableTimestampOnCopy (Copy with Names + Timestamps)
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Include Names and Time Stamps in Copy&Paste to Text

### Motivation

We often copy parts of chats to our issue tracker for documentation purpose.

* Ctrl+C in the Chat Window
* Ctrl+Shift+V in the issue tracker (to exclude formatting)

This worked great until half year ago or so and produced text output like this:

[12:10] Me: Which color should be the button?
[12:14] John Doe: Blue please

But nowadays it produces only


Which color should be the button?
Blue please

So it's heard to see who said what.

I would be nice to include this user info and timestamp.

### Alternatives

Adding those infos tediously manually.

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: Reimplementation of call events + new incoming call toast
issues.body:
Added a reimplementation of the call events to revive the incoming call scripts.
Also added an incoming call toast just like the one from the discontinued Linux Teams App from Microsoft.

To use the incoming call script feature there are two new config properties introduced **incomingCallCommand** and **incomingCallCommandArgs**.

incomingCallCommand:
This property contains the command or executable to run on an incoming call.
Caution: ~ in the path is currently not supported.

Example:
`{
    "incomingCallCommand": "/home/user/ring.sh"
}`

At default the following arguments get passed to the command/executable:
1.  The name of the caller
2.  The subtext of the original toast from the Teams web app
3.  The image of the caller as base64 data image string

incomingCallCommandArgs:
This property can contain an array of arguments that get prepend to the default arguments.

Example:
`{
    "incomingCallCommandArgs": ["-n", "example"]
}`


To use the new incoming call toast the new config property **enableIncomingCallToast** needs to be set to **true**.

Example:
`{
    "enableIncomingCallToast": true
}`

Attention: The incoming call toast should open on the screen where your cursor is, unfortunately there is currently a bug in electron on Linux that prevents that feature -> https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/42519

issues.title: Add video menu entry that allows control of video elements
issues.body:
This PR adds a command line switch `--videoMenu`.

When set to true, a new menu entry `Video` is added to the menu.
This menu entry contains two sub entries to manipulate video elements on the current page:

- `Force enable PiP mode for shared screen`
- `Force toggle controls for all video elements`

issues.title: [Bug]: Version 2 fails to start login with Godaddy accounts,  v1 worked.
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

Teams is sourced via Godaddy,  with v1 everything worked.  with v2 we get an error saying we have a "strange browser".

### What package are you using?

rpm

### Version

2.0.12-1

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.install client (teams-for-linux-2.0.12-1.x86_64)
2.launch it.   Login window pops up with error message.
3.

This is if a user never had teams-for-linux installed.   I had 1.12.7 installed and using perviously,  when I upgraded to 2.x it worked as expected.

Any new users who did not have 1.x installed previously (logged in with it) gets the error (in the image below).

can' provide more debugging output later (on the road atm).


...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.allow user to log in.
2.
3.
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

### Additional context

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0348954c-9e14-454f-8f0e-a83d2ee86818)

issues.title: [Bug]: Windows build not working - teams-for-linux.exe is not valid Win32 application
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

Windows build is not able to start and work under Windows 7. After starting of application, error "teams-for-linux.exe is not valid Win32 application" is reported and application closed.

### What package are you using?

Windows

### Version

2.0.12

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Use Windows 7 Professional 64bit (Slovak language pack)
2. Download and install teams-for-linux exe
3. At the end of installation, error window appears with text "C:\Users\username\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\teams-for-linux.lnk  |  Unknown error"
4. After starting application, error window appears with text "C:\Users\username\AppData\Local\Programs\teams-for-linux\teams-for-linux.exe is not valid Win32 application." and application closes.

(please note - error messages are displayed in Slovak language, above is English translation of error messages).
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
It would be great if app could be used under Windows 7.
```

### Debug

```markdown
Starting team-for-linux.exe with --webDebug produces same error "teams-for-linux.exe is not valid Win32 application".
```

### Additional context

I have tried previous version 2.0.11 with the same result.

issues.title: [Bug]: Opening up links don't use the default browser again
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

Links open up in chrome, not in default browser

### What package are you using?

snap

### Version

2.0.9

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Open up teams
2. open up link sent from another user
3. Link opens in chrome.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Open up teams
2. open up link sent from another user
3. Link opens in default browser

issues.title: fixing 1681 by only applying those changes to macOS
issues.body: Fixes #1681

issues.title: [Bug]: Teams for Linux 2.0.11 does not support hardware security keys
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

When using a hardware security key, in a normal browser the key will prompt for e.g. a PIN to unlock it so it can be used. That pop-up for key entry never shows up in Teams for Linux.

### What package are you using?

tar.gz

### Version

2.0.11

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
You get presented with:

> Face, fingerprint, PIN or security key
> Your device will open a security window. Follow the instructions there to sign in.

during logon, but no window ever shows up.
```

issues.title: Update node version in CONTRIBUTING.md
issues.body: Update build instructions to use node:20 for containerised builds. Fixes #1687.

```

### Additional context

Tray Icon: 

![Icon](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b158246c-ddad-43f7-8375-54458831d300)

I have personally noticed this erratic operation when upgrading from 2.0.5 to 2.0.11 (I skipped a few in between). If I had to bet this bug seems to be caused by the changes introduced in 2.0.10 and I imagine all Arch Linux users will be more or less the same.

UPDATE: I have discovered a workaround to get the icon working normally again. You have to either download the [GNOME trayicons extension](https://github.com/ubuntu/gnome-shell-extension-appindicator) and reload it or lock the session and open it again (which also reloads the extension).

labels.name: known issue/workaround
labels.description: Workaround available in the know_issues.md file

issues.title: [Bug]: Teams takes a long time to load
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

When I start Teams, it takes almost a minute to load. Before, it used to start right away. I tried reinstalling, but it didn’t help.

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

2.0.11

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Start portal for teams.
2.Fans are spinning.
3.Nothing happens.
4.After a minutes or so the program starts.
...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.Start portal for teams.
2.Window comes up immediately.
3.Login.
4.Use the program.
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Feat]: Option to start minimized to tray.
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

When starting up Teams for Linux, have a setting that makes it only start minimized to tray.

### Motivation

Teams for Linux is one of the windows that I have set on my work profile to auto-open. To reduce clutter and maintain a cleaner working environment, I like to start it minimized using the --minimized flag on the startup command. However, this was not trivial to find as it is not exposed anywhere in the UI. 

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Teams app notifications are not showing up in system notification center
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

The issue is that when I get a notification on the app, that is not reflected in the system notification center. For other apps, when they receive a notification, it is shown in the system notification center. Everytime I need to check the teams notifications, I have to open the app. PS: The popup appears but just that it is not collected in the system notification center.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.11

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Notification comes in the app
2. Pop up is displayed
3. But in the system notification center, the notification is not present.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown

```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Teams window sharing uses very low resolution
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

Sharing a screen uses 192X108 resolution

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.9

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Log in
2.Join a meeting
3.share a screen
   -> shared screen is at a VERY low resolution: 192X108 (This looks like 10% of 1920X1080)  The lowered resolution makes it difficult to present and share desktop.
...
This seems intermittent or timing related. It happens 90% of the time when I attempt it. It does not seem related to internet speed or processing power. Neither does it seem to be respecting the resolution that I specifically select when sharing a specific screen.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.Log in
2.Join meeting
3.Share screen, but screen is at appropriate resolution
...
```

issues.title: Fix: removing apt update warning "doesn't support architecture 'i386'"
issues.body:
This PR fixes the warning during apt update && upgrade 
> "N: Skipping acquire of configured file 'main/binary-i386/Packages' as repository 'https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian stable InRelease' doesn't support architecture 'i386'"

by appending the "Architectures: amd64" into `/etc/apt/sources.list.d/teams-for-linux-packages.sources`.

Small change and Thanks for reviewing!

issues.title: [Bug]: outdated image in build instructions for docker / podman
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

The build instructions in CONTRIBUTING.md reference docker image node:14 which no longer works.

### What package are you using?

Built from source

### Version

2.0.11

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Clone the git repo 
2. Excecute per CONTRIBUTING.md: docker run -it --rm --volume .:/var/mnt:z -w /var/mnt/ node:14 /bin/bash -c "apt update && apt install -y rpm && npm ci && npm run dist:linux"
3. npm ci fails with "error Cannot read property '@homebridge/dbus-native' of undefined"
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. the app is being built correctly.
```

### Solution
I found that the referenced image "node:14" seems outdated. Replacing it with "node:20" leads to a successful build.

issues.title: [Bug]: No tray icon since 2.0.9 (Ubuntu 22.04 with Mate desktop 1.26.0)
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

No tray icon since 2.0.9

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.10, 2.0.11

### Reproduction steps

Run `teams-for-linux` and observe no tray icon in `notification-area-applet`.
Ubuntu 22.04 with Mate desktop 1.26.0.

```
ii  mate-desktop   1.26.0-1     amd64        Library with common API for various MATE modules
ii  mate-applets   1.26.0-1     amd64        Various applets for the MATE panel
ii  mate-panel     1.26.2-1     amd64        launcher and docking facility for MATE
```

I have two `teams-for-linux` apps and no tray icon for both since 2.0.9:
* One is the default one: `Exec=/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux %U` + `Icon=teams-for-linux`
* The other one is "custom": `Exec=/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --appIcon=/home/georgii/pics/icons/teams-for-linux-skype.png --class=teams-for-linux-skype --user-data-dir=/home/georgii/.config/teams-for-linux-skype %U` + `Icon=teams-for-linux-skype` (the icon exists and worked w/o issues in 2.0.8)

### Expected Behavior

The app icon appears in tray (notification area) in Mate panel.

### Debug

```shell
> teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
Initialising logger with config: "{}"
14:55:41.261 › File logging at /home/georgii/.config/teams-for-linux/logs/main.log with level silly
14:55:41.262 › Console logging enabled with level silly
14:55:41.262 › Logger initialised
14:55:41.262 › configPath: /home/georgii/.config/teams-for-linux
14:55:41.262 › configFile: {
  '//README': 'https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/tree/main/app/config/README.md',
  closeAppOnCross: true,
  defaultURLHandler: '/home/giermulnik/bin/xdg-open.yz',
  followSystemTheme: true,
  notificationMethod: 'electron',
  optInTeamsV2: true,
  spellCheckerLanguages: [ 'en-GB', 'uk', 'ru' ]
}
14:55:41.324 › App already running
14:55:41.327 › (node:519056) [DEP0180] DeprecationWarning: fs.Stats constructor is deprecated.
(Use `teams-for-linux --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created)
[519056:0426/145541.329680:ERROR:ozone_platform_x11.cc(246)] Missing X server or $DISPLAY
[519056:0426/145541.329732:ERROR:env.cc(257)] The platform failed to initialize.  Exiting.
```

Not at that laptop at the moment hence can only run via SSH. If this is not sufficient, please let me know, and I'll update by running the same at that laptop's GUI session on Monday.

### Additional context

The issue isn't in place on older installation of Mate on Linux Mint 19.3 (Tricia):
```
ii  mate-desktop        1.22.2-1+trici amd64          Library with common API for various MATE modules
ii  mate-applets        1.22.2-1+trici amd64          Various applets for the MATE panel
ii  mate-panel          1.22.2-1mint1+ amd64          launcher and docking facility for MATE
```

issues.title: [Bug]: electronCLIFlags are not picked up from config.json
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

electronCLIFlags statements in config.json don't seem to apply

### What package are you using?

rpm

### Version

2.0.10

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
If I run teams-for-linux with these command-line arguments:

$ teams-for-linux --enable-features=UseOzonePlatform,WaylandWindowDecorations --ozone-platform-hint=auto

I get Electron to use Wayland protocol (the flags are based on https://dev.to/archerallstars/how-to-run-electron-apps-under-linuxs-wayland-session-like-a-pro-2g25).

Based on app/config/README.md, I've edited `.config/teams-for-linux/config.json` and added a top-level property:


    "electronCLIFlags": [
      [ "enable-features", "UseOzonePlatform,WaylandWindowDecorations"],
      ["ozone-platform-hint", "auto" ]
    ]


I tried adding leading `--`, doesn't make a difference.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
I expect that the same flags, as specified in the command line, would apply if listed in `/electronCLIFlags` property.
```

### Debug

```markdown
[vps@druid]~/ws/teams-for-linux/app/config$ teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
Initialising logger with config: "{}"
18:59:43.981 › File logging at /home/vps/.config/teams-for-linux/logs/main.log with level silly
18:59:43.982 › Console logging enabled with level silly
18:59:43.982 › Logger initialised
18:59:43.982 › configPath: /home/vps/.config/teams-for-linux
18:59:43.982 › configFile: {
  electronCLIFlags: [
    [ 'enable-features', 'UseOzonePlatform,WaylandWindowDecorations' ],
    [ 'ozone-platform-hint', 'auto' ]
  ]
}
18:59:43.984 › Running under Wayland, switching to PipeWire...

issues.title: [Feat]: Ability to join/transfer an ongoing call from another device
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Allow joining or transferring an active Teams call from another device to the teams-for-linux client.

### Motivation

Hi Ismael (and contributors),
First of all, thank you for your great work on teams-for-linux. It's an invaluable tool for Linux users!
I would like to request a new feature that mirrors functionality available in the official Microsoft Teams clients (Windows, macOS, mobile).

Currently, if a user is already in a Teams call or meeting on one device (e.g., a Windows PC or a smartphone), the official Teams client on another device, upon launch, detects the ongoing call and offers options like:
"Add this device": Join the ongoing call with the second device, adding it to the session (often muting the mic/speaker initially).
"Transfer to this device": Move the entire call session from the first device to the second one, ending the call on the original device.

Current Behaviour in teams-for-linux:
As far as I can tell, teams-for-linux does not currently detect if the user is already in a call on another device signed into the same account. Therefore, there is no option to join or transfer an existing active call to the Linux client.

Desired Behaviour:
It would be extremely helpful if teams-for-linux could detect an ongoing call associated with the user's account on other devices and provide similar options ("Join" / "Transfer") to seamlessly bring the Linux client into the active session.

Use Case / Motivation:
This feature significantly enhances flexibility. Users often need to switch devices during a call (e.g., move from a mobile device to a desktop for better screen sharing or a more stable connection) or might want to use their Linux machine alongside another device (e.g., manage video/screenshare on Linux while handling audio on a phone).

Thank you for considering this feature request!

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/56d4738f-6d24-475a-bb63-e04037df2035)


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/417d860c-f4ee-4bce-90b5-426c2958922f)

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Online meetings links replace the current instance
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

When opening an online meeting link, it replaces the Teams view with the meeting, making the rest inaccessible. On leaving the meeting, a message indicating to close the window remains, with no way of resuming the old view: one needs then to restart `teams-for-linux`

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.9

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Open a `https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_<***>` link with `teams-for-linux` (with a default MIME association et, merely clicking on it)
2. Notice the current view/window being replaced by the meeting one
3. Click on `Leave` and notice there is no way of resuming the old view
4. Closing the window (cross) and reopening the Teams with clicking on the taskbar icon keeps reopening the meetup view: no way to access the normal Teams view.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Open a `https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_<***>` link with `teams-for-linux` (with a default MIME association et, merely clicking on it)
2. The meeting opens in a new windows
3. Leaving the meeting and/or closing the windows does not impact the "main" window
```

[Feat]: Automatically mute/unmute in meeting when Teams detects muted/unmuted mic on system side
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

I use system mic mute and really need to update my mute/unmute status in meetings

### Motivation

It would be nice to have conditional feature to be automatically muted/unmuted in meeting when Teams for Linux detects that Teams detected muted/unmuted mic on system side. 
Teams already shows buble about muted mic on system side during meeting, but don't switch mute/unmute status on meeting.

Is possible to detect this event in Teams for Linux and switch this mute/unmute status on meeting?

### Alternatives

Or is alternatively possible create mute/unmute DBUS event listener in Teams for Linux so I mute/unmute from external script?

issues.title: dynamically set Dock icon for macOS
issues.body:
**Issue to resolve**
on macOS, the Dock icon is controlled by the app bundle, not the BrowserWindow icon.  therefore on macOS the icon display on the Dock is always the icon comes from the app bundle.

**Change**
This change allows the app to display the specified icon on Dock based on the icon configuration

**Key Consideration for the change**

1. the app-icon config provides customisation icon settings for **tray icons**, this is cool for windows but not Mac because it does not apply to the dock icon
1. the app-icon is mis-leading, in my opinion it needs to be named **tray-icon** but I think we do not want to rename it to avoid unnecessary changes
1. I also do not want to introduce a new "dock-icon" configuration either because I think it is unnecessary so I decided to reuse the app-icon configuration and added the auto icon resize on tray.js to accommodate  it 

example:

```
# run from app source

electron ./app --trace-warnings --app-title='New Custom Title' --app-icon=`pwd`/app/assets/icons/icon-custom-128x128.png
```

issues.title: removing unused and deprecated config options
issues.body:
- Remove disableMeetingNotifications as it is not possible anymore - found from #1659
- Removing the incomingCallCommand and incomingCallCommandArgs as they are not working anymore 
- Removing ntlmV2enabled as it is not documented anymore in Chromium and it should be the default value
- Removing deprecated `clearStorage` in favor of `clearStorageData`
- Removing deprecated `customUserDir` in favor of `--user-data-dir` 
- Changing the default value of `onNewWindowOpenMeetupJoinUrlInApp` to `true` - #1379

issues.body:
### Describe the feature

A Teams-Call should open in a new window

### Motivation

(i'm sorry, my english is not the best)
I would like to see cameras and shared screens in the call and at the same time to be able to see other chats in Teams or browse through Sharepoint-Files.
In Teams, i often have to search for a specific file or a link from another chat for the participants in the current call. During searching, i miss everything that is currently being shared by another call participant.
This request corresponds to the behavior of the Windows app.

### Alternatives

I know that this feature is not offered in the browser version of Teams either, which certainly makes it difficult to implement. Perhaps there would be a way to display the current window as a split screen instead of opening a new window?
(i'm not a programmer, but if i can help with translations in German or something like this, it would be a pleasure to help)

issues.title: dynamically set Dock icon for macOS
issues.body:
**Issue to resolve**
on macOS, the Dock icon is controlled by the app bundle, not the BrowserWindow icon.  therefore on macOS the icon display on the Dock is always the icon comes from the app bundle.

**Change**
This change allows the app to display the specified icon on Dock from on the configuration

**Key Consideration for the change**

1. the app-icon config provides customisation icon settings for **tray icons**, this is cool for windows but not Mac because it does not apply the dock icon
1. the app-icon is mis-leading, in my opinion I believe it should be named **tray-icon** but I think we do not want to rename it
1. I also do not want to introduce a new "dock-icon" configuration either because I think it is unnecessary so I decided to reuse the app-icon configuration and added the auto icon resize on tray.js to accommodate  it

```
# run from app source

electron ./app --trace-warnings --app-title='New Custom Title' --app-icon=`pwd`/app/assets/icons/icon-custom-128x128.png

issues.title: [Feat]: Better options for focusing
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Ability to hide message notifications, unread message count

### Motivation

There are already some very nice options for disabling notifications:

- Disable all notifications
- Disable meeting notification
- Disable notifications sound
- Disable sound when not available (e.g. busy, in a call)
- Disable window flash on new notifications

I would like two additional options:

- Hide unread message count: Sometimes I need to really focus, and the message notifications can be really distracting. Even the unread message count can be difficult to ignore when I need to focus on something difficult but my brain wants an excuse to do something else :)
- Disable message notifications: Similar to disable meeting notifications, however I typically want the opposite; I don't want to hide meeting notifications because I don't want to miss a meeting, but message notifications can be frequent and disatracting

Beyond this, I think it's great that that there's an option to _Disable sound when not available_. However, why is this the only option that applies when not available? I would like this ability for all of the options. However, with the current UX this would result in duplicating all of the options, e.g.

- Disable meeting notifications
- Disable meeting notifications when not available
- Disable notifications sound
- Disable notifications sound when not available
- Disable window flash on new notifications
- Disable window flash on new notifications when not available

Rather than duplicating all of these options, what if this was made a separate option, such as:

- Only disable notifications when not available

Then it could be applied to all.

So my final proposal would be something like:

- Disable all notifications
- Disable meeting notifications
- Disable message notifications
- Disable notification sound
- Disable window flash on new notifications
- Hide unread message count
- Only apply these settings when not available (e.g. busy, in a call)

What do you think?

I would be happy to work on a PR for any or all of this functionality.

Thanks!

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: lost ability to have multiple instances running with --customUserDir option
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

I have been running with a couple of instances of t4l and gave it two separate userdirs per the --customUserDir=/path/to/customdir but now it's not working. It won't allow two concurrent t4l to run. It just is ignores

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.5 

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Launch first instance via launcher /usr/bin/teams-for-linux --customUserDir=/home/me/t4luserdir1 --appTitle=Instance1
2. Application starts, all good. 
3. Launch a second instance as I have for many months now via launcher /usr/bin/teams-for-linux --customUserDir=/home/me/t4luserdir2 --appTitle=Instance2
4. Nothing happens. Instance1 is fine. 
5. Running for direct command line as non-root and no errors there. 




...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Launch first instance via launcher /usr/bin/teams-for-linux --customUserDir=/home/me/t4luserdir1 --appTitle=Instance1
2.Application starts - all good and accessing Teams on that specific login
3. Launch a second instance as I have for many months now via launcher /usr/bin/teams-for-linux --customUserDir=/home/me/t4luserdir2 --appTitle=Instance2
4. Additional instance of team-for-linux starts and uses the different userdir (with a different login)
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
This is when trying to start the second instance with the second userdir.

$ /usr/bin/teams-for-linux --customUserDir=/home/me/t4l/<dir2> --appTitle=Name2 --webDebug~
No config file found, using default values

and it does nothing.
```

### Additional context

First one, no matter which one I try will start if there is not an instance running, but a second instance now seems impossible to do all of a sudden...

issues.title: [Bug]: Error when quitting with "Quit (Clear Storage)"
issues.body:
### Describe the bug

Error when quitting with "Quit (Clear Storage)"

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.0.3

### Reproduction steps
- Start the app
- Click on "Quit (Clear Storage)" and the Yes
- App doesn't quit and in terminal I get:
```markdown
21:14:20.326 › (node:16783) UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'clearStorageData')
    at Menus.quit (/opt/teams-for-linux/resources/app.asar/app/menus/index.js:64:63)
    at click (/opt/teams-for-linux/resources/app.asar/app/menus/appMenu.js:45:26)
    at MenuItem.click (node:electron/js2c/browser_init:2:35295)

This error originated either by throwing inside of an async function without a catch block, or by rejecting a promise which was not handled with .catch(). [Bug]: cannot join teams meeting
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

I have meetings in the calendar, with teams links and code/password. However, for some meetings the "Join" button is not shown. When opening the calendar item and clicking the link nothing happens (firefox opens, but it does not forward to teams app - this might be an issue with firefox settings, but i dont know what to do), also there is nowhere an option to "join meeting with code". So sadly this teams port is useless for me as approx 25% of the meetings cannot be joined. I have not found out what the differences are in the invite (i use thunderbird).

```

issues.title: [Bug]: Sign in with HW key does not work
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

I belong to two organizations. When signing in to the guest org I want to use a HW key connected to my computer. This works well in the PWA app but fails in the Teams4Linux client. The reason probably is that the PIN entry for the HW key is not shown hence the key cannot be used.

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

1.13.2

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. (Sign out from team)
2. Initiate a sign in
3. Pick the account (in my case there is only 1 account to use, HW key login enabled for that)
4. If prompts for password choose "Other ways to sign in"
5. Choose "Face, fingerprint, PIN or security key"

At this point a message is shown:

Face, fingerprint, PIN or security key
Your device will open a security window. Follow the instructions there to sign in.


However the security window with PIN entry is not shown. <<< This is the problem
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. (Sign out from team)
2. Initiate a sign in
3. Pick the account (in my case there is only 1 account to use, HW key login enabled for that)
4. If prompts for password choose "Other ways to sign in"
5. Choose "Face, fingerprint, PIN or security key"
6. PIN entry window pops up to "unlock" the HW key
7. Touch HW key to send login code
8. login success
```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

[teams.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19561988/teams.log)

issues.title: [Feat]: Add documentation about how to run multiple instances of the app
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add documentatoin about how to run multiple instances of the app.

### Motivation

Every now an again, someone will ask about been able to run the app in multiple instances. 

It is possible to archive this using some config arguments and environment variables, whoever, the how to do it, is not documented other than in comments across multiple issues.

This ticket is to find a way to document this.

### Alternatives

Keep it as it is. 

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: fix readme config file table
Feature 1584 remove teams v1 support
issues.body:
- Remove deprecated config enableBackgroundCallsAuthentication that was never announced but people didn't complain about
- optInTeamsV2 and customBGServiceIgnoreMSDefaults config options are no longer available as they where teams-v1 specific
- Cleaning up teams-v1 logic in the app
- Set default url to be https://teams.microsoft.com/v2 instead of https://teams.microsoft.com
- Remove activityHub eventHandlers for the teams-v1 events

issues.title: [Bug]: Auto-away is not working
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

Whenever I go away from my computer, I will keep being shown as 'online' (green icon). My status only changes when I am in a meeting/call (red) or have an entry in the calendar that says 'working elsewhere' (icons turn dark green / dark red). I need to explicitly set my status to 'away' or 'offline' for people to see I am not at my computer.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

1.13.1 (I think, can't find it in the UI!)

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Start Teams
2. Go away for a while
3. Check status from another account
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Status should at some point change to 'away' and preferably) mention how long I've been away
```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Teams doesn't start / refuses to open window of running instance after update
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

After updating teams4linux, the application doesn't start when clicking the icon. If it was still running, the application window won't open either.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

So far: any!

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Close Teams
2. Update (maybe also happens on a fresh install -- not sure & not tested)
3. Try to open Teams

If I try to run Teams from bash, the reason for it not starting is shown:

[48366:0328/145817.029483:FATAL:setuid_sandbox_host.cc(163)] The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755.

When changing the permissions everything works again. I would prefer however if I didn't have to change this myself after every update.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Teams opens when clicking the icon after an update
```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

OS (in case it's specific to this OS):
Distributor ID:	Ubuntu
Description:	Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS

Yes

I just passed from ubuntu 20.04  (teams and sharing screen was working one week ago on that ubuntu version) to 22.04 and reinstalled it via snap (tried both 1.12.7 and 1.13.0) via terminal V1.12.7
```
sudo wget -qO /etc/apt/keyrings/teams-for-linux.asc https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/teams-for-linux.asc
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/teams-for-linux.asc arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture)] https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/teams-for-linux-packages.list
sudo apt install teams-for-linux
```
and via flatpak:
```
sudo apt install flatpak
sudo apt install gnome-software-plugin-flatpak
flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo
flatpak install flathub com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux
flatpak run com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux
```
flatpak worked on a VM machine of ubuntu 22.04 but doesn't on my native ubuntu 22.04 pc. 
I tested with Teams snap, via firefox, opera and google chrome, same results every time. 

my camera is working just fine, but if I want to share, it gets :
Via snap: disconnected with the error "your video isn't working, we couldn't access your camera" (and if i press the button to get the camera it works again, but each time i want to share, it get disconnected again) 
via terminal install: 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a013e780-8571-41a2-a0a4-eeb6512ff1a1)

I select built-in-display and share, nothing happens, my camera is still running and i have the widget like I'm sharing my screen but no screen sharing. 
According to the snap store, I have all the required permissions. (Via terminal I'm not sure how to check) 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/904834bc-c6fd-4970-a44d-73c1f2872ba3)
(The video is black due to the cache on the webcam). While on a call, the other user will see nothing as well, so it's not just display on my computer. 

The issue is really just Teams, I can share screen via discord (I select monitor and it actually share it). 
In both cases, I have the icon on the upper right screen of ubuntu actually informing the user the screen is being shared.

So the command is passed to ubuntu but the image is not displayed in Teams. 

The most unsettling info is that Flatpak works on a VM machine of ubuntu, could be a clue that the issue is deep in the ubuntu 22.04 software. But I do not have the competency myself.

issues.title: [Feat]: Compatibility with password managers
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Teams for Linux should support integrating with password managers such as 1Password.

### Motivation

When running the PWA from a regular browser, I can use the browser's plugins, including 1Password. This enables me to log in without pasting email addresses and passwords between 1Password and Teams — and allows me to use the passkey saved in 1Password for 2FA.

This will be particularly important if my employer mandates passkey-based 2FA and locks out TOTP and other methods on company accounts.

### Alternatives

- Continuing to use a PWA installed under Chromium: not ideal as it keeps resetting the custom Teams icon I have given it to the Chrome icon, as it does with every other PWA
- Using a less secure method of 2FA: not ideal, as pasting email and password is already enough hassle without having to do it with TOTP codes too
- Asking my boss for a Macbook Pro and using macOS with its native app instead of Linux: at around ₤3200 for an equivalent machine, probably not very likely :)

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Hyprand - Teams window never shows again after being closed (hidden)
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

On Hyprland, hidden (closed) windows never show again.

### What package are you using?

build from source, flatpak, AppImage

### Version

reproducible on main

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Use hyprland desktop
2. Launch teams-for-linux (make sure tray is enabled)
3. Close the teams window
4. Do anything that should open it again, ie.
  - Launch a second instance
  - Left click tray icon
  - Right click tray icon and click open
5. Observe that window does not open
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Teams window re-opens
```

### Debug

```markdown
Logs don't display anything out of the ordinary
```

### Additional context

This can be fixed on Hyprland (and possibly other wayland compositors?) by calling [`window.setVisibleOnAllWorkspaces(true)`](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/browser-window#winsetvisibleonallworkspacesvisible-options-macos-linux) before any window.show() calls. This is a common workaround for electron apps in certain wayland compositors. I've tried this myself and it works.

It's worth noting that it *doesn't* make the window visible on all workspaces in hyprland, but would probably cause unintended side effects on other desktops, so might need a check for the desktop environment before calling.

We can use `process.env.XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP === "Hyprland"` to check.


Happy to submit a PR for this, but hoping someone has a more elegant solution in mind.

flatpak

4. When the workspace is focused, the session starts (splash screen then teams session)

issues.title: [Bug]: Unable to resize window; can only maximize
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

I am unable to resize the window. The minimize and maximize buttons do work. Maximize will  will maximize the window on first click then restore it to the smaller window on second click (expected behavior). I  am unable to get resize icons when trying to drag on the edge of the window to resize.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

1.13.1

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
Noted in description.
```

issues.body:

### Describe the bug

setting permissions properly

### What package are you using?

1.12.7

```markdown

```

```markdown
1. just open
```

### Debug

```markdown
bean@bean-82fe:~$ teams-for-linux 
[14817:0317/211910.791195:FATAL:setuid_sandbox_host.cc(163)] The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755.
Trace/breakpoint trap (core dumped)



just a suggestion, please add these commands to fix the issue, some people may not know about these steps to fix this

sudo chown root:root /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox
sudo chmod 4755 /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox

after using the commands above will make the app work normally
```

_No response_

issues.title: [Feat]: Remove teams-v1 support (EOL)
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Remove teams-v1 support as it reaches end of life

### Motivation

Currently, we default to teams-v1 allowing users to enable teams-v2.

This is a soft configuration that gracefully handles when people has already moved to teams-v2, but implies a lot of extra code that is now dead for teams-v1

As https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-classic-client-end-of-availability is reaching EOL in the 1st of july, it will be great to have a release ready before that. 

That will remove a lot of code that is only teams-v1 specific.

### Alternatives

Leave the dead code in there and default to teams-v2, or pretend this didn't happen

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Filter by keyword, for Planner, moved and is now missing in the application.
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the website/PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

In the Planner the keyword search has moved out from the "Filters" menu to the far left side of that row and is now represented by a magnifying glass. In addition the "Filters" and "Group by Bucket" menus have moved below the "Grid", "Board", etc... selections. As a result the ability to search via the new keyword search option is not present within this application.

The new view is observable in the website.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

v1.12.7

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Open Teams-for-Linux
2.Go to a tab with a Planner app.
3. I go to Board view but that should not be relevant.
4.Observe the lack of a keyword search option.
...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. To see the keyword search option within the Planner tab.
2.
3.
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'

Nothing interesting here.
```

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: moving electron to 33.4.1, and multiple auth refresh tries
issues.body:
- Removing enableBackgroundCallsAuthentication config option as it was broken, and probably not doing anything for us
- Update electron to 33.4.1
- Add website next to PWA in the bug template
- Adding permissionCheckHandler config option to verify if 3rd party cookies are affecting token refreshing. Default allowed domains/permissions are:
```
allowedDomains : [ 'microsoft.com', 'microsoftonline.com', 'teams.skype.com', 'teams.microsoft.com', 'sfbassets.com','skypeforbusiness.com'], allowedPermissions: [ 'background-sync', 'notifications', 'media', 'speaker-selection']
```

Fixes https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1573 
Trying to fix https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1357 (but I don't think it fully works... but worth a try I guess)

issues.title: [Feat]: Choose view (Chats, Teams, ...) to open on startup via config file
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

The app is by default opening the teams view. Customizing this by an entry in the config file would be great.

### Motivation

We use teams-for-linux in our company mostly for chatting and calling.
I almost never use the Teams view which is open by default when starting the application.
And the first thing that I do almost every morning is switching to the Chat view.
Sometimes, I forget to do this and ignore some chat message since I don't see any bold entries in the left pane.
Until I realize that this is the pane that lists all my teams instead of all my chats.
As given in the title, it would be nice to have an option in the config file for defining the startup view.
I would imagine that this is not a big deal, right?

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

No (preview is not supported there)

### Describe the bug

Previewing a document e.g. from a meeting invitation does not work anymore; trying to open it in e.g. Excel Web yields to an infinite loop of error messages

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

1.12.7

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Open meeting invitation with excel file attached
2. try to preview / open in Excel Web

```markdown
1. The file is previewed / browser is launched to open Excel for web
