issues.title: [Bug]: Output audio transmitted with the microphone
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

During a call, with microphone unmuted, if an audio is played is transmitted along the mic audio

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

1.12.7

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Start or enter a call
2. Check that the microphone is unmuted
3. Play whatever audio from whatever software (browser, VLC, WhatsApp or Telegram audio message, other players)
4. The other call participant(s) will listen to your audio transmitted along with the microphone audio (this not happens with muted mic)
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
The output audio is reproduced on the output device (speakers, headphones) for the user only and not transmitted along the microphone.
```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

To be clear, it's not like the output audio "re-enters" in the microphone, it's like the output audio is mixed with the input mic stream. Bug reproduced on Fedora 41, Portal for Linux installed from flatpak.

issues.title: [Bug]: Trouble Joining External Meeting with Meeting ID and 'Request to Join' - Possible Configuration Issue?
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

I am unable to join external Teams meetings that require the "Request to Join" feature when using a Meeting ID and Passcode in the teams-for-linux client. After entering the Meeting ID and Passcode, I do not see a "Request to Join" button or any indication that I'm in a lobby waiting to be admitted. Instead, I receive an error message.

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

1.12.7

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Receive an invitation to an external Teams meeting that provides a Meeting ID and Passcode and explicitly states that I need to "Request to join" the meeting.
2.Open the teams-for-linux client.
3.Attempt to join the meeting using the "Join with Meeting ID" feature.
4. Enter the provided Meeting ID and Passcode.
5. Observe that there is no "Request to Join" button or any indication of being placed in a lobby.
6. The client displays the error message: "Używane konto nie ma dostępu do tego spotkania ze względu na zasady organizacji." (The account being used does not have access to this meeting due to organization policies).
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
After entering the Meeting ID and Passcode, the client should either:
1. Display a "Request to Join" button that I can click to request entry to the meeting.
2. Automatically place me in a virtual lobby and indicate that I am waiting for the meeting organizer to admit me.
```

### Debug

```markdown
I'm not sure how to effectively use the --webDebug option in this scenario, as the issue seems to be related to the client's handling of the Meeting ID and "Request to Join" process, rather than a specific web element. If you can provide more specific instructions on how to use --webDebug to diagnose this particular issue, I'm happy to try it.
```

### Additional context

I am able to join the same meeting successfully using the Teams web app in my browser. In the web app, after entering the Meeting ID and Passcode, I am placed in a lobby, and there's a clear indication that I need to "Request to Join" before being admitted.

I initially tried creating an event in the teams-for-linux client by pasting the meeting details into a new calendar entry. However, the resulting event doesn't seem to be "joinable." When the meeting time arrives, I can't find a way to directly join the meeting from the event. It appears as a blank event. This may be a separate issue.

issues.title: Edit launch command to lower video receive resolution?
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Can someone help me lower video receive resolution via launch command? 

### Motivation

Hi there

Can anyone help me edit my launch command for Teams so that it runs on a much lower video receive resolution?

I ask because I cannot find any settings to edit in the application itself. I only have a low-megabit internet connection, and the high quality of the received Teams video is causing lag and delay.

I also ask because I was able to edit the launch command to disable GPU, which solved a blurry video problem I kept running into. If there is a command for that, I’m hoping that there will be a command to drop the receive video resolution to super-low res.

Here is the launch command as it stands:

**env BAMF_DESKTOP_FILE_HINT=/var/lib/snapd/desktop/applications/teams-for-linux_teams-for-linux.desktop /var/lib/snapd/snap/bin/teams-for-linux %U disable-gpu**

Thanks

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Password not saving & have to sign-in every time
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

When I would login before, it would save my login successfully if I ever restarted my PC or closed out the app. Now, every time I close it or restart my PC, I have to login again every single time. 

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

1.12.6

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Sign in to Teams
2. Close the application
3. I have to sign in again.
```

```markdown
1. Sign in to Teams
2. Close the app
3. Don't need to sign in again until I'm forced to by my org (1 week)
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Window stays offscreen after unplugging a monitor
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

When monitor, which displays the app, is disconnected, app remains offscreen

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

1.12.6

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Have 2 monitors, put Teams window on external monitor
2. Unplug the monitor
3. Resut: app is offscreen. When minimizing/maximixing, you can see it going away from screen. Only remedy is close/open the app again.
...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. App should detect being offscreen and move to main screen.
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

### Additional context

This happens everytime on my laptop, when I disconnect my external monitors.

Operating System: TUXEDO OS 
KDE Plasma Version: 6.2.5
KDE Frameworks Version: 6.10.0
Qt Version: 6.8.1
Kernel Version: 6.11.0-108013-tuxedo (64-bit)
Graphics Platform: Wayland

issues.title: [Bug]: debian package : the app fails to start due to a permission issue
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

/opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is not setuid root (user) after installation

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

1.12.6

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. install the debian package
2. start Teams
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
The /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox would be both owned by root and have mode 4755.
Suggestion : the postinst script could execute "chmod 4755 /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox".
```

### Debug

```markdown
Running teams shows the following message :

"[8062:0124/175501.605834:FATAL:setuid_sandbox_host.cc(163)] The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755.
"
```

### Additional context

_No response_

labels.name: duplicate
labels.description: This issue or pull request already exists

issues.title: [Bug]: Clicking Notification does not bring App in Foreground
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

When clicking on a Notification the app is not brought to the foreground

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

1.12.6

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Teams for Linux running in the background (not minimized)
2. Receive Message
3. Click on the Notification Popup
```

issues.title: [Feat]: Add tel: protocol support for Teams Phone Calls
issues.body:
### Describe the feature

When clicking on a tel:[phonenumber] link teams should open a message box and ask for starting a call

### Motivation

My company is using Teams Phone as our telephone system. Would be cool if we were able to open tel: links in teams-for-linux.

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

On Windows it look like this
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b257950-a85c-4a68-af87-0d228e2c729a)

I'm using Nobara as my distro.
I have added the following line to ~/.config/mimeapps.list
`x-scheme-handler/tel=teams-for-linux.desktop;`
It starts teams but then nothing happens.

issues.title: [Bug]: Meetings freeze after 20 minutes
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

After around 20 minutes, the meetings will freeze. Audio stops working and the video feeds stop. 

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

1.12.5

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Join or host a meeting.
2. Turn on video feed.
3. Leave the meeting up for about 20 minutes.

The meeting will freeze and I need to leave and rejoin the existing meeting.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
The meeting should not freeze.
```

### Debug

```markdown

```

### Additional context

I have reproduced under both Wayland and Xorg. I have no permissions overridden in the Flatpak. The application functions well otherwise and has until very recently. This has only begun happening since the last update.

I am happy to help provide any other technical details.

issues.title: Feature enable background authentication calls
issues.body:
- Adding enableBackgroundCallsAuthentication config option to validate if this keeps the user authenticated for longer
- Removing the writeUrlBlockLog function that use to log those requests into a file for debugging
- Handling of UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'webContents') at PowerMonitor.refresh (.../app/connectionManager/index.js:31:34)
- Checking the tray status before trying to update the badge count

Fixes https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1545. Might fix https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1521, https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1495 and https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1357

issues.title: Enable Background Authentication Calls and handing of undefined on lock resumption
issues.body:
* Adding enableBackgroundCallsAuthentication config option to validate if this keeps the user authenticated for longer
* Removing the writeUrlBlockLog function that use to log those requests into a file for debugging
* Handling of UnhandledPromiseRejectionWarning: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'webContents') at PowerMonitor.refresh (.../app/connectionManager/index.js:31:34)
* Checking the tray status before trying to update the badge count

Fixes #1545. Might fix #1521, #1495 and #1357

issues.title: [Bug]: Cannot switch back to company account after having temporarily used personal one
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

Yes

### Describe the bug

Cannot switch back to company account after having temporarily used personal one

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

1.12.5

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.Have a company and a personal Teams account
2.From a clean state (fresh app install/or all app data reset), log into your company account
3.In the top right corner, log out using the menu in your avatar
4.when the window refreshed to the login one, authenticate with your personal account
5.Now logout from your personal account
6.when the window refreshed to the login one, authenticate with your company account again
7.error saying the email address is unknown (see details for screenshot), although that email definitely exists
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.app state should be cleared properly, allowing to switch back and forth between work (or school) and personal accounts. I suspect it's only working well in one direction (work to personal), but not back?

Workaround was to use `Quit (Clear Storage)`
```

### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

### Additional context

Screenshot of error showing when trying to authenticate on my work account again, after having temporarily used my personal one:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24dea61f-ecfa-4bb5-addb-88af562901dc)

### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

No

### Describe the bug

Passkey Logins trigger a "Your device will open a security window" message, which never opens

### What package are you using?

aur

issues.title: [Bug]: Custom CSS not (fully?) applied when connecting as guest
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

`--customCSSName compactDark` only affects the loading screen, not the meeting itself

### What package are you using?

aur

### Version

1.12.5-1

### Reproduction steps

- Open a meeting using the `--customCSSName compactDark` command-line option (no Microsoft account)
- When loading, the screen is in dark mode for a moment. However, the "join meeting" screen is in light mode as usual, as is the actual meeting.

This *used* to work a few weeks ago, I think the CSS on the server side might have changed. Can anyone confirm the behavior? I'm not sure if there's an account setting on the server side that can affect this as well.

### Expected Behavior

Apply the dark theme also in the meeting itself.

issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Ability to open a meeting using only id and key

### Motivation

Currently Teams help at MS try to say that open calendar in teams and open from there. I just had a public conference meeting that was copy&pasted into calendar, so teams did not recognize the link. Link opens browser. Could not find anywhere to use ID/key and MS web-page for id/key again just opens the browser.

This might not be a problem with teams-for-linux as such, but looking for a work around. Perhaps teams-for linux could have a menu-item where one could enter id/key, perhaps also a meeting url since recent MS shenanigans.
Personally I rather not use Teams at all, unfortunately it is not something I can do at work.

### Alternatives

_No response_

issues.title: [Bug]: Cannot use fcitx to change input method in teams-for-linux
issues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug in the PWA?

YES

### Describe the bug

Cannot use fcitx to change input method in teams-for-linux

### What package are you using?

AppImage

### Version

1.12.5

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Install teams-for-linux 1.12.5 (I tried appimage, the tarball, and deb file, but none of them worked).
2. Make sure fcitx is running in the background and the user can switch the input methods in other apps, such as firefox, brave-browser, etc.
3. Try to switch fcitx input method in teams-for-linux

Observation: I can only input English in teams-for-linux, even if the fcitx is switched to use Chinese. I also verified that if I move the focus to other apps, I will be using Chinese, but I still can only input English in teams-for-linux.
```


### Expected Behavior

```markdown
Teams for linux should follow the fcitx's current input method.
```


### Debug

```markdown
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```


### Additional context

I am running Solus linux with hyprland. `

The workaround suggested in #1340 does not work for me. and `QT_IM_MODULE`  and `GTK_IM_MODULE` are both set to `fcitx`. I am using fcitx 4.2.9.9

Keeps processes running after quitting
issues.body:
After quitting the application (from the tray), ps shows there are still processes running.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start Teams and log in.
2. Log out and quit Teams (using menu in tray).
3. In a terminal type ps aux | grep teams | grep -v grep
4. Find three processes labeled  /snap/teams-for-linux/724/teams-for-linux.

**Expected behavior**
Quitting an application should remove all its processes IMHO.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Kubuntu 24.10
 - Installation package snap
 - Version 1.12.5
 

**Debug**
See attached file.
[teamsdebug.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18218425/teamsdebug.txt)

issues.title: Feature: Make sure the version in use is stable
issues.body:
According to this issue [link](https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/msteams/forum/all/web-teams-microphone-issue/6406dab7-4f50-4ec6-b38d-af9aca2ea132), Microsoft Teams can choose some users to be on some testing group.

As we in linux have so much barriers to overcome, I think it would be great if we could always be on a stable environment.

According to the issue, there is a way to force the use of a stable web version of msteams.

```
For now, changing the link to the following works:
https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/?ring=general
This forces teams to use the pool of regular mainstream users that doesn't get any experimental features such as the one that's currently breaking everything...
```

Can/should this be the default for this snap? 
If not, is it possible to create some documentation that allows the user to change it if wanted/needed?

issues.title: Receiving "Waiting for Network" window when trying to open app in Nobara > Fedora 40
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
On initial install/launch, the application window hangs on "Waiting for Network", and will not progress

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install application
2. Open Application post install
3. Login using a gmail (or self hosted) e-mail address
4. Upon authentication, the end result is a window where the header = "Waiting for Network"

**Expected behavior**
Upon login, I should be able to access the GUI.

**Screenshots**
I have included a console snippet along with the window: 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c198d4a2-2807-4df4-a331-783025f8e8fa)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Nobara (Fedora 40)
 - Installation package: Flatpak and rpm -i ...
![Version:](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ee41c33e-c8d5-4dee-b5b6-4501d2b21c3f)

**Debug**
Above screenshot shows the output from the console; network shows normal behavior (200, 300 HTTP response types_

The provide in this section the output from both the terminal and the browser debug console.

```bash
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

*IMPORTANT:* Ensure that you mask any sensitive information before posting the output.

Add any other context about the problem here.

issues.title: Add option for supporting camera via pipewire
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

When using camera that relies on libcamera (the most famous is ipu6 cams from intel, cf https://jgrulich.cz/2024/12/13/when-your-webcam-doesnt-work-solving-firefox-and-pipewire-issues/ for some related context), chrome must be configured to use pipewire for the camera in one of its flags:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e371e13-b086-4ca3-bfdf-c334b499c594)

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Ideally I would like to have a switch somewhere in the config (because I guess not everybody wants this feature) that would enable the flag.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Using chromium or firefox directly and not this app :)

**Additional context**

I worry that it's not possible to do this from teams-for-linux and that support must be added directly at the electron level?

issues.title: `firejail teams-for-linux` requires `/opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox` owned by root and has mode 4755
issues.body:
#### `firejail teams-for-linux` requires `/opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox` owned by root and has mode 4755

#### To Reproduce

1. Open linux terminal (gnome-terminal or tilix)
2. Type `firejail teams-for-linux`
3. See error: `The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755. Parent is shutting down, bye...`

#### Tried the [proposed solution](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1426#issuecomment-2384889316) to add apparmour file. It didn't solve the issue.

#### Proposed solution(s)

To fix this, the debian install script needs to make a `chmod 4755 $(pkgdir_buildroot_debian)/opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox` or have a `debian/postinst` file which does `chmod 4755 /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox` after install. Probably needed for all distributions, such as Fedora/openSUSE/Archlinux/Manjaro.

#### System

 - OS: Ubuntu Budgie 24.04
 - Installation package: deb (from your repository)
 - Version: 1.12.3

#### Reference(s)

Maybe similar root cause as in issue [#1426](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1426#issuecomment-2431985357) but there is no concrete solution in the thread mentioning `firejail` and the choice to force changed permissions on the affected directory when the DEB package is created or include a `postinst` file to fix the issue after install.

issues.title: use console.warn instead of console.warning
When waking from sleep, messages don't get updated
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When I put my computer to sleep and then someone sends me messages on Teams, sometimes those messages don't show up when I wake the PC up, even after the network connection is re-established.

Further incoming messages do show up though, placing the chat in an inconsistent state (missing messages).

If I press CTRL+R to reload Teams, the missing messages show up and everything goes back to normal.

This is not 100% reproducible every time, which makes me thing it might depend on how fast the PC can restore the network connection after waking up.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Teams.
2. Put PC to sleep.
3. Ask someone to send you a message while the PC is sleeping.
4. Wake PC from sleep.
5. Notice that the message does not show up on the chat.

**Expected behavior**
The messages should show up as soon as the network connection is re-established.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch Linux
 - Installation package AUR
 - Version 1.12.3

If you believe it can help, I can provide logs the next time it happens.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Intune does not support the system that I am using.

I hope the answer to this request is not something already available: my search (and my understanding) didn't lead me to anything useful.

I'd like to pass an `oauth` token obtained form another machine to `teams-for-linux`. Is this currently possible or can it be potentially enabled?

Thanks.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
That's the only solution to the problem that I can think of.

**Additional context**
Available on specific request.

issues.title: Blank/silent audio input (Ultramarine / Fedora 41 KDE Plasma)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
On two different PCs recently upgraded to Ultramarine 41 6.11.10-300.fc41.x86_64, Teams is unable to receive any audio data. Devices are set correctly and the mixer shows audio coming in for the application, but there is no audio on the receiving end. This is confirmed when doing an echo test call.

**To Reproduce**
Launch Teams
Connect to a call
Verify audio devices
Connect, audio will show on OS mixer VU for microphone, but Teams does not receive the audio data

**Expected behavior**
Outgoing audio would be heard by meeting participants

**Screenshots**
N/A

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ultramarine Linux (Fedora 41) Plasma Edition 6.11.10-300.fc41.x86_64
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version 1.12.3
 - Issue present with Intel Alder-Lake built-in audio, ASUS Xonar SE 5.1 PCIe, Plantronics USB headset and Logitech C270 webcam.

**Debug**
When possible, please run the application from the terminal using `--webDebug` and try to reproduce the error.

The provide in this section the output from both the terminal and the browser debug console.

```bash
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

*IMPORTANT:* Ensure that you mask any sensitive information before posting the output.

**Additional context**
Testing works via Chrome browser to mictests.com
pavucontrol shows the application under recording when a call is live, and audio is visible on the meter
This also seems to affect the Linux Zoom application, but not Webex.

issues.title: Screen sharing refreshing time
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.
After I join the call and share my entire screen, the refresh time for the screen sharing is very long, often taking several minutes, sometimes even up to five minutes. It is not observed at the beginning of the call but rather later.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Join any call
2. start sharing entire screen
3. change pages on ppt/browser/VSC
4. Sreen share will not show currently showed page

**Expected behavior**
refresh time will be more or less similar to what I see

**Screenshots**
n/a

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package deb
 - Version 1.9.4 but today my colleague was presenting using the newest version and the issue is still observed

**Debug**
When possible, please run the application from the terminal using `--webDebug` and try to reproduce the error.

The provide in this section the output from both the terminal and the browser debug console.

```bash
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

*IMPORTANT:* Ensure that you mask any sensitive information before posting the output.

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

issues.title: [SNAP] version LIBDBUS_PRIVATE_1.12.20 not found
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When installed via Snap, Electron cannot target the correct libdbus version.

**To Reproduce**
1. Install Teams-for-Linux via Snap
2. Add the following to ~/snap/teams-for-linux/current/.config/teams-for-linux/config.json : 
```
{
	"ssoInTuneEnabled": true,
	"ssoInTuneAuthUser": "your-corporate-email",
	"logLevels": "debug"
}
```
3. Start the app via cli with `/snap/bin/teams-for-linux`

**Expected behavior**
The DBus lib should succesfully query the DBus intune broker


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package snap
 - Version 1.11.5

**Debug**
```
dbus-send: /snap/teams-for-linux/715/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libdbus-1.so.3: version `LIBDBUS_PRIVATE_1.12.20' not found (required by dbus-send)
```

**Additional Context**
I believe it's the same bug as described here : https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/issues/8234
They fixed it in Bitwarden with this commmit : https://github.com/bitwarden/clients/pull/11932/files

Would it be possible to take a look ?

Thanks in advance!

issues.title: Ubuntu, Wayland, screenshare not working
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I am using the Teams for Linux client on Ubuntu 24.04.1 and I cannot get the screenshare function to work on Wayland. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go on "Meet now" from Calendar
2. Start meeting
3. Click on screenshare
4. See error
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e5bb2bf1-5b60-4355-b0b9-d5985c656947)


**Expected behavior**
I expected the behavior to being able to share my screen. If I run Teams through Firefox I am able to share my screen without issues.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.04.1
 - Installation package: snap I think (?)
 - Version: 1415/24102001318.

**Debug**

The provide in this section the output from both the terminal and the browser debug console.

```bash
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

issues.title: Call in separate window
issues.body: Currently call or meeting opens same window, it would be great having call in separate window. I know can move to chat and call will be minized to small popup, but yet is not how desktop version do. Just wondering if that could be possible.

issues.title: How to call a secondary ringer script?
issues.body:
I am using teams-for-linux (1.11.5, installed from tar.gz; **a great project, thanks!**) and since a few days we use it as phone too. That works, but there is (neither in the web version nor in teams-for-linux (of course)) the option to use/configure a "secondary ringer" (as in the Windows version), so that the PC speakers would ring too on an incoming call. And only the headphones - if I do not wear them - are very silent.

There seems to be a perfect solution for that problem - a command line option:

`teams-for-linux --incomingCallCommand /path/to/my/secondary-ringer-program.sh`

However, it does not seem to work. Is that option not meant for that task? What is it used for?
There are some bug reports, which mention secondary ringer scripts (I saw them), but no description, how it they should be called.

Best regards, Wolfgang

issues.title: SSO doesn't work for Flathub variant
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
My company uses SSO with TFA for Microsoft products. This issue occurs only for Flathub variant. Meanwhile deb variant works, it is inconvenient without auto-update.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install from Flathub
2. Run
3. Enter SSO credentials on login prompt
4. The window with TFA token request is empty (see the screenshot)

**Expected behavior**
Should successfully request thee TFA token and thus pass authentication

**Screenshots**
![Screenshot from 2024-11-29 10-04-48](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2fad347f-45f7-4a94-bac7-8c10a2c81775)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04 (x11 mode)
 - Installation package flatpak from Flathub
 - Version 1.11.5

issues.title: libffmpeg.so: cannot open shared object file
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I just installed Teams-for-Linux on a brand new Ubuntu 24.04.1.
To remove the "The SUID sandbox" error message, I add the appamor file as described on https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1426#issuecomment-2384889316

Now, I have this new error message when I start teams-for-linux and it exits
> teams-for-linux: error while loading shared libraries: libffmpeg.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory

**To Reproduce**
Install teams-for-linux on a new Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS using
```
sudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings
sudo wget -qO /etc/apt/keyrings/teams-for-linux.asc https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/teams-for-linux.asc
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/teams-for-linux.asc arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture)] https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/teams-for-linux-packages.list
sudo apt update
sudo apt install teams-for-linux
```
and
* copy content of [teams-for-linux.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17193424/teams-for-linux.txt) to /etc/apparmor.d/teams-for-linux
* reload AppArmor: sudo systemctl reload apparmor.service
* starts teams-for-linux
```
$ teams-for-linux
teams-for-linux: error while loading shared libraries: libffmpeg.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
$
```
**Expected behavior**
Teams-for-linux starts.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.04.1
 - Installation package : sudo apt install teams-for-linux
 - Version 1.11.5 (from "apt-cache policy teams-for-linux")

issues.title: fix(Intune): correctly iterate when ssoInTuneAuthUser is provided
issues.body:
We need to iterate the values, not the indexes.

closes: #1466

issues.title: Join Teams meeting without a login
issues.body:
Upon startup, this app prompts for a login.  I would love it if the app could also connect to a Teams meeting _without_ a login such as through the URL provided in a Teams invite and/or by specifying the meeting ID and passcode via the method indicated here: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-teams/join-a-meeting

I'm in a computing environment where the Admin gave us a video conferencing tool other than Teams and they make it difficult for folks to connect to a Teams meeting (I even get an error message about Teams being restricted by my Admin if I try to login on a device from home).  Unfortunately, most of our vendors host meetings via Teams, so being able to use this app to join a Teams meeting without a login could go a long way towards improving my Teams experience.

issues.title: Calendar doesn't sync anymore
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Calendar in teams does not sync. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Calendar View
2. Notice the Warning logo next to Last Updated time and the Try Again link
3. Click Try Again
4. Notice Last Updated doesn't change and meetings planned are not up-to-date

**Expected behavior**
Planned meetings should be present in calendar and sync should occur normally

**Screenshots**

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.10
 - Installation package : deb
 - Version 1.11.5

**Debug**
When possible, please run the application from the terminal using `--webDebug` and try to reproduce the error.

```bash
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}'
```

None

issues.title: URLs don't open in an external browser in Plasma 6
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Since the latest Plasma Update in Debian Sid, external URLs won't open in the system's default browser (in my case Firefox) window anymore. The Not-Allowed cursor shorty flashes and the debug console writes:
```
08:51:48.380 › Requesting to open 'https://some.link'
```
but nothing happens. Internal links to other channels seem to work fine.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Find a message with an external URL
2. Click on the URL

**Expected behavior**
The link opens in your default browser in a new tab.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Debian Sid, KDE Plasma 6.2.3, Wayland
 - teams-for-linux 1.12.1, Debian package

If I can help out with more information, please let me know.

issues.title: Crash when clicking sys tray icon to re-"open" app on RHEL8

A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

Using the flatpak, if one closes the app to the systray and then tries to re-open, it crashes.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. RHEL8 in wayland mode with nouveau driver in classic gnome shell with redhat's using gnome-shell-extension-top-icons-3.32.1-39.el8_10.noarch
2. version 1.11.5 of the flatpak
3. Start teams for linux
4. click the 'x' to close the app to the systray
5. click the systray icon and choose to reopen the app

issues.title: White screen after login
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I am getting the email and password prompts; and after that, even the question to "stay signed in" – it doesn't matter whether I click Yes or No here.

After that, there are some loading icons but eventually, I'm stuck on the white screen afterwards. (Technically, there is a white box with rounded corners and drop-shadow).

Upon opening the debug mode and refreshing, the console shows a multitude of errors.

```
[Auth]: auth_cache initialized

[Auth]: [Telemetry][f9109af9-7bca-4755-aee5-b10a77f51336][auth_web_initialization] [start] - telemetry provider created for createAuthenticationServiceInstance, source: core-auth-tasks::init with shouldCreateScenario: [true]

Failed to override MS Method
Failed to start Activity Hub by assigning Event Handlers
Failed to set Default Title
Failed to set Machine State
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 () manifest.json:1 
```

I get the same result when visiting https://teams.live.com/v2/ directly – which seems to be what this app is doing too. (tested in Chrome, Firefox, even Edge, deactivated all browser extensions, tried in incognito mode, turned off the VPN I am behind)

Further interesting error messages I get over in the browser:

```
Unhandled error/rejection {"isTrusted":true}
GET https://teams.live.com/v2/manifest.json 404 (Not Found)

Uncaught (in promise) {
	code: "Unknown"
	context: "DataLayerClient"
	errorCode: undefined
	isExpected: false
	message: "RequireStatusFailed"
	name: "PresenceCreateEndpointJob"
	originalErrorMessage: "RequireStatusFailed"
	originalErrorReason: "RequireStatusFailed"
	reason: undefined
	serverResponse: {
		status: 500
		url: "https://presence.teams.live.com/v1/me/endpoints/"
	statusCode: 500
	subContext: "PresenceCreateEndpointJob"
}
```

Why is the manifest not reachable? And why only for me?

Honestly, I'm not sure whether there is anything you can do if it's a server-side issue. But is it truly? Maybe you can help me debug this. Just because you have the insight about how this login flow works, I mean. The last commit to this repository having been a few hours ago makes me believe you aren't getting this. It all points to a local issue with my personal machine. Otherwise, I'd find way more search results about login issues like this.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open app.
2. Enter credentials and log in.
3. → White screen / error in console.

**Expected behavior**
The user is either logged in and lands on a dashboard or landing page – or they get an error message displayed in the frontend, not dev console.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version: 1.11.5 stable

issues.title: Adding contentIsolation to the loginForm and removing the already deprecated/automated onlineCheckMethod config option
issues.body:
* Improving security on the loginForm by removing the need of contentIsolation (#986)
* Removing the already deprecated `onlineCheckMethod` option from the config options
* Updating the HISTORY section to be more inline with the current stated of affairs

issues.title: Flatpak: Generic Wayland icon is shown in KDE task switcher
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
On KDE, when you use Alt+Tab to switch between applications in a Wayland session the generic Wayland icon is shown.

This is probably caused by some metadata missing in the `package.json` (see below).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Log in to a KDE Plasma 6 Wayland session
2. Install and start the flatpak
3. Press and hold Alt+Tab
4. See generic yellow wayland icon

**Expected behavior**
The same icon as in the task manager should be displayed.

**Screenshots**
Can't take a screenshot of the task switcher

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: KDE Neon
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version: 1.11.5

**Additional context**

According to https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/electron.html#using-correct-desktop-file-name should this be fixed by adding a line
```json
  "desktopName": "com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux.desktop",
```
to the file `package.json`. But only for the flatpak builds.

Please give me some advices how to start teams (flags to enable logs,change levels), which logs/files deliver etc. to help you investigate problem - unfortunately I got very busy time now and have no time to RTFM :)

Thanks in advance gw.

issues.title: Window cannot be resized
issues.body:
After the update to v1.11.5, the window cannot be resized anymore. It can only be used in a preset size (too small for many use cases) or in full screen.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start fresh session.
2. Move mouse pointer to any edge or corner of the Teams window.
3. The mouse pointer does not change to the expected "icon" (like the "corner" or "edge" icon) and the size cannot be changed.

**Expected behavior**
When moving the mouse pointer to any edge or corner of the Teams window, the mouse pointer should indicate that here, the size can be changed and the window size should be adjustable.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux 6.11.9-arch1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun, 17 Nov 2024 16:06:17 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
 - Installation package: flatpak
 - Version: 1.11.5
 - Gnome-shell 47.1-1, Wayland, electron 32.2.5-1 and 33.2.0-1

**Additional context**
While the version from flatpak shows this behaviour, the one from the Arch Linux repository appears not to be affected and works as expected.

labels.description: Workaround available in the know_issues.md file

issues.title: Error "Fetched custom background remote configuration but failed to save ... configJSON is not iterable" in logs
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

See: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1424#issuecomment-2480912442

When starting teams-for-linux, I see an error `Fetched custom background remote configuration but failed to save at '/home/myuser/.config/teams-for-linux/custom_bg_remote.json'. configJSON is not iterable` in the logs. Everything works normal and I can select my custom backgrounds, it's just the error log that confuses me.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have a working custom-background server
2. Configure teams-for-linux to use the custom-background server
3. Start teams-for-linux from a terminal (so you see logs)
4. See error logged

**Expected behavior**
If the custom backgrounds are working, I would expect no error to be logged.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux / Gentoo
 - Installation package: [ebuild](https://github.com/SuperTux88/gentoo-overlay/blob/860fa03bca8592a8e55e96d2bd6d2a7055ff788d/net-im/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux-1.11.5.ebuild) based on the `deb` package
 - Version: 1.11.5 (but I noticed that the error was already logged with an older 1.11.0 version)

**Debug**

```bash
teams-for-linux --webDebug --logConfig='{}' --customBGServiceBaseUrl=http://localhost:8080
```
```
all good with appLogLevels you aren't using them
all good with onlineCheckMethod you aren't using them
Initialising logger with config: "{}"
19:45:45.164 › File logging at /home/myuser/.config/teams-for-linux/logs/main.log with level silly
19:45:45.166 › Console logging enabled with level silly
19:45:45.166 › Logger initialised
19:45:45.166 › configPath: /home/myuser/.config/teams-for-linux
19:45:45.166 › configFile: {
  optInTeamsV2: true,
  isCustomBackgroundEnabled: true,
  customBGServiceBaseUrl: 'https://my-usual-custom-bg-url.example.com',
  followSystemTheme: true
}
19:45:45.168 › Running under Wayland, switching to PipeWire...
19:45:45.332 › Fetching custom background configuration from 'http://localhost:8080/config.json'
19:45:45.370 › Custom background service url is 'http://localhost:8080'
19:45:45.370 › processArgs: [
  '/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux',
  '--webDebug',
  '--logConfig={}',
  '--customBGServiceBaseUrl=http://localhost:8080'
]
19:45:45.371 › testing RegExp processArgs false
19:45:45.371 › testing RegExp processArgs false
19:45:45.371 › testing RegExp processArgs false
19:45:45.371 › testing RegExp processArgs false
19:45:45.371 › Waiting for network...
19:45:45.371 › Testing network using net.request() for https://teams.microsoft.com
19:45:45.372 › Spellchecker is disabled!
19:45:45.476 › Fetched custom background remote configuration but failed to save at '/home/myuser/.config/teams-for-linux/custom_bg_remote.json'. configJSON is not iterable
[1428790:1117/194545.615229:ERROR:nss_util.cc(345)] After loading Root Certs, loaded==false: NSS error code: -8018
19:45:45.650 › Network test successful with method https
...
```

**Additional context**

> You need a server running to use the custom background (see the docs and the example in the custom background section. Config area). My guess is you are probably not running a server or you haven't got the right config, but I will be able to tell more by looking at the config and your logs.

So as written in the comment on the other issue, it's mostly about the error log that appears. I don't know since when this error gets logged, I just noticed it yesterday (I usually don't look at the logs), but I also checked on another computer with an older version and at least on 1.11.0 it also already gets logged.

I have a working server running, I use it since a long time (even before v2, but I then changed the json to match the v2 format). And it's working, I see my custom backgrounds from my server. But I also see above error message in the logs.

My existing server which is configured in my config is nginx based. However when I clone the repo and start `npx http-server -p 8080 --cors "*" -g` in this folder: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/tree/main/app/customBackground/example, and start teams with `--customBGServiceBaseUrl=http://localhost:8080` to override my own server, it also still logs the error, but it now shows me the 3 images from that folder instead of my own images. So it loads the config from the temporary localhost server (I can see it in the access log). If I start teams without that parameter again, it uses the URL from the config file again, and all my own custom backgrounds are back. So it loads the custom backgrounds, but still logs the error every time teams-for-linux gets started.

However, the error isn't about that custom backgrounds can't be loaded, but that it can't store the downloaded backgrounds config locally. But the file still exists and has the content from my usual custom background server (If I start with the localhost background server, it doesn't get changed to that new content). It was last changed on September 3rd (so that's probably since around when the error gets logged, but I just didn't notice it?). If I remove the file, it doesn't get recreated and the error is still logged. But custom-background also still work.

So, my questions are:
* Why is this file needed? Custom backgrounds seem to work perfectly even if it can't be written to that file.
* Why is the error logged? It must have worked at some point, but something is broken now (but it doesn't affect the actual usage of custom backgrounds, just storing the local file is broken)

issues.title: "The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly"
issues.body:
Whenever I upgrade teams-for-linux, I can't run it:

```
teams-for-linux                  
[12870:1115/084054.418986:FATAL:setuid_sandbox_host.cc(163)] The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755.
[1]    12870 trace trap (core dumped)  teams-for-linux
```

So, after every upgrade I have to run:
```
sudo chmod 4755 /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox
```

After this it starts normally. So, I suspect that it might be some packaging issue...

**Desktop:**
 - OS: KDE Neon(based on Ubuntu Noble/24.04)
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 1.11.5

issues.title: Explicitly setting "ssoInTuneAuthUser" is broken
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When explicitly specifying `ssoInTuneAuthUser` in the config, the inTune account will not be found even if it exists.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. set `ssoInTuneEnabled` to `true` and `ssoInTuneAuthUser` to an account that exists

**Expected behavior**
The account is found.

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.04 with working InTune
 - deb Installation
 - Version 1.11.2

**Debug**
When setting `ssoInTuneAuthUser`:

```
09:12:17.139 › Failed to find matching InTune account for myname@mycorp.de.
```

When only setting `ssoInTuneEnabled` to `true` and not specifying the user, so it uses the first one found:

```
09:58:14.080 › Using first available InTune account (myname@mycorp.de)
```

**Additional context**

It seems to be a bug in this part of `app/index.js`:

```javascript
		for (const account in response.accounts) {
			if (account.username == ssoInTuneAuthUser) {
				inTuneAccount = account;
				console.debug('Found matching InTune account (' + inTuneAccount.username + ')');
				break;
			}
		}
```

I will just quote our friend GitHub Copilot here:
The behavior you are observing is due to the way the `processInTuneAccounts` function is implemented. Here is a brief explanation:

**When `ssoInTuneAuthUser` is specified:**
   - The function iterates over the `response.accounts` array to find an account with a username that matches `ssoInTuneAuthUser`.
   - The loop uses `for (const account in response.accounts)`, which iterates over the keys (indices) of the `response.accounts` array, not the actual account objects.
   - As a result, `account.username` is `undefined`, and the comparison `account.username == ssoInTuneAuthUser` always fails.

issues.title: Video from own webcam is low resolution and pixelated
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

When in a teams meeting, my video from my own webcam is at a very low resolution and quite pixelated.
It looks like it's at a much lower resolution than 360p, and downscaled using a poor scaling.  I checked with other people and they also see my video quality is poor and pixelated, consistent with the way I'm seeing myself.

My webcam is 1080p capable.  There are no settings for choosing resolution in teams.  This problem only started happening a few weeks ago; previously it was fine.  Due to difficulties reproducing this unless I'm in a meeting with multiple people I haven't had much of a chance to test.

The webcam is a HiCamera UVC Camera.  In Cheese, it defaults to 1280x720 but I can set it as high as 1920x1080 and it works.  Interestingly, even at 360p, its lowest option, my camera looks fine, just soft, not pixelated like the problematic behavior I'm seeing in Teams.

Other people in the meeting appear normal (their video from their webcam is clear, not pixely, and high resolution).

If I turn off my webcam and turn it on again, while still in the meeting, for the first 1-2 seconds my video is normal and high resolution without the pixely effect then it starts.


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Join an existing meeting that multiple other people are already in (I couldn't reproduce this in a meeting I started with myself as the only participant)
2. When joining, observe the video preview on the left
3. After joining, observe the view of your own webcam video in the meeting

I tend to use a background effect on my video but when I was testing it happened both with and without the background effect enabled.

**Expected behavior**
Video is clear and looks like it would in any other app (eg in the "cheese" app)

**Actual behavior**

Low resolution poorly scaled pixelated video for me and everyone else's video looks normal.

**Screenshots**
Difficult to add a screenshot without privacy issues

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux Mint 21 (Ubuntu 22.04)
 - Installation package: flatpak
 - Version: 1.11.2

**Debug**
Was unable to reproduce in a meeting with myself.  Will

issues.title: App lets you click through it on Wayland
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Application lets click through the window's border (both titlebar and app's content) instead of receiving the input itself.
Check video, it should be explanatory enough :)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Use Wayland
2. Open app
3. Try to click on the outer border of the app (like 10px more-less)
4. App or object behind (on Z axis) receives the input

**Expected behavior**
App should receive the input, not what's behind it.

**Screenshots**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9408e6e5-174c-4e30-8bdd-c08a29158749


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f0a5a2e4-ef99-4159-b316-159b3d668da5


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 41 Workstation
 - Installation package: RPM
 - Version 1.11.2

issues.title: Custom Virtual Background
issues.body:
**Choosing a virtual background for meetings can be helpful for several reasons:**

- **Professionalism**: A virtual background can help create a more professional and polished appearance for your meetings, even if you are working from home or in a less-than-ideal environment.
- **Focus**: A virtual background can help you stay focused on the meeting by minimizing distractions in your background.
- **Brand consistency**: If your organization has a standard virtual background, using it can help create a consistent brand image for your meetings.
- **Engagement**: A virtual background can help make your meetings more engaging by adding a touch of personality or creativity.

Allowing users to add a custom background to meetings would be a great feature because it would give users more flexibility and control over their meeting experience. This could be especially helpful for users who want to represent their brand, hobbies, or interests in their virtual background.

issues.title: The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly.
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

After updating to version 1.11.0, the following error appears, when trying to launch the application: 

[25746:0930/073047.604531:FATAL:setuid_sandbox_host.cc(163)] The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755.

Seems to be caused by the Electron update and related to this issue: https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/42510

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install Teams for Linux v1.11.0 on Ubuntu 24.04.
2. Try to launch the application.
3. Error.

**Expected behavior**

I launch the application and it launches.

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 1.11.0

**Debug**

Does not output anything extra besides the error mentioned at the beginning.

**Additional context**

Can be worked around by lifting Ubuntu's restrictions: https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/42510#issuecomment-2171583086, I am however pretty sure this is not the correct solution.

Since moving to Ubuntu 24.04.1, I cannot get TFL to load my config

**To Reproduce**

1. Install TFL snap package
2. Start TFL - `teams-for-linux --appLogLevels error,warn,info,debug`
3. Note the configPath `configPath: /home/akk/snap/teams-for-linux/671/.config/teams-for-linux`
4. Note that configFile is empty `configFile: {}`

**Expected behavior**

issues.title: When attempting to join a Microsoft Teams meeting, I get the following error: "...Make sure you're connected to the internet..."
issues.body:
When attempting to join a Microsoft Teams meeting, you may encounter the following error:
"Sorry we couldn't connect your call. Make sure you're connected to the internet and try again."

This issue can occur even when you have a working internet connection.

Solution:
The problem can often be resolved by changing your DNS (Domain Name System) settings. Here's how to do it on both Linux and Windows systems:

For Linux (Ubuntu/Debian):

1. Open a terminal.
2. Edit the resolv.conf configuration file:
   ```
   sudo nano /etc/resolvconf/resolv.conf.d/head
   ```
3. Add the following lines:
   ```
   nameserver 8.8.8.8
   nameserver 8.8.4.4
   ```
4. Save and exit the file (Ctrl+X, then Y, then Enter).
5. Update the system's resolv.conf:
   ```
   sudo resolvconf -u
   ```
6. Verify the changes:
   ```
   cat /etc/resolv.conf
   ```

For Windows:

1. Open the Control Panel.
2. Go to Network and Internet > Network and Sharing Center.
3. Click on "Change adapter settings" on the left side.
4. Right-click on your active network connection and select "Properties".
5. Select "Internet Protocol Version 4 (TCP/IPv4)" and click "Properties".
6. Select "Use the following DNS server addresses".
7. Enter the following:
   Preferred DNS server: 8.8.8.8
   Alternate DNS server: 8.8.4.4
8. Click OK to save the changes.
9. Open Command Prompt as Administrator.
10. Run the following command to flush the DNS cache:
    ```
    ipconfig /flushdns
    ```

After making these changes, restart your Microsoft Teams application and try joining the meeting again. The connection issue should be resolved.

Note: The DNS servers used in this solution (8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4) are Google's public DNS servers. They are generally reliable and fast, but you can use other public DNS servers or your ISP's DNS servers if preferred.

If you continue to experience issues, please contact your IT support team or Microsoft Teams support for further assistance.

Citations:
[1] https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/networking/dns/troubleshoot/troubleshoot-dns-client
[2] https://www.ricmedia.com/tutorials/set-permanent-dns-nameservers-ubuntu-debian-resolv-conf
[3] https://learn.microsoft.com/en-US/windows-server/networking/dns/troubleshoot/troubleshoot-dns-server
[4] https://www.tecmint.com/set-permanent-dns-nameservers-in-ubuntu-debian/
[5] https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/configuring_and_managing_networking/manually-configuring-the-etc-resolv-conf-file_configuring-and-managing-networking
[6] https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/networking/troubleshoot-dns-guidance
[7] https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftedge/forum/all/dns-isnt-working-on-my-pc/86a6b382-2bf2-4263-b059-e816554a1897
[8] https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/msteams/forum/all/dns-resolution-failed-microsoft-teams-network/ba79627c-d39f-421b-9d5e-afa770c8ac3a

issues.title: Meeting window doesn't appear during group calls

The meeting window doesn't appear during group calls, but works fine for one-on-one calls.
The issue occurs only when I join an ongoing call.

issues.title: Increase electron to 32.1.2 and electron-build to 25.0.5
Feature/move notification number bottom right
issues.body: This seems to be the best area after trying all other options.

issues.title: Configure User-Agent to circumvent block?
issues.body:
Today I noticed T4L is not working any more, the sign-in page in my company says "You cannot access this right now" and Microsoft's error code is 53003. It seems like there is a new policy preventing me from logging in.

After some research, I found out:

- Login via Browser (Vivaldi / Chrome / Firefox) does also not work.
- Browser in Windows (Vivaldi) works, strange...
- When I use Vivaldi on Linux and spoof my user agent as "Chrome 122 on Windows", it works (I am using a browser extension to do this).

Can we somehow spoof the User Agent in teams-for-linux or make this configurable in a future release?
IMHO the problem is my company not allowing some user agents...

issues.title: [Feature Request] Join via Meeting-ID
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Currently, when I want to join a Microsoft Teams meeting from within the Linux desktop application, I need to access the meeting by clicking on a meeting invite link that was sent to me via email or another method. This can be 
cumbersome if I don't have access to my email client at the time or if I simply forgot the link.

Additionally, as mentioned in issue #1370, there is already an existing workaround (installing a Firefox plugin to redirect the Teams link to msteams://) that only works for users with Mozilla Firefox installed and configured 
correctly. This solution still requires additional steps and might not be practical for everyone.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I would like to see two additional options in the teams-for-linux application:

1. An option to join a Microsoft Teams meeting using only the Meeting ID and User PIN (or other authentication methods), allowing me to quickly look up the meeting details, enter the Meeting ID and PIN, and instantly join the 
meeting without needing to access any other apps or websites.
2. A simple input field, maybe in the tray icon menu, where I can manually paste the meeting invite link and have it automatically join the meeting

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

As mentioned earlier, installing a Firefox plugin (issue #1370) is an existing workaround that doesn't cover all users' needs and requires additional configuration. This feature request aims to improve the user experience for both 
new and existing teams-for-linux users.

**Additional context**

This feature would be particularly useful for Linux users who frequently participate in Microsoft Teams meetings from their desktop environment or especially if you are in a hurry to join the meeting. Having a seamless experience within the teams-for-linux application would greatly enhance our productivity and reduce frustration caused by the current workflow.

A further step would be to suggest, maybe one time only?, to install the e.g. Firefox plugin afterwards or on first launch.

issues.title: Camera Flickering with Wayland on Electron 30.*
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
In meetings with cameras the camera picture flickers (it looks like it partially interlaces a couple of previews frames with the current one).

This only happens when setting `ELECTRON_OZONE_PLATFORM_HINT=auto` for teams-for-linux. It doesn't happen with xwayland it also doesn't happen in chromium 129 with wayland.

I have two independent systems that observe this, both are on Arch with KDE and have Intel graphics (`Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 7 155U`).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set `ELECTRON_OZONE_PLATFORM_HINT=auto`
2. Start teams-for-linux
3. Join a call with someone that has the camera active
4. Let them waive at the camera

I assume this might behave differently on differently on different GPUs and/or desktops

**Expected behavior**
Clean video feeds

**Screenshots**
I don't have screenshots, but it's very noticable when it happens, especially with fast horizontal movement.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch (non-testing)
 - Installation package: AUR teams-for-linux package (fyi, I'm one of the maintainers)
 - Version: 1.10.2

**Additional context**

Given that no issue exists in chromium 129 and that it seems to be wayland/ozone specific, I just tried building teams-for-linux with electron 32.1.2 and that actually fixes the issue.

issues.title: Safelink Meeting links are not 'trapped' within teams-for-linux
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When clicking a Teams 'join meeting' link in the Teams-for-linux calendar tab, I am directed out to Firefox and the meeting attempts to start in Firefox, instead of in the Teams-for-Linux client. 

**To Reproduce**
1. Go to teams-for-linux
2. Go to the calendar tab
3. Click on a meeting invitation (from an outside organisation). Find the 'join meeting' button.
4. Click the 'join meeting' link
5. Firefox opens the link and redirects to the meeting within Firefox.

**Expected behavior**
Expect the meeting to open within Teams for Linux.

**Screenshots**
Not applicable.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 20.04
 - Installation package teams-for-linux 1.10.2 deb
 - Version 1.10.2

**Debug**
Not done

**Additional context**
I note that my organisation uses aus01.safelinks.outlook.microsoft.com as a server for protecting users from phishing links etc. Perhaps teams-for-linux needs to do some processing on clicked links before handing off to firefox. Alternatively, perhaps a firefox add-in is needed to kick the request back to teams-for-linux.

issues.title: Disable GPU ownership of dGPU?
issues.body:
Is it possible to provide a way to avoid using dGPU for Teams?

By some reason, by opening MS Teams, my dGPU card is being in use. It has the same behavior if I use DRI_PRIME=0 at Flatpak Env.

Not sure if a bug or a feature, so adding this as a Feature Request

dGPU normally: D3cold state
![Screenshot_20240913_134029](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ec8f3c9-89e2-4b86-8479-24dddbd4d7ce)

dGPU when I open MS Teams: D0 state
![Screenshot_20240913_134118](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e5ab750-277f-43da-b832-2872e6ef65c3)

I'm assuming this is something from ElectronJS, but if wants to use GPU acceleration, then it should use only the default GPU instead. Right now it's using both. iGPU and dGPU.

Current version: 1.10.2
Node: 20.15.1
v8: 12.4.254.20-electron.0
electron: 30.4.0
chrome: 124.0.6367.243

issues.title: Teams for Linux fails opening the security window on GNOME3+Wayland : WebAuthn FIDO/FIDO2 Support

issues.title: [1.10.2] Application does not terminate after closing
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When the application is started on a command line via `teams-for-linux`, it starts polling in the form of  
`16:36:11.324 › GetSystemIdleState => IdleTimeout: 300s, IdleTimeoutPollInterval: 10s, ActiveCheckPollInterval: 2s, IdleTime: 0s, IdleState: 'active'`.

When the application is closed, it never stops polling, nor does it return control to the command prompt.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. In Debian Bookworm, install application [as per the Readme](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux#debianubuntu-and-other-derivatives)
2. open a terminal, execute `$ teams-for-linux` => polling starts, the GUI opens, Teams can be used normally
3. (Did some tests: call, camera on/off, screen share to/from; same for meeting)
4. Close application
5. Look at terminal, wait for several minutes => polling never stops, command prompt never reappears; Ctrl+C is necessary to properly terminate the application

**Expected behavior**
The application terminates shortly after being closed. As normal for applications.

**Screenshots**
The second line is emitted when closing the application. It repeats at the end after terminating the application forcefully with Ctrl+C, but none of the other lines associated with termination are present before that moment.

issues.title: fix the appdata file not to have an &
Please, provide a howto on running multiple instances for multiple organizations
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Microsoft teams web edition is not helpful at all when you are in multiple organizations. It does not let you manage the log in via the different accounts gracefully. You can only be logged in on one account at a time. This makes the switch unpractical and makes you loose the notifications from the account you are not logged in to.

This teams for linux application lets you work around the issue by running multiple instances of the application at the same time, each logged in to a different account, which is great.

Unfortunately, the documentation on how to do it is dispersed among the comments in multiple bug reports.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Would be great to have a howto file (possibly linked in the main readme) with instructions to follow in order to make the multiple instances work, also answering some questions that the users might have. For instance:

1. It the "config folder location" mentioned in the `KNOWN_ISSUES.md` file the same thing as the user directory in the `--customuserdir` parameter?
2. What is the difference among using 2 custom user dirs and different electron partitions as suggested in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/438#issuecomment-1717507135. Does the latter work at all?
3. Are there different instructions to be followed depending on how you have installed teamsforlinux (deb, arch/aur, flatpak, etc.)?
4. Do you actually need different desktop files?
5. What are the `WM_CLASS` and the `--class` parameter? Do they work the same in different desktops (gnome/KDE, etc)?
6. Most important: often you receive teams invitations via email (invitation to teams or to meetings). How do you assure that when you click on the link you get to the correct teams instance? I guess you need the link to be associated to the two "desktop files" so that a dialog is shown to pick the correct one, but how?

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
N/A

**Additional context**
Obviously, there is a risk that once a howto is put together Microsoft will finally fix the management of multiple organizations in the web client making the howto not relevant. But because there is no way to know what Microsoft will do, I think that preparing the howto would be worthy.

issues.title: Implement emulateWinChromiumPlatform Flag for OS Emulation
issues.body:
**Challenge**

Some organizations use a Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) application that supports only Windows and Mac systems. Users with unsupported operating systems, like Linux, are denied access due to system checks.

**Problem Analysis**

Web-based MFA applications often use properties such as `navigator.userAgent`, `navigator.userAgentData.platform`, `navigator.platform`, and `navigator.userAgentData.getHighEntropyValues` to detect the user's operating system. Although there is an option to modify the user agent, other properties also need emulation to fully mimic a Windows environment.

**Technical Details**

The implementation introduce a new PlatformEmulator class. It will be executed as part of preload script for chromium. This class will override specified property to emulate windows platform. 

**Solution**

Add the `emulateWinChromiumPlatform` flag to the CLI and configuration file, allowing users to opt-in to Windows platform emulation in Chromium browsers. This flag is turned off by default to avoid interference with existing workflows.


**Documentation**

Updated the README and the configuration documentation to include instructions on how to use the new `emulateWinChromiumPlatform` flag.

**Potential Impacts**

This change should not impact existing users as the default behavior remains unchanged. The feature is opt-in to ensure that only users needing this emulation enable it.

issues.title: Added support for OpenSUSE to rpm
issues.body:
OpenSUSE uses slightly different package names than CentOS/Fedora. 

This changes the requires section in the rpm to allow both the CentOS/Fedora (libXtst,libuuid) and the OpenSUSE (libXtst6,libuuid1) dependencies.

Upstream PR: https://github.com/develar/app-builder/pull/130

Fixes #521, #1183.

issues.title: Teams & browsers keyboard shortcut conflict (important message)
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
`Ctrl + Shift + I` is the shortcut for important messages (still working, despite that feature having disappeared from message formatting: go figure… M$ as usual?).

However, it conflicts with browsers' shortcut for console (queries inspection).
One of the other behaviour might be triggered in the same context.

For my own experience, that shortcut in a new message marks it as important.
However, replying in a conversation initially triggers console, and after a few tries, triggers the "important message" feature.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I am undecided on what course of action would be best.
On one hand, following Teams shortcut might be best as part of that application, but on the other hand, since in the end we are in a modified browser, following their "standard" might be nice too.
Both features have value.

**Additional notes**
Would there be other cases of conflict?

issues.title: Feature Request: Add Logfile option
issues.body:
It would be helpful to have the option of writing the application output to a log file.
There are already a option to adjust log level `appLogLevels`but you can currently not write it to a file.
For example, it happens that meetings have a notification about the start in the electron UI, but no notification arrives at the notification daemon of the os.

If there is an option to write program output to a file, you could react to events while parsing the log file with an other process.

Unfortunately `teams-for-linux 2>&1 >> logfile` is not really an option, as there can be problems here depending on the start method of teams-for-linux. For example, if you start it with a key combo in window managers like i3 there will be no log because of how its started by the wm.

**Describe the bug**
Deep links (`msteams://`) for meeting invites does not work.

**To Reproduce**

issues.title: handle teams v2 meeting invites differently than v1 invites. making the default logger electron-log, as the config options seeem to be broken
issues.body:
* handle teams v2 meeting invites differently than v1 invites. This might fix #1373 and #1370
* making the default logger electron-log, as the config options seem to be broken

issues.title: Autostart mimimized in system tray
issues.body:
Hello, how to run application in background in system tray at startup system ?

Like it works in telegram-desktop application, in menu present check button which automatically set autostart and another check button set start minimized in tray.

issues.title: reverting changes from default url to use teams v1 and updating libraries (electron to 30.4.0)
issues.body:
* Revert the default url to be teams v1, not v2. (fixes #1373)
* Update the logger to be `console` by default, as the documentation indicates
* Update electron from  30.2.0 to 30.4.0
* Update electron from  eslint and global libraries

issues.title: "Appearance and accessibility" settings blank, can't disable automatic translation
issues.body:
Hi there, thanks very much for this packaging of Teams for Linux, the switch to Teams v2 has been working very well for me.

I just have a small but annoying issue: I accidentally activated the automatic message translation feature and now I can't disable it. The context menu on the translated text has a "Settings..." option that I assume would let me disable it, but it takes me to an "Appearance and accessibility" section of the settings that doesn't load:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23a67d31-2e53-40ae-aaee-611391103e89)

the team's app is reloading and I am going back to the main page with all the chats

issues.title: Quits with `render-process-gone` when joining a call
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Quits when joining a call with just `render-process-gone` and no other errors in the log. 

Interestingly, turning on `disableGpu` does not resolve this problem but makes the quits occur less often. Without `disableGpu` the app quits almost every time I try to join a call.

Most likely related to https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/32760 where people suggest that a good workaround is adding `app.disableHardwareAcceleration()`. See https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/32760#issuecomment-2111717155.

This is currently not in effect when `disableGpu` is enabled. Can we introduce it and see if this helps?

https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/fedf73ac4bdd1f4279c54f4f2f6e0c345e47faba/app/index.js#L124

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Go to calendar
2. Click _Meet now_ on the top or _Join_ on any of the events
3. You don't get a chance to _Join now_ as the app quits when the screen with meeting controls loads

**Expected behavior**

Can join the call

**Screenshots**

n/a

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

 - OS: Arch
 - Installation package: https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/teams-for-linux

issues.title: When simultaneously connected on android and Linux, many discussion elements are not displayed
issues.body:
When simultaneously connected on android and Linux, many discussion elements are not displayed

**To Reproduce**

Started a discussion with a contact today on PC. Most of its answer were not displayed in Linux version but correctly on android. If writing things myself on android, it did not appear.

If I clode and restart, then I get the full exchange. 

**Expected behavior**
See all exchanged things on both displays.


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux
 - Installation package deb
 - Version 1.9.0
 - 
dpkg -s teams-for-linux 
Package: teams-for-linux
Status: install ok installed

issues.title: JavaScript error in writeOut
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Teams deb 1.8.1 under Debian 12, running since 12 days, window in background. When idle, a window with the following contents pops up:

Error

A JavaScript error occurred in the main process

Uncaught Exception:
Error: write EIO
  at afterWriteDispatched (node:internal/stream_base_commons:161:15)
  at writeGeneric (node:internal/stream_base_commons:152:3)
  at Socket._writeGeneric (node:net:953:11)
  at Socket._write (node:net:965:8)
  at writeOrBuffer (node:internal/streams/writable:564:12)
  at _write (node:internal/streams/writable:493:10)
  at Writable.write (node:internal/streams/writable:502:10)
  at console.value (node:internal/console/constructor:304:16)
  at console.warn (node:internal/console/constructor:384:26)
  at writeOut (node:internal/process/warning:65:3)

Ok

After clicking on "Ok", the window appears again. After clicking "Ok" again, teams continues to work normally.

**To Reproduce**

I couldn't identify specific steps to trigger the issue. Happens about once a week. The process started from GNU screen. The starting pty may or may not be open, not sure. This time this happened some minutes after resume in a different net.

**Expected behavior**
No unhandled exceptions.

**Screenshots**
![20240817-javascript_exception](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65a4025e-c1c6-44af-901c-48d712f795ff)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Debian
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 1.8.1

**Debug**

As I don't know how to reproduce this, I've restarted teams-for-linux with --webDebug --logConfig='{}' and will comment if the problem occurs.

**Additional context**
None
