issues.title: pip for screen sharing
issues.body: It would be nice to have the possibility to pip the shared screen in a call.

issues.title: other sign in methods if passkey is enabled
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I cant login anymore when i enable my passkeys in Microsoft.  It defaults to my pin/fingerprint login but i cant select a other sign in method.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A extra option to sign in via a other methode when passkey is trying?

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Removing my passkey but it is also useful for other applications/web based login


EDIT: 
Apparently it selects my last login method as default. If i login somewhere else through other means, the teams-linux client will remember that somehow.

issues.title: Please sign in again? - Way to keep non-idle?
issues.body: Assuming this is due to org policy around timeout. Can we do something that would keep the connection active enough to not trigger this? Native Teams or Outlook or other apps seem to be able to hold on to this. Maybe through certs or something? Pretty annoying. I understand there is only so much one can do here but asking.

labels.name: known issue/workaround
labels.description: Workaround available in the know_issues.md file

issues.title: Update README.md to add required JSON comma
issues.body:
When running the example config provided here for Electron CLI flags, I got a JSON parse error for a missing comma:

`A JavaScript error occurred in the main process
Uncaught Exception:
SyntaxError: Expected ',' or ']' after array element in JSON at position 61 (line 4 column 3)
    at JSON.parse (<anonymous>)`

This is a simple PR to add a single comma.

issues.title: Show shared content by a peer in full screen mode
issues.body: It would be great to have the chance to show the content shared during a call (e.g. shared screen during a presentation) in full screen, without buttons or overlays covering what's received.

issues.title: Execute a command on call join
issues.body:
Hi there! Thanks for the client :)

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I want to get the details of the current meeting every time I join a call for my journaling.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
An option like incomingCallCommand (joinCallCommand?) would work if I could put the meeting's JSON to the args (or at least the meeting ID/join link)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
MS Graph APIs won't let me see the call I've joined.

**Additional context**
Ideally, that would be a JSON like the one provided by MS APIs, but even a meetingID or a join link would work. With ID or a join link I could query the Graph API to get the data.

MS call JSON object:
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/call-get?view=graph-rest-1.0&tabs=http

issues.title: Widow size not persisted after system restart

After system restart window size of teams is not persisted and is reset to very small one

2. Reset the system
3. Open Teams again
4. See  the reset windows size, back to small one

**Expected behavior**
Set window size should be persisted 

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux Mint 21.3 Cinnamon
 - Installation package deb
 - Version 1.9.0

issues.title: Add cli option to set WM_CLASS property
issues.body: This makes it possible to run multiple instances of teams-for-linux with different task bar icons (by setting StartupWMClass in the .desktop file and the --class option to the same value).

issues.title: Feature/move from lucidlog to electron log
issues.body:
Quite a few changes, mostly around the logger and refactoring the custom background. This repo now includes a customBackground example.

* Moving from lucid-log to electron-log
* Adding --logConfig option to allow choosing between console (default), electron-log or Falsy (no logs in console)
* Adding --help option to show the config options
* Deprecating --appLogLevels in favor of --logConfig
* Adding deprecated warning popup if deprecated options found to be in use
* Refactoring the customBackground service and including an example
* Updating electron to 30.2.0, and other small dependency updates

issues.title: problems with backticks
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When typing a backtick (`) for me it does not trigger an inline code, not does typing triple-backticks trigger a code block.
It's especially relevant, that my keyboard requires me to type shift+^ for the backtick, and then followed by the space to "finalize" the backtick.
It works in the web app. Just not in this electron app. So it could be related.

issues.title: Single window sharing is blurry / low res.
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

When I share a single windows (versus sharing the complete screen) the shared window image received at the other is very poor: low resolution and blurry. I need to share the complete screen for the image to be received properly.


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start a call (video or only voice)
2. Click on share button
3. Select window
4. Choose the window
5. Wait until the other end receives the image
6. Share image at the other end is low res/blurry

**Expected behavior**
The window is shared and received properly at the other end.

**Screenshots**
As the problem is exposed at the other end, I don't have a screen shot, sorry.

issues.title: Video vertical stripes on new Version
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
If I start / join a meeting and people sharing their webcam, the video has vertical stripes on the current version. Downgraded to the version before and everything works fine.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Teams (v1.8.0)
2. Click on existing (even old) Meeting
3. Share your webcam
4. Move a bit around and see the stripes 
5. Downgraded to the Version 1.7.5 on Flatpak "resolved" this issue as a workaround for me

**Expected behavior**
Clear image, no stripes

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Fido Key on Ping ID, password prompt is not showing and the screen is stuck on the change auth device screen
**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have an account with fido key activated with a password
2. Try to sign in
3. See the auth screen without password prompt


**Expected behavior**
When authenticating with Fido key a password prompt is shown to enter it and then the authenticator asks to touch the key 

**Screenshots**
This is the fido key tutorial and example of behaviour https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sI7yWHim-2Y

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: ubuntu 22.04
 - Version 1.7.5

issues.title: increase electron to 30.1.2 and adding feature flags for contextIsolation and sandboxing
issues.body:
* Increase electron version to 30.1.2
* Fix handleOnCallConnected not calling disable screen lock
* Adding disableContextIsolation and disableSandbox config options. These will break most functionality but are added as a WIP to help assessing what it would break. They both default to false. (Partly addresses https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/986)

issues.title: Screen Sharing is Not Working - NOT Wayland
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Clicking share screen brings up the option to share screen and click on it:

![Teams_Share_Screen_1](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/4388558/c3a7bb7d-5a61-417f-acfb-f15a899e2ad4)

Shows the option to select the Entire Screen and clicking on it never results in sharing the screen.  You can't close this bottom panel either and I have exit the application to get it to go away:

![Teams_Share_Screen_2](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/4388558/615644d8-aa7c-49ab-b1c2-b94a32c201a1)

I have been using teams-for-linux since late last year.  Screen sharing has never worked for me.  If I need to share my screen I've been having to use Microsoft's abandoned v1.5 Teams Preview.

 - OS: Devuan Daedalus (Debian Bookworm)

issues.title: CustomCSSLocation not working in FlatPak version
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I'm trying to load a customCSS into teams-for-linux following the mentions I found in two previous issues. I can't get it to work no matter where I put the CSS. This is most likely a 'me' issue, but I thought I'd ask.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
run teams-for-linux with: 
`com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux --customCSSLocation=/home/user/.var/app/com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux/config/teams-for-linux/teams-catppuccin.user.css`

Contents of CSS:
```
.ts-message-thread-body .padded-content {
    background-color: green !important;
}
```

I have also tried creating a config.json file under:
/home/user/.var/app/com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux/config/teams-for-linux/
and
/home/user/.var/app/com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux/config/
and
/home/user/.config/teams-for-linux/
with the contents:
```
{
"customCSSLocation": "/home/user/.var/app/com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux/config/teams-for-linux/teams-catppuccin.user.css"
}
```
with no success. I have also tried execution both with and without the --customCSSLocation parameter while the config.json is in all three locations. 

**Expected behavior**
Based on the CSS above, the chat body should turn green.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: EndeavourOS
 - Installation package Flatpak
 - Version 1415/24061318406

issues.title: Status shown as "Away" when locking the screen with awayOnSystemIdle = false
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When locking the screen, status is set to "Away" when `awayOnSystemIdle = false`, see https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1081.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open teams-for-linux, make sure the status is set to "Available".
2. Lock the screen and wait for the set `IdleTimeout` to elapse.
3. Unlock the screen.
4. Status is set to "Away" and transitions to "Available" shortly after.

**Expected behavior**
Status remains "Available" if `awayOnSystemIdle = false`.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Fedora 40 x86_64
- Installation package: RPM
- Version: 1.7.5


**Debug**

```
[DEBUG] GetSystemIdleState => IdleTimeout: 3600s, IdleTimeoutPollInterval: 30s, ActiveCheckPollInterval: 2s, IdleTime: 0s, IdleState: 'active'
[DEBUG] GetSystemIdleState => IdleTimeout: 3600s, IdleTimeoutPollInterval: 30s, ActiveCheckPollInterval: 2s, IdleTime: 0s, IdleState: 'locked'
```

**Additional context**
Copied discussion from https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1320#issuecomment-2202185148.

I have traced this logic to the following code path:

* When the screen is locked, this will evaluate to `activityHub.setMachineState(2)`:

https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/9d2c1ddf4f447898db5a290b0fe608e98936ea51/app/browser/notifications/activityManager.js#L22

* Which in turn will transition to idle:

https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/9d2c1ddf4f447898db5a290b0fe608e98936ea51/app/browser/tools/activityHub.js#L45

I am not sure of the logic here so apologies if I am mistaken, but the outcome is that `awayOnSystemIdle` does not work when locking the screen.

Thanks for taking a look at this :wink:

While on a meeting when clicking the share button it open the menu for sharing
but when clicking on Screen / Window nothing happen

![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/99732661/4c20e935-24d1-455c-b8f5-fa934806c9ab)

is this known issue? is there any walk around?

issues.title: I would add the ability to provide warning messages to users on app start
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I would add the ability to provide warning messages to users on app start

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A dialog that the user needs to act upon

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Logs, but they aren't great for ensuring people make the changes

**Additional context**
We don't have a clear deprecation functionality/warning.

issues.title: Feature/feat 1307 reload config on file change
issues.body:
* Add watchConfigFile option to restart the app if the configuration changes.

  - Notice that the some UI settings would trigger an app restart if this functionality is on.
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/430992/1adc2256-683d-41a0-9dcf-db41684586f9)

* Modify configError message to have an icon and adding the ability to provide warning dialogs (for config deprecation)

  - warning dialog example
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/430992/0d2e9e32-8efa-44c0-b8f6-2a40edd62a52)

  - error dialog example
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/430992/d2c88ace-6668-4c0d-a220-dfd594125739)

* Refactoring around inTune and the config file handling
* Refactoring events to all be kebab-case instead of a mix of kebab-case and camelCase

Closes #1307 and #1326

issues.title: Possible guide to enable HW DEcoding and ENcoding in electron
issues.body:
From using teams4linux daily i notice my laptop goes into turbo mode whenever i enter a call and everyone turns on their cameras (and I have to turn on mine)
The teams v2 has a call health feature and displays that the videos are decrypted using SW.

Looking to solve my issue, i tried to force enable HW encoding on chromium. Managed to do it but my video comes out with artifacts... But the performance improved.
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/103443867/01cb7892-6f93-4b9c-9ee4-75659b5f24cb)

So I found this repo: https://github.com/StaZhu/enable-chromium-hevc-hardware-decoding which may help to enable HW encoding/decoding in electron. I know it talks about hevc and teams uses h264, but there might something that helps enabling both.

For reference, my work laptop is running Pop!_OS 22.04 on a i7-10610U

issues.title: appIdleTimeout not respected?
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Hi,

I am using Teams-for-Linux on NixOS and with Hyprland, I have set the following config in `.config/teams-for-linux/config.json`:

```JSON
{
  "appActiveCheckInterval": 6000,
  "appIdleTimeout": 6000,
  "appIdleTimeoutCheckInterval": 6000,
  "awayOnSystemIdle": false,
  "optInTeamsV2": true
}
```

With the following settings, I would expect the status not to go idle before 6000s (1.6h) but the app goes to idle after 300s.

If I launch the app via the terminal with the following command:

`teams-for-linux --appLogLevels debug --enableDesktopNotificationsHack --enable-features=UseOzonePlatform --ozone-platform=wayland`

The terminal output:
```bash
[DEBUG] GetSystemIdleState => IdleTimeout: 6000s, IdleTimeoutPollInterval: 6000s, ActiveCheckPollInterval: 6000s, IdleTime: 0s, IdleState: 'active'
```
However, teams go to idle after 300s (the default value). I check with a stopwatch, and regardless of my activity on my pc, as long as I leave the mouse cursor out of the teams app, my status will turn from available to idle after 300ms.

I tested by setting appIdleTimeout to 100s either in the config.json or via passing as an argument when launching teams via the terminal. Teams only go idle after 300s thus not respecting the 100s.

**Expected behavior**
Teams should go idle after the appIdleTimeout value, so in my case 6000s.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Nixos
 - Installation package Nix packages
 - Version 1.4.27

issues.title: Possible SE-Linux issue related to execheap (?)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Last 2 release of t4l.  1.7.3 and 1.7.4  -- but certainly 1.7.4  generates a SE-Linux alert on startup, and then t4l shuts down.     If persistent, I can muscle through a few times and t4l will just start working, while ignoreing the SE-Linux alerts. 

No I have not applied remedies suggested by SE-Linux alert browser;  I wanted to keep the condition and submit an issue or question to see if this is a problem or not. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start t4l
2. observe SE-Alerts 

**Expected behavior**
NO SEAlerts and t4l starts the first time. 


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS:  fedora 40  (6.9.5-200.fc40.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC) 
 - DNF/RPM based installed. 
 - current version:   teams-for-linux-1.7.4-1.x86_64   (but the previous version, seemed to do it also; version before that, didn't seem to this.) 



**Additional context**
Here is details from SE-Linux/SETroubleshooting tool:  

SELinux is preventing teams-for-linux from using the execheap access on a process.

*****  Plugin allow_execheap (53.1 confidence) suggests   ********************

If you do not think teams-for-linux should need to map heap memory that is both writable and executable.
Then you need to report a bug. This is a potentially dangerous access.
Do
contact your security administrator and report this issue.

*****  Plugin catchall_boolean (42.6 confidence) suggests   ******************

If you want to allow selinuxuser to execheap
Then you must tell SELinux about this by enabling the 'selinuxuser_execheap' boolean.

Do
setsebool -P selinuxuser_execheap 1

If you believe that teams-for-linux should be allowed execheap access on processes labeled unconfined_t by default.
Then you should report this as a bug.
You can generate a local policy module to allow this access.
Do
allow this access for now by executing:
# ausearch -c 'teams-for-linux' --raw | audit2allow -M my-teamsforlinux

issues.title: Screen sharing option not showing when content sharing policy applied
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
This issue can be seen when an organizations use content sharing policy to prevent users from sharing the entire screen and only allow sharing one window at a time.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Content sharing policy should be set to "Single application" mode in the policy.
2. Try to share screen in a meeting.

**Expected behavior**
If there are no issues it should allow users to select what application to share

issues.title: Video background is not persisted between calls
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When I select a background for video calls it is not persisted: I have to set it again on every new meeting.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior (translated from German - actual wording may be different in english):
1. Open a video call
2. Click on "Video effects & settings"
3. Select a background
4. Click on "Apply"
5. Close the call
6. Start a new call

**Expected behavior**
The background selected in the first call should be reused.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux Mint 21.3 Virginia
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 1.6.1

**Debug**
Running with `--webDebug` gives me a core dump:

```
teams-for-linux --webDebug --appLogLevels=error,info,warn,debug

issues.title: Reload config data on file change
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When the yargs config file is changed, a restart of the app is required to pick up the new values

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like for the configuration to "watch" the file and apply any changes in it.

Unfortunately this is not natively supported in yargs (https://github.com/yargs/yargs/issues/585#issuecomment-552150474), but we should be able to use https://github.com/paulmillr/chokidar to watch the file and re-load the config as stated in the yargs comment.

Once this is implemented, leave a note into the yargs with the example, so others to benefit in the future.  

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Adding some command line arguments.

**Additional context**
This could provide the ability of people changing arguments by modifying the config file. The config file is just a json file (like for example using `jq '.foo.bar = "new value"' file.json` (see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/31034746/how-do-i-update-a-single-value-in-a-json-document-using-jq)

This comes from issue https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/948 and https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1081

issues.title: Set status message from command line
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No, it is not related to a problem.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to be able to set my teams status from the command line.
This saves me from opening the application.
It also allows me to pipe status information from other apps to teams.
It would be great if this feature could be used even while teams is already running.
If the feature is used when teams is already running, no other visual teams instance will appear.
The teams instance used for setting the status will set the status and terminate.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
There is no alternative found.

**Additional context**
The commands could look like this:

`teams --set-status "having lunch" --clear-after 1h`
`teams --set-status "playing: the weeknd - blinding lights" --emoji 1f929`

**Pseudo Pipe examples:**
`mprisctl current | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "playing: {}"`
`build_status | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "build: {}"`
`task current | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "working on: {}"`
`calendarctl next | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "next meeting: {}"`
`batteryctl status | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "battery: {}"`
`weatherctl current | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "weather: {}"`
`uptime | awk '{print $10}' | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "load average: {}"`
`pomodoroctl status | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "pomodoro: {}"`
`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "working on branch: {}"`
`docker ps --filter "status=running" --format "{{.Names}}" | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "containers running: {}"`
`vpnctl status | xargs -I{} teams --set-status "VPN: {}"`


You get the picture!

issues.title: Icon not showing for snap latest/edge 1.7.1
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
On Ubuntu 24.04, when the latest/edge 1.7.1 snap is installed the incorrect icon is showed. It's the icon of electron.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have the Team for Linux app pinned in the Dock
2. Go to snap store
3. Install snap latest/edge 1.7.1
4. See incorrect icon in the dock

**Expected behavior**
It should show the icon of Teams For Linux

**Screenshots**
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/160134680/dfa78a6d-036a-408b-addf-b20416703d78)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 24.04
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version: latest/edge 1.7.1

**Debug**
When possible, please run the application from the terminal using `--webDebug` and try to reproduce the error.

The provide in this section the output from both the terminal and the browser debug console.

issues.title: Teams v2 meeting links are always sent to an external brower rather than captured
issues.body:
Teams for Linux used to (somehow) process links to join meetings so that the meeting will run inside the app.
Since the the change to Teams v2 (or at least it feels like), links to join meetings always open in an external browser, and I have not been able to find a way to join a meeting in the app.

**To Reproduce**
1. Enter a link in a chat of the form: [https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting), the old Teams style.
2. When you click on the link, nothing happens (maybe because the link is not correct?)
3. The debug output says: `[DEBUG] DEBUG - captured meetup-join url`
4. Enter instead a new-style link: [https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/?meetingjoin=true#/l/meetup-join/19:ameeting](https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/?meetingjoin=true#/l/meetup-join/19:ameeting)
5. When you click on the link, it always opens it in an external browser.
6. The debug output now says `[DEBUG] Requesting to open 'https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/?meetingjoin=true#/l/meetup-join/19`

**Expected behavior**
When clicking on a new-style meeting join link, I would expect the "meetingjoin" URL to be captured so it is opened inside the app, not in an external browser.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Debian GNU/Linux v12
 - Installed from the repository at repo.teamsforlinux.de
 - Version 1.5.1

issues.body: fixes #1279

issues.title: Hotfix/snap publish failed

Spellchecking not working, unable to find out why
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Note: English is not my first language.

I have to use Teams in a somewhat restricted network, that means only some domains are accessible via a proxy. And now I have the problem that no spellchecking seams to work. 

**To Reproduce**

Setup a proxy that only allows:
.microsoft.com, .microsoft.de, login.live.com, login.microsoftonline.com, 
.gstatic.com, .googleapis.com, .googlecode.com, .googleusercontent.com, apis.google.com,
code.jquery.com, consent.google.at, consent.google.com
.github.com, .github.io, .githubusercontent.com,
.mozaws.net, .mozilla.com, .mozilla.net, .mozilla.org,
.npmjs.org

Note: that is not everything that is allowed, but that should be everything that teams for linux needs, if there is something missing, than I found the bug.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start teams-for-linux with following arguments:
   --webDebug true --appLogLevels "error,warn,info,debug" --proxyServer "<proxy server>" --spellCheckerLanguages "en-US,de,de-DE"
3. Click on any chat
4. input something that is spelled worng.
5. no "redline" under the word, and a context menu does not show suggestions.
6. webDebuger and log output does not give any hints on what is wrong

**Expected behavior**
Nice would be a working spellchecking, but what I need more is a way to find out why it is not working, there is no debug log that says that something does not work. Also the webDebuger has no 403 or something like that.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: debian 12.5
 - Installation package deb
 - Version 1.5.3
 - Installed hunspell packages: 
   - dictionaries-common/stable,now 1.29.5 all [installed,automatic]
   - hunspell-de-at/stable,now 20161207-11 all [installed]
   - hunspell-de-de/stable,now 20161207-11 all [installed]
   - hunspell-en-us/stable,now 1:2020.12.07-2 all [installed,automatic]
   - libhunspell-1.7-0/stable,now 1.7.1-1 amd64 [installed,automatic]


**Debug**
```

issues.title: Add support for InTune Single-Sign-On
issues.body:
This add support for communicating with the Microsoft Authentication Broker over its DBus interface in order to retrieve an authentication cookie, that can be used to automatically login the user currently logged-in via InTune. This also adds support for MFA and Conditional Access, which allows use of Teams outside of corporate network in case the organization has chosen to only allow access from registered devices.

Behind the scene this uses the same mechanism as Microsoft Edge on Linux: upon loading a website from login.microsoftonline.com the URL is passed to the authentication broker in order to prepare a token based on the PRT (Primary Refresh Token). The returned refresh token is passed to the server via the 'X-Ms-Refreshtokencredential' HTTP header. With this token in place the server will skip any interactive prompts and generate a proper OAuth authentication token. Since the PRT is tied to the device credentials, the resulting refresh token carries the MFA attribute, which causes it to be accepted even if the Conditional Access policy mandates strong, device-based authentication.

issues.title: 1.5.0: generic window icon instead of Teams application icon shown in taskbar and Alt-Tab task switcher window
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
As of 1.5.0, the usual Teams application icon is no longer shown in the taskbar and in the `Alt-Tab` application switcher. Instead, the generic Adwaita window icon is shown. Going back to 1.4.37, the usual Teams icon is shown again. This happens with both the linux-x64_64 tarball install and the AppImage. The _systray_ icon is _not_ affected, it shows the normal Teams icon as usual.

I'm running GNOME 46.2 with a GNOME-flashback session.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. upgrade from 1.4.x to 1.5.0
2. look at the taskbar icon, or the icon shown in the `Alt-Tab` app switcher window
3. scratch your head wondering where the Teams icon has gone :wink: 

**Expected behavior**
I would expect the normal Teams icon to be shown as usual.

**Screenshots**
Taskbar with 1.5.0:
![Screenshot from 2024-05-30 13-03-52](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/12568948/3002370f-e126-4516-b11b-dcc8394d68de)
Taskbar with 1.4.x:
![Screenshot from 2024-05-30 13-05-43](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/12568948/f8640d18-af1e-449d-9ccb-1480241012fc)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Slackware64-15.0
 - Installation package: tar.gz, AppImage
 - Version: 1.5.0

If you need any further information please let me know.

Thanks for listening,
Thomas

issues.title: Running multiple instances of TFL - to handle different usernames?
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Many of us have multiple Microsoft 365 logins and for a single login with multiple connections to organizations in Microsoft 365, TFL has been awesome. But I want to avoid multiple ins/outs on logins to hit these. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Does it make sense to isolate a couple of instances of TFL to have each connected to different M365 usernames/organizations?

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Currently running TFL for my main username and I have multiple organizations under that. It's been awesome for the last year or so for that. I have dedicated a separate browser for the other instances with different usernames connected to different organizations. Just thinking I would try to run multiple TFL instances.

labels.name: duplicate
labels.description: This issue or pull request already exists

issues.title: Support for SSO basic-auth without dialogue.
issues.body:
Added two new config keys : `ssoUser`, `ssoPasswordCommand` that will be used instead of the regular login/password dialogue.

Authentication will be setup with the `login` with content of `ssoUser` key, and the password will be the stdout of the execution of the command in `ssoPasswordCommand`.

Example of config :

```json
{
  "ssoUser": "Thomas",
  "ssoPasswordCommand": "gopass -o work/mycompany.com"
}
```

issues.title: No persisted login
issues.body: I'm not sure, if this is a bug or works-as-designed, but I have to log in to my teams company account every time when starting teams-for-linux. When I log in to my account in a browser (e.g. Chrome), the login is persisted even if I closed Chrome or rebooted my PC. I'm using teams-for-linux 1.4.37 for AMD64 Debian 12.5 (Bookworm).

issues.title: Clicking on a notification does nothing.
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Clicking on an incoming message notification does nothing.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1) Get a notification
2) Click on it
3) Stare at your monitor as it does nothing

**Expected behavior**
Clicking on a notification should take you directly to the message.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04 - GNOME 42.9
 - Package: Flatpak version
 - Version: Latest Flatpak app

Additional notes:
This has been an issue with this app well before the Teams v2 update, but at least back then some notifications did put the app in focus (but still not the message). Now it doesn't even do anything anymore, so I had to switch to using it on Firefox or Chrome.

I read and follow the instructions to setup custom background imagens. 
I Already read the issue #1159 and the problem is not the same, but i get a few tips from debug.
When the screen is launched, the browser gets "http 200" for 2 callls on "config.json" from my custom adress as espected, but the third call  get a config.json from https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/evergreen-assets/backgroundimages/config.json?v=6

After thad, when the Teams try get the  thumbs to fill the  images on selection screen, it uses the name of the images from my config.json, but using "https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/" as prefix.

issues.title: Tabs maybe possible?
issues.body:
I am referring to #973 
in the browser (firefox) the right button menu on a teams tab shows "open in new window"
whereas in the teams-for-linux app it shows "go to website" and "open in desktop app".

So somehow microsoft's knows that it's not a real browser?
Can maybe one of the two be catched to open another browser tab or window? Or have a way to open a second window for the same tenant (ctrl-T / ctrl-N)? Example use is to have a window/tab with the chat and another with the planner.

issues.title: Teams login problem.
issues.body:
Hello everyone
I'm facing the issue at login side. I m using teams in UBUNTU.

Normally I have logged in but sometimes it creates a problem in login me. I have adjusted the date and time of windows and than login try is successful. But previous two or three days, I'm unable to login. I have checked the data and time which is correct. Several times I have restarted my software and Ubuntu too but totally failed in login. I have uninstalled team, installed again but failed.

What Does I do? I opened teams and Teams asked me to enter the username, then asked for password, then asked for a dialog box "Stay signed in". Whatever the action, I do, nothing fine. Although normally, I checked "Don't show again' checkbox and click the button yes. It shows the white page for few seconds with a title "checking the credentials" and then go back to the Sign in dialog box. Means enter username, then password, then dialog box. I'm in a loop of login side but nothing happens.

I'm unable to login my account on online teams using chrome and firefox. Same error on chrome and firefox. But I signed in successfully on my android phone.

Some one has told me to open an issue ticket. I don't know how. 

I'm new to Ubuntu so I need a proper guidance.

Kindly help me.
Regards

issues.title: Outlook In Teams
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Some users may find it inconvenient to switch between Microsoft Teams and Outlook to access their emails, calendars, and contacts, resulting in decreased productivity and workflow interruptions.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to see a dedicated tab for Outlook integrated directly into Microsoft Teams. This tab would allow users to access their emails, calendars, and contacts seamlessly within the Teams interface, eliminating the need to switch between applications.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
An alternative solution could be to continue using Outlook and Teams separately, but this may lead to inefficiencies and workflow disruptions as users switch between applications to access different functionalities.

**Additional context**
Adding a dedicated tab for Outlook within Teams would streamline the user experience and enhance productivity by providing easy access to email, calendar, and contact features without leaving the Teams environment.

issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Short issue:  my custom background (and thumbnail) are both black.  NOTE:  I am using jpg, not png.. Is that a problem? 

I am running with `teams-for-linux --customBGServiceBaseUrl=https://my.host.name/my_base_url --isCustomBackgroundEnabled=true --webDebug=true` and the system starts up..  But it does not appear to be reaching out to my.host.name to pull the config.json.  It DID reach out ONCE, but didn't do it again even after I changed the config.json.  So it must be caching the data.

Also, I do see an error in the debug log that implies something is not being overridden:

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Run the application
2. Trying to join a meeting
3. Try to set the backgroun

**Expected behavior**
I would expect Teams to try to read my config.json on every startup, or at least attempt to see if it changed.  And I would expect the graphics to show.

**Screenshots**

issues.title: Fix awayOnSystemIdle flag in Teams V2
Video hardware acceleration on Linux (on Intel Comet Lake)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Running a meeting with a moderate amount of participants (4-6) causes full CPU usage on a Intel-based laptop, which gets busy with video encoding/decoding. This dramatically slows down any other operations and drains the battery. Additionally encoding a shared screen causes further slowdowns: typing in an IDE comes to a crawl of one character every two to three seconds.
Screensharing on Intel hardware is a painful experience.

**To Reproduce**
1. Start a meeting with 3+ participants
2. Share the screen or window of an IDE (e.g. VSCode with Pylance running)
3. Type some code into the IDE, do some simple copy&paste
4. Observe UI reaction speed

**Expected behavior**
The screenshared meeting should not put the system into a crawl.

**Screenshots**
The hardware & OS supports hardware encoding/decoding, as shown in chromium-browser:
![Screenshot from 2024-05-03 16-33-04](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/1126467/fc6b7f49-93bc-4ce8-877b-24913677d218)

**Laptop**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS, Xorg session
 - Installation package: from this project's apt repo
 - Version: 1.4.35
 - Hardware: Thinkpad X1C 8th, Core i7-10510U Comet Lake (with Quickysnc supporting VP8, VP9, H.264 and HEVC in mpv)

**Additional context**
`intel_gpu_top` shows the GPU being used for rendering by X, but nothing on Video.
`htop` shows high percentages across all cores being used for teams alongside VSCode. Only these two open cause a load of 3+ even after letting them "settle" for a minute.

`renice -n 19 -p $(pidof teams-for-linux)` doesn't noticeably improve the experience.

VSCode without Teams behaves fast enough (for a heavy Electron app...)

Chromium has been supporting [hardware acceleration](https://discourse.ubuntu.com/t/chromium-hardware-accelerated-build-for-intel-based-platforms-available-for-beta-testing/35625) for a year now. Either by default or forcing it with
`chromium-browser --ignore-gpu-blocklist --enable-features=VaapiVideoDecoder,VaapiVideoEncoder,VaapiVideoDecodeLinuxGL,VaapiIgnoreDriverChecks`

Launching `teams-for-linux` with the same arguments doesn't help. Assuming it's not based on a modern Chromium version, could this still be ported?

issues.title: About section is bugged
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When you hit the About section you will see weird letters, just rectangles.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Teams for Linux
2. Click on the tray button
3. Click on About section
4. See error

**Expected behavior**
A window showing information about the package and developer.

issues.title: Question: Is there a way to force classic teams v1?
issues.body:
I've had to reinstall my OS and thus also teams-for-linux.
Now after logging in to teams I get the following message:

![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/148882470/1695be99-078e-4d59-930b-a99c0e2ece65)

I cannot click cancel or abort, my only choice is "Switch now".
Once I do that, usually there would be a "Go back to classic teams" button somewhere at the top but now it's not available anymore.

**Is there any way I can stay on classic?**

issues.title: Meeting notifications not working with "new teams"
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Notifications sent to notification-daemon no longer happen with the "new teams".
This does work - if somewhat erratically - with the "classic teams". I have tried both "web" and "electron" notification methods with the same results.
UseMutationTitleLogic does work for systray icon changes for "permanent" notifications, it's just the transient ones which are missing.

**To Reproduce**
1) Start Teams
2) Have someone else start a meeting you are present at / send a chat message
3) Nothing happens
4) Use `notify-send` to test notification daemon
5) Receive notification as normal
(There is presumably more to it than this as other people have mentioned in other issues that it works for them, but I can't tell what, unfortunately)

**Expected behavior**
Notifications of new meetings, chat messages, etc. sent to notification daemon as with classic teams

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Debian stable, Windowmaker desktop with notification-daemon to handle notifications
 - deb package install
 - 1.4.27

**Debug**
The main obvious error seems to be

Failed to start Activity Hub by assigning Event Handlers
error @ v2/:3
(anonymous) @ /opt/teams-for-linux/resources/app.asar/app/browser/tools/activityHub.js:44

Full log: [teams.log](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/files/15150302/teams.log)



(Sorry, this doesn't feel like a lot of help - happy to answer more detailed questions about my system config and try other config settings, etc.)

Usually, it's enough to rename appimage file and it will create separate config, but not in this case.

issues.title: Custom backgrounds in Teams V2
issues.body:
Teams V2 changed the image and configuration search url to: https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/evergreen-assets/backgroundimages/config.json?v=6

This implementation only replaces the root url (https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/evergreen-assets/backgroundimages/) to fetch the local custom images.

This will replace Microsoft's default images.

To use standard Microsoft images, you must activate the flag --isCustomBackgroundEnabled=false

Fix #1167

issues.title: Crash on Apple Silicon Macs because of unsigned code
issues.body: Hello, the Mac build is now universal, but because it is unsigned, it crashes on Apple Silicon macs on startup: It [appears](https://code.videolan.org/videolan/vlc/-/issues/26432) that Apple Silicon Macs simply can't run unsigned code (not sure about that, but I was not able to find a workaround). A build from this repo signed with my developer certificate works, but that is trusted only on my machine. I'm not about to accept Apple's $99 extortion to _checks notes_ be able to run code on my HW, so I guess we're looking for somebody willing to do that. In the meantime, the universal build now crashes on start on Apple Silicon macs, instead of just being slow - I suppose we should revert  #1212?

issues.title: Show a startup error dialog in case of invalid syntax of the config.json
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** I just noticed an issue that should be quick to solve and improve the UX part by telling to the user what's the problem. If by mistake you leave a comma at the end of the last key in the JSON config file, like this: ```json { "disableNotificationSound": true, } ``` and you start the app, nothing visually appears: no systray icon, nor Gnome Dock symbol that tells if the app is started (as it's the case for others, see the white dots below the icons for the apps that are started/running). ![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/14140226/fd5a6e1f-d8e2-40c9-b183-224d3def1b97) But the app's processes are running: ```shell ~ ❯ ps -ef | grep teams dxps 567424 6428 6 09:15 ? 00:00:00 /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux dxps 567428 567424 0 09:15 ? 00:00:00 /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=zygote --no-zygote-sandbox dxps 567429 567424 0 09:15 ? 00:00:00 /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=zygote dxps 567431 567429 0 09:15 ? 00:00:00 /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=zygote dxps 567465 567428 2 09:15 ? 00:00:00 /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=gpu-process --enable-crash-reporter=2a4601bf-5f85-41cb-8dcf-ce6f48625117,no_channel --user-data-dir=/home/dxps/.config/teams-for-linux --gpu-preferences=WAAAAAAAAAAgAAAEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABgAAEAAAA4AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABAAAAGAAAAAAAAAAYAAAAAAAAAAgAAAAAAAAACAAAAAAAAAAIAAAAAAAAAA== --shared-files --field-trial-handle=3,i,10697308941186590350,4592701824263653,262144 --enable-features=kWebSQLAccess --disable-features=HardwareMediaKeyHandling,SpareRendererForSitePerProcess --variations-seed-version dxps 567469 567424 0 09:15 ? 00:00:00 /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --type=utility --utility-sub-type=network.mojom.NetworkService --lang=en-US --service-sandbox-type=none --enable-crash-reporter=2a4601bf-5f85-41cb-8dcf-ce6f48625117,no_channel --user-data-dir=/home/dxps/.config/teams-for-linux --shared-files=v8_context_snapshot_data:100 --field-trial-handle=3,i,10697308941186590350,4592701824263653,262144 --enable-features=kWebSQLAccess --disable-features=HardwareMediaKeyHandling,SpareRendererForSitePerProcess --variations-seed-version ~ ❯ ``` Of course, removing that last comma and doing a `killall teams-for-linux` it makes all back to normal. The app starts and shows the main window.

issues.title: No notification on tray icon in Teams V2 mode.
issues.body:
Recently, I got popup banner to switch to new Teams, so I chose to switch to new Teams. After that, Teams V2 loads all the time by default. It works, however I'm not getting any notification on tray icon, for example for incoming unread messages/chats.

Is there any way to make it working? Is it known issue? Problem is only on new Teams, legacy mode was ok.

issues.title: followSystemTheme not effective anymore since teams v2 - UI stuck in light mode
issues.body:
I'm using 1.4.27 as a flatpak, and synced theming with the OS was working fine until recently.

I suppose it stopped working when I switched to teams v2.

This is my config.json:

```
{
	"followSystemTheme": true,
	"optInTeamsV2": true,
	"url": "https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/",
	"disableNotificationSound": true
}
```

Has the name of the feature flag changed, do I need to change something here?

Thanks! :)

EDIT: ok it didn't take long to run some more tests.

Indeed it's a v2 regression.
I temporarily switched back to v1, toggled back and forth between the (KDE) Breeze and Breeze dark themes, and with v1 the teams UI is repainted. With v2 it stays in the light mode.

issues.title: teams-for-linux.desktop Ignored Soft Link
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
`teams-for-linux` rpm package created a soft link using `alternatives`
This soft link is installed to `/usr/bin/teams-for-linux`
However, in the `teams-for-linux.desktop`, it is still
`Exec=/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux %U`
It should be
`Exec=/usr/bin/teams-for-linux %U`

Because I use Wayland KDE as my desktop, I need to add
`--enable-features=UseOzonePlatform --ozone-platform=wayland --enable-wayland-ime`
parameters when starting teams-for-linux

So I created a `/usr/local/bin/teams-for-linux-wayland` script containing
```
#!/usr/bin/bash
/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --enable-features=UseOzonePlatform --ozone-platform=wayland --enable-wayland-ime $@
```
Then use command
`sudo alternatives --install /usr/bin/teams-for-linux teams-for-linux /usr/local/bin/teams-for-linux-wayland 0`
to install it.

So I need teams-for-linux.desktop to open my script

Please help fix this issue.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add configuration option to disable in-app notifications for "electron" notificationMethod
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

I find the in-app notification windows in TeamsV2 to be distracting, and when they appear in the bottom right of the screen they block screen real estate of the message typing bar and the send button. 



**Describe the solution you'd like**

I would like a configuration option, documented in the config README which disables the display of these in-app notification windows, without affecting the system notifications delivered by the `"notificationMethod": "electron"` config option.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

An alternative to this for me would be to move these notification windows to the top left of the screen where they are less in the way, and use a less bright color scheme. However these notifications would still be redundant with the system notifications I already get.

**Additional context**

An example notification window which I would like to disable:
![TeamsNotificationBlur](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/48334845/6742135c-4b6e-48ad-be53-a26a7c8b31ce)

issues.title: RPM fails to install on FIPS enabled system.
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I am trying to install teams-for-linux from the yum repo on a RHEL9 system with FIPS mode enabled, it fails the digest because of the version of rpmbuild used.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Boot a RHEL or CentOS 8+ system with `fips=1` on the kernel command line
2. Download latest teams-for-linux RPM
3. run `rpm -ihv teams-for-linux-1.4.27.x86_64.rpm`

**Expected behavior**
RPM should install without error

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 w/[FIPS](https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/how-rhel-8-designed-fips-140-2-requirements) mode
 - Installation package RPM
 - Version 1.4.27-1

**Debug**

$ sudo rpm -ihv teams-for-linux-1.4.27.x86_64.rpm 
Verifying...                          ################################# [100%]
Preparing...                          ################################# [100%]
Updating / installing...
   1:teams-for-linux-1.4.27-1         ################################# [100%]
error: unpacking of archive failed on file /opt/teams-for-linux/LICENSE.electron.txt;662108f4: cpio: Digest mismatch
error: teams-for-linux-1.4.27-1.x86_64: install failed
$


**Additional context**
See RH KB article: https://access.redhat.com/solutions/4460971

This is a pretty common issue and other maintainers have run into it as well https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/omnibus-gitlab/-/issues/5938

I can manually download and install if I ignore Digest settings:

$ sudo rpm -ivh --nodigest --nofiledigest teams-for-linux-1.4.27.x86_64.rpm 
Preparing...                          ################################# [100%]
Updating / installing...
   1:teams-for-linux-1.4.27-1         ################################# [100%]
update-alternatives is /sbin/update-alternatives
$

But this means I will be unable to automatically install updates using the yum/dnf repository.

I am requesting the build team look into providing an RPM version that includes the SHA256 Digest that is FIPS compatible, this requires the use of rpmbuild version 4.14 or later, which is standard in RHEL/CentOS 8+.

Thanks!

issues.title: Window Transparency
issues.body:

when applying custom styles to the UI, you are unable to create a transparent window.

Changing all background css in-app to transparent results in a white-background

This might be wrong and the solution could be something else, I'm no Electron expert

Would like window to be transparent underneath the app css

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
N/A

**Additional context**
Running on Ubuntu 22.04 with wayland + gtk, app is running through the deb install

issues.title: easier "Quit normally"
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Quitting (normally) from the app requires one more click than really needed. The "Quit" in the menu (on the system tray icon) opens a submenu where I can chose the real "Quit"
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/14099928/6f2c6e9b-ab6f-4bc6-92d8-df65225d235c)

**Describe the solution you'd like**
There is enough space, the "Quit Normally" and the "Quit with storage cleanup" could be directly in the menu, not in a submenu.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Additionally or alternatively the Gnome app right click menu (on the task bar), could have a "Quit Normally" item. Even more confusing the "Quit" item visible there just closing the window, totally not quitting (but I guess that comes with Gnome).
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/14099928/1a681bc3-7e64-4591-a63b-fcbb477c706b)

"Your org is switching to the new Teams, and it's ready for you to use"
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Since today, our Teams is displaying the prompt in screenshot.
There is no "close" or "opt-out" button.
I am, however, able to bypass this by selecting Debug (from the right-click menu on the tray icon) and deleting the element. The system works fine, showing that the transition is not necessary.
I would suggest adding an automated procedure to delete said element - either triggered by default or upon user choice.

**To Reproduce**
Run teams-for-linux. May not happen when you have a self-hosted server.

**Expected behavior**
The window not appearing and the user directly accessing Teams.

**Screenshots**

![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/21334530/e4a501d2-31a0-4993-9724-c1b36839d80a)
Element to delete:
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/21334530/d95b1629-92dc-45c1-8fce-4d0c2fd8cfdb)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux (Ubuntu 20.04), but I suspect this is happening in general on "classic" teams.

**Debug**
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/21334530/092e8c20-9fc0-499e-b683-54e8e7b27a40)
Though, I suspect it doesn't help here.

**Additional context**
Switching to the new UI leads to the new UI upon reload. Top-right corner menu allows me to switch back, but I get this window again.

issues.title: Log of messages
issues.body:
Is there a way to log every message received or all messages in a channel to a file?

The ideal solution would be a way to indicate a few channels and get a file for each that update as I receive new message for them.

This can lead to lots of possibilities. Since a log of messages is easy to parse and we can hook that to different services (like getting a SMS whenever you had a message from Bob)

Thanks by advance,

issues.title: Automate flathub upload
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently the upload process for flathub is manually done

**Describe the solution you'd like**
For this to be automatically done 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Getting more people to be able to manually create a release is also a good temporary alternative

**Additional context**

To do a release, please go to https://github.com/flathub/com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux and fork it.

Send a PR by changing the 2 files.

*    com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux.appdata.xml which contains release information. Currently flathub and this repo are having duplicate files. This is something we need to improve.
*    com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux.yml. This is the flatpak build manifest file. Usually this is modified

```
 sources:
      - type: file
        url: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/download/v1.4.16/teams-for-linux_1.4.16_amd64.deb
        sha256: 8507a00f3a562abafa8382d235027eb361fb0464ae01db242266e17e226cd968
```

Flathub people then review the PR and either accept it and merge it or not.

issues.title: Attention span related settings (full-mute, disable app icon animation)
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I'm always frustrated when I'm focused and Teams plays sounds. I already disabled all configurable sounds, but some sounds still play. Also I don't appreciate the icon moving regularly when the window is disabled. I would like the Teams client to not constantly try to grab my attention when it's minimized. I would like to have control over when I use Teams, in order to be able to be "in the zone" more effectively.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
* A setting could full-mute the teams client, as in "It does not play any sounds, ever."
* Another setting could disable the animation of the Teams icon.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Usually I mute applications that play unsolicited sounds using the per-application volume mixer of my OS, but Ubuntu desktop seems to have removed this.
I do not think there is an easy solution to prevent applications from showing animations on their icons.

**Additional context**
None.

issues.title: optInTeamsV2 does nothing
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

I tried to use the `optInTeamsV2` option to switch to the new version of Teams, but without success.

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

  - Stop Teams
  - Edit `~/.var/app/com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux/config/teams-for-linux/config.json`
```json
{
  "closeAppOnCross": true,
  "minimized": true,
  "optInTeamsV2": true,
}
```
  - Start Teams
  - See the warning about Teams Classic

**Expected behavior**

From what I understand from the README, this option should force the switchover to the new Teams.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora Workstation 39
 - Installation package: FlatPak
 - Version: 1.4.13

**Additional context**

I was able to solve the problem by specifying the `url` option like that:

```json
{
  "closeAppOnCross": true,
  "minimized": true,
  "optInTeamsV2": true,
  "url": "https://teams.cloud.microsoft"
}
```

issues.title: Feature request: Automatic office/home location
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I recently discovered a setting under the user options (when clicking on my avatar in the top-right) to specify whether I am working remotely or in the office. Given the flexible working arrangements, this may be quite useful to know if colleagues are in, and to let them know if I am, but the setting is manual (it resets daily), and it is easy to forget to set it up.

![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/5610438/e94fc6cd-392b-40b4-abb5-071e5b20486a)


**Describe the solution you'd like**
I do have an office computer and a home one, so potentially a launch or configuration setting that would set this up automatically would be great (i.e., my office computer always sets it as "in the office"). Alternatively, this could also be defined by IP (i.e., my institution has a static IP range, so it should be easy to check whether I am in the office or somewhere else and set this automatically), which might be more flexible.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Alternatively, some kind of popup when the setting is not yet set and teams are started for the first time or something could be useful to remember to set this (ideally enabled through a config?).

issues.title: Tray (Notification) icon broken in MATE with snap images
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The tray icon appears broken when running from snap using MATE desktop.
The same icon works fine if installed via DEB package on MATE desktop.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Switch to MATE desktop (can be installed with sudo apt-get install mate-desktop-environment)
2. Install teams-for-linux via snap
3. Invoke teams-for-linux either by menu or by /snap/bin/teams-for-linux, etc.
4. Note that a system tray (notification area, indicator, whatever people call it these days) has wrong icon

**Expected behavior**
The proper Teams icon should appear in the system tray (notification area, indicator area, etc)

**Screenshots**
MATE + snap: broken
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/16827398/ec873155-a1ab-431d-98b5-04d8087aabbf)

MATE + DEB: good
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/16827398/e650532b-40c9-4888-ad5e-a7aaff6a0d37)

MATE + DEB + snap running at same time: suddenly good
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/16827398/409c6c0b-bffc-4fb4-aa70-0ab1df4b3ce7)



**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
 - Happens on MATE desktop only, snap image only (XFCE, GNOME, Unity are fine, DEB is fine in all cases)
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version 1.4.14 (also tried 1.4.15 beta/edge, and also broken up to this point on older versions)


**Additional context**
Tried using workaround from other bugs (#506) to no avail.
Only happens with snap image.  DEB package has proper icon, as expected.
XFCE, GNOME, Unity also have correct icon when running the snap.  This is MATE only.

Additional testing:
Installing both DEB and snap at the same time, still broken icon running snap version only.
Running both DEB and snap at the same time, both icons suddenly appear correct.
Closing DEB version with snap version still running, snap icon reverts back to broken again.

issues.title: Feature request: Sync microphone mute with audio source
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Having no mic mute indicator on OS level or hw level (mute LED hidden, because lid closed) makes it hard to determine if you're currently muted or not. Other people don't see you as muted as well.
On Windows the button is synced.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be great if there was a way to sync the teams mute button with the currently used audio source mute status.
At least teams detects that the microphone is muted on OS level so it might be possible to intercept that and toggle the mute button.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Currently I'm using a custom mute indicator on OS level based on dzen2. It's just a weird hack, though.

none

issues.title: Fails to start with flatpak
issues.body:
**Description**
Fails to start using flatpak

**To Reproduce**
Run
`$ flatpak run com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux`

See the following output:
```
LaunchProcess: failed to execvp:
xdg-settings
[2:0318/130221.460924:ERROR:ozone_platform_x11.cc(244)] Missing X server or $DISPLAY
[2:0318/130221.460944:ERROR:env.cc(257)] The platform failed to initialize.  Exiting.
```

**Expected behavior**
Application starts and opens a new window

**Desktop**
 - OS: Arch Linux
 - Flatpak
 - Version 1.4.13
 - Desktop: Plasma 6
 - Desktop session: Wayland

**Additional info**
Started happening to me after i ran the following commands:
```
$ flatpak --user override --filesystem=/home/$USER/.icons/:ro
$ flatpak --user override --filesystem=/usr/share/icons/:ro
```
As suggested in [This](https://www.reddit.com/r/kde/comments/yi6kfu/comment/iuig6c9/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web3x&utm_name=web3xcss&utm_term=1&utm_content=share_button) Reddit answer about a bad cursor question.

But the error message was different, something about flatpak not sharing /usr/share/icons. Then I ran 
```
$ flatpak --user override --reset
```
And the problem with desktop appeared instead.

issues.title: Configuration file has to be a regular file, and can not be a symbolic link
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The configuration file `config.json` is silently ignored, when the file is not regular, but, for example, a symbolic link.

The following `info` message is printed (but the log level has to be amended so the message is visible):

    [INFO] No config file found, using default values

**Expected behavior**
Either the configuration file is read and interpreted, or a warning log message is printed so the message is visible by default.

issues.title: Sound volume
issues.body:
I am placing this issue just for others that may get into the same problem that I had. Here is the context: - Running any recent version (currently ver. 1.4.13) of Teams for Linux - On an up-to-date Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS distro - The sound received from others calls participants was quite low compared with any other app running in the system.
During a call, I finally found that this _Chromium_ app represents Teams for Linux instance. Therefore, just increasing that volume solved my issue. :partying_face: ![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/14140226/47397400-890c-4f32-8970-d919e925daf4)
:information_source: Not sure if you can either rename that app name, as seen by the system, to properly state the app, or have an option to increase the sound volume from the app itself. I'm still a happy user of this app & thanks again for maintaining it! :pray:

issues.title: Dumps core at 4PM every day after eating entire heap
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
As title says, around 4PM every day Teams for Linux will dump its core. This is also normally about 8 hours after I started it. **The main symptom of this is Teams just disappears**. You can only tell its a core dump by looking in the core dumps folder.

Its particularly annoying because the size of a Teams core dump is 600mb+ compressed so it soon starts eating disk without regular clearouts.

This has only started happening in the past month or so. It could be specific to me, my hardware and drivers but at this point I am just wanting to see if anyone else has noticed the same.

I have been using the "new" Teams, not sure if its relevant. I've reverted back to ye olde Teams and will report back if it solves it or not.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Teams
2. Use Teams
3. Wait about 8 hours (or 4PM) - :laughing: 
4. Teams dumps its core

**Expected behavior**
Teams not to dump its core.

**Screenshots**
N/A

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch Linux
 - Installation package: AUR
 - Version: latest over the past month or so (I keep it up to date)

**Additional context**
Some nuggets from the logs:

```
Feb 29 16:06:51 teams-for-linux.desktop[35000]: <--- Last few GCs --->
Feb 29 16:06:51 teams-for-linux.desktop[35000]: [35000:0x1b20003e8000] 29058495 ms: Scavenge 3878.6 (4049.6) -> 3878.6 (4049.6) MB, 106.55 / 0.00 ms  (average mu = 1.000, current mu = 1.000) task;
Feb 29 16:06:51 teams-for-linux.desktop[35000]: [35000:0x1b20003e8000] 29058600 ms: Scavenge 3878.6 (4049.6) -> 3878.6 (4049.6) MB, 104.47 / 0.00 ms  (average mu = 1.000, current mu = 1.000) task;
Feb 29 16:06:51 teams-for-linux.desktop[35000]: [35000:0x1b20003e8000] 29058703 ms: Scavenge 3878.6 (4049.6) -> 3878.6 (4049.6) MB, 103.68 / 0.00 ms  (average mu = 1.000, current mu = 1.000) task;
Feb 29 16:06:51 teams-for-linux.desktop[35000]: <--- JS stacktrace --->
Feb 29 16:06:51 teams-for-linux.desktop[35000]: FATAL ERROR: NewSpace::EnsureCurrentCapacity Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory
```

I could post the entire stack trace but probably no point since the reason looks fairly clear for the core dump.

issues.title: Support downgrading version of deb package
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When issues like #1108 happen, I'm not able to downgrade to the last working version using `apt`.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like to access older versions of `teams-for-linux` using the apt repo.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
An alternative would be installing the `deb` manually from the GitHub release, but that's kinda awkward and breaks the intention of the apt repo.

**Additional context**
Right now, only the latest released version is available in the apt repo to me:
```bash
$ apt list -a teams-for-linux
Listing... Done
teams-for-linux/stable,now 1.4.11 amd64 [installed]
```

Edit: If you can pinpoint me to where the deb release happens I'd happily help out (and learn)!

issues.title: The New Microsoft Teams
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Microsoft has announced that Classic Teams will no longer be available after March 31, 2024. Currently, in the teams-for-linux application, there is a banner at the top with the text:

> Classic Teams won't be available for use after March 31, 2024. To use the new Teams, update your browser to meet requirements or contact your IT admin. See requirements

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to see the teams-for-linux application support the New Teams implementation. However, I don't know if that is technically possible, or if this update is already on your roadmap.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
An alternative would be to not use the teams-for-linux application. However, since the only other desktop application option for Linux is the teams preview app, which hasn't been updated in the Arch Linux AUR since March 2023, my only other option would be to use a browser to access the web interface for Teams.

**Additional context**

issues.title: Wrong time zone set in meeting scheduler
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
My local time and the time setting of my computer is UTC+1. The Teams-for-linux calendar shows the local time, red current time line is position of my local time. But when I click in the calendar to schedule a new meeting the selected time zone in this new window is UTC. I think you can call this a bug as it is very easy to forget to switch to the correct time zone and schedule the meeting to the wrong time.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open calendar in Teams-for-linux
2. Click in the calendar to schedule a new meeting
3. Notice that the meeting will be scheduled using UTC not the local time (must be corrected via upper list box)
4. If you now click to send invitation to participants it is too late to realize that wrong time zone was used.

**Expected behavior**
The new meeting scheduler should start with the local time of the computer selected.

**Screenshots**
Two screenshots appended.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package: deb installed from teamsforlinux.de repository.
 - Version: teams-for-linux ver. 1.4.10

**Additional context**
![Teams_new_meeting_window](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/56929472/eed3d4da-7d7b-48df-a5a6-0f6894f86b65)
![Teams-for-linux_calendar](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/56929472/3487e3c8-75e9-49d6-bb28-4d261f801b88)

issues.title: Remove `Option + Left` and `Option + Right` keyboard shortcut on MacOS
issues.body:
Fixes https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/1101

I first wanted to replace the problematic `Option + Left` with `Command + Left` for history navigation, but it did not work for some reason, so I ended up removing the history navigation shortcuts completely for Mac.

issues.title: Mac keyboard shortcut Option (⌥) + Left
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
On Mac when writing text, the default action of Option + Left keyboard shortcut is to the beginning of the previous word. See https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201236
> Option–Left Arrow: Move the insertion point to the beginning of the previous word.

Similarly with the Option + Right, which jumps to the next word.

However, teams-for-linux uses this shortcut for back and forward history navigation. It seems that the `ALT` defined in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/dd2fe2ff3cccd5820c1459e5ec10ac2610fcd9ef/app/browser/tools/shortcuts.js#L34-L35 is translated as `Option` on Mac, which causes this.

This is very annoying, as it disallows jumping by words when editing message, which is something pretty common and something stored pretty deep in my muscle memory.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start writing message, write multiple words
2. Use `Option + left` keyboard shortcut
3. Expect to jump to beginning of previous word, but you are navigated to the previous screen (message, etc) instead.

**Expected behavior**
Expect to jump to beginning of previous word instead of back navigation.


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: MacOS
 - Installation package https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/download/v1.4.10/teams-for-linux-1.4.10-mac.zip
 - Version 1.4.10

issues.title: Notifications for Meetings created in a Team are mixed up
issues.body:
DESCRIPTION:
The bug present itself when a meeting is created inside a team.
If any chat message is sent in those meetings, whether they are still happening or not, the notification shows as if a new meeting has started, so it's a wrong notification and it does not point out which meeting specifically, but a generic "a meeting has started" with the name of another re-occurring meeting of the same channel (but not the correct one where the message was sent), considering teams allows for more than one meeting in the same team.

STEPS TO REPRODUCE:
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Teams
2. Click on "Teams" from the left sidebar
3. Select any channel from one of the Teams you're in
 3.a This steps assume a Team exists, with a channel already created, and that two or more people are in this team and channel 
3. On the top-right corner, press the "Meet Now" button to create a meeting
5. Now, if anybody who was or is in that meeting writes a message the meeting's chat, the notification received by other people will be the one described above.

EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:
Show the name of the meeting (custom names can be set) and the chat message instead of "a meeting has started"

SYSTEM:
 - OS: ArchLinux
 - Installation package: Archlinux AUR Package
 - Version: 1.4.10-1
 - Using Wayland and Mako to handle notifications

issues.title: Meeting Links Not Opening
issues.body:
Meeting links using msteams://teams.microsoft.com are not starting a meeting inside teams-on-linux.  Copying and pasting the meeting link inside teams-on-linux and then clicking on the link does work.  I've gone through the issues and it seemed like this was already fixed, so I don't know if it's "just me"

Example:
```
/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux "msteams://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/<meeting link goes here>"}" --appLogLevels=error,info,warn,debug

DEBUG] configPath:,/home/chris/.config/teams-for-linux
[DEBUG] configFile:,[object Object]
[INFO] App already running
```

I'm running on Devuan Daedalus (Debian Bookworm).  Running Teams-For-Linux deb package 1.4.10

If run 
/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux https:/teams.microsoft.com/<meetinglink>
It gives me the same already running error, but it opens a whole new window, replacing the previous window, and opens up the meeting.

I do have Microsoft's Teams client installed as well.  xdg-mime settings shows teams-for-linux as being the default:
```
xdg-mime query default x-scheme-handler/msteams
teams-for-linux.desktop
```

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

issues.title: Add `latest-linux-x86_64.AppImage` and `latest-linux-aarch64.AppImage` download link
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It's very useful to have a direct download link to the latest AppImage version for both `x86_64` and  `aarch64`, not having to research which version the file name contains and adapt scripts manually. There is already a `latest-linux.yml` but no `latest-linux.AppImage`. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to have a `latest-linux-x86_64.AppImage` and `latest-linux-aarch64.AppImage` (like Fedora Asahi Remix OS uses) which maybe is the same as `latest-linux-arm64.AppImage`.

**firejail sandbox**
How to properly invoke execution via sandbox firejail for the different types of packages offered. Examples of what could work:

```
firejail --appimage --profile=/etc/firejail/teams-for-linux.profile teams-for-linux.AppImage
firejail --profile=/etc/firejail/teams-for-linux.profile electron "/usr/share/teams-for-linux"

```

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Using manual download/install scripts to detect architecture and set the file name version to latest version manually.

issues.title: Add followSystemTheme config option
issues.body:
- New command line option `--followSystemTheme`. Defaults to false.
- When following system theme, set Teams theme to the current light/dark setting after a small delay on startup. (2.5 seconds, if you attempt to do this too soon Teams freaks out and only updates the outer window with the new theme)
- Update Teams light/dark theme to match system theme when the system theme is changed.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
add a cli option to either:
- send a http request as if it was from inside the app, this would enable me to simply extract the correct request using dev tools and do e.g.: `teams-for-linux curl 'https://presence.teams.microsoft.com/v1/me/forceavailability/' -X 'PUT' --data-raw '{"availability":"Away"}'
- dedicated function for this, and maybe other things in the future, e.g.: `teams-for-linux set-availibility away`

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually set my self to `away` every time I lock my screen and unset it afterward.

issues.title: rework wayland screenshare source selection
issues.body:
I've been experiencing the issue described in #912 and did a bit of investigation. It seems that the issue can be resolved by calling the electron desktopCapturer getSources method rather than navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia directly as the app is currently doing. 

This pull request makes the following changes:
- Amend chromeApi.js to call desktopCapturerGetSources rather than navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia on wayland and hence fix #912
- Remove the bypassWaylandSourceSelection parameter since you can now request screen or window on desktopCapturerGetSources (the portal dialogue has a tab for screens and another for windows, see screenshots which removed the need for the current separate selection dialogue)
- Removed unused wayland handling code from streamSelector/browser.js (this is only used for X11 now).

Tested on KDE Wayland, Gnome Wayland and  Hyprland

KDE desktop portal
![teams-for-linux-kde](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/56479007/66a1127c-0aac-47c1-8e59-13c66ec1dff0)

Gnome desktop portal
![teams-for-linux-gnome](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/56479007/772b6add-9878-4de5-b861-87c80e4fead5)
