issues.title: fix getting version from cli arg with yargs after the Electron 28 bump
issues.body:
After the Electron 28 bump, `teams-for-linux --version` is not working for us (nixpkgs) when packaged as ASAR and using our own build of Electron (it gives an output text of "unknown")

This seems to be because yargs expects `require.main.filename` to exist (see [here](https://github.com/yargs/yargs/blob/e517318cea0087b813f5de414b3cdec7b70efe33/lib/yargs-factory.ts#L1617) and [here](https://github.com/yargs/yargs/blob/e517318cea0087b813f5de414b3cdec7b70efe33/lib/platform-shims/cjs.ts#L23)), but that is no longer the case with Electron 28 and is a more-or-less intended change by Electron upstream: electron/electron#40501

I'm not sure if this PR is the best solution (I'm not too familiar with the codebase), but more or less this PR propagates `app.getVersion()` to the yargs call and manually tells it what version number to use, since the `package.json` finding now fails for yargs, but Electron does know the correct app version number

Let me know if you would like the implementation tweaked. Also thank you for your work on teams-for-linux!

Side note: Switching from CJS entrypoint to ESM entrypoints would probably also fix the issue, but that would be a much more involved change to teams-for-linux than I am comfortable with doing myself, so I opted for a simple fix we can apply now

issues.title: Cropped screensharing of windows
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When sharing a screen from a window using Teams-for-Linux, the displayed view is cropped at the top and bottom. This issue is specific to Teams-for-Linux and does not occur with other applications that utilize the Wayland screen-sharing backend.

**To Reproduce**
1. Share a screen by selecting a window within Teams-for-Linux.

**Expected behavior**
The entire window should be shared without any cropping.

**Screenshots**
Actual window:
![original_window_censored](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/42340241/a61e598c-3894-4863-a78a-c0f6f12afd0c)

View on Client:
![client_view_censored](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/42340241/1cf674c4-da8a-4ead-8b1c-b62ff88d931d)

**Host Desktop**
 - OS: Fedora 39 with Gnome and Wayland
 - Installation package: Flathub
 - Version 1.3.19

**Additional observations**
- The issue does not occur when sharing a window through https://www.webrtc-experiment.com/Pluginfree-Screen-Sharing/#2653888812629166, despite using the same sharing backend. This suggests that Teams-for-Linux might be cropping the image.
- When the window is made very wide, the entire window can be shared without cropping, indicating a possible relation to the aspect ratio of the shared stream.
- Sharing a 21:9 screen does not present this issue, and this aspect ratio does not cause issues with window sharing either.
- A related issue may be found in this post: [Issue Comment](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/961#issuecomment-1857139209), where a user mentions a similar cropping problem.

Further debugging information can be provided upon guidance. Any assistance is greatly appreciated.

I dug around in the settings a bit - could not get OS notifications to work.
I know there are known issues in this area.
Should this work on my system ?
Can I tweak anything ?

Thanks !!

issues.title: quick suggestion re ubuntu install
issues.body:
Some quick suggestions regarding the Ubuntu installation process
(https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux#debianubuntu-and-other-derivatives)

```lang=sh
wget -qO - https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/teams-for-linux.asc | sudo apt-key add -
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture)] https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/teams-for-linux.list
sudo apt update
sudo apt install teams-for-linux
```
It doesn't seem to be necessary to store the key with a specified filename, because the correct key gets identified automatically.
I thought it might be posible to remove the `dpkg --print-architecture` option as well, but this didn't work, because on my system, it attempts to access an `i386` version of the package. This doesn't seem to happen with other repos, so maybe there is some other config issue on the deb repo server.

- changed kill singal for "incomingCallCommand" from SIGKILL to SIGTERM to get access to the trap functionality in scripts

- The incomingCallCommand now terminates any previous running command.

issues.title: Make sure executable is writable before running fuses in afterpack script
issues.body: When the electron binary that is used to create the `teams-for-linux` binary isn't writable, the `teams-for-linux` binary isn't either. This results in errors when running the afterpack script. This PR makes sure that the binary is writable before running the fuses.

issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Every time I open the client I have to type my username&password credentials into my company's login page. Since I use a rather involved password (which is usually remembered by the browser when I browse other sites of my company) I have to retrieve it from my vault every time. This is a quite inconvenient workflow for me.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Since my company's SSO service allows the usage of X509 certificates it would be much more handy for me to install my personal certificate in Teams For Linux and log in with that.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I have gnome-keyring and kwallet installed, but still my company's login page as displayed in Teams For Linux does not remember my username&password credentials. So I thought that using X509 could be a viable solution to my problem.

issues.title: Blurry window when using wayland with integer scaling
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Recently chrome introduced a [new feature that enables fractional scaling on wayland](https://bugs.chromium.org/p/chromium/issues/detail?id=1356014), however that features makes windows appear blurry on integer scaled screens. To fix this we can use the disable-features switch with WaylandFractionalScaleV1.
However the application does not allow for arbitrary values in the --disable-features switch, therefore I'm stuck with a blurry teams since version 1.3.14.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. If you use gnome, disable 'scale-monitor-framebuffer' experimental feature
2. Set your screen to use an integer scale bigger than 1
3. Open teams
4. You should get a blurry window

**Expected behavior**
You should be able to pass custom flags to --disable-features, allowing to disable WaylandFractionalScaleV1

**Screenshots**
Blurry window at 200% scale in a 4k monitor
![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/38868063/ed597f81-9ecc-415b-bf0c-bdaeeded6659)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 39
 - Installation package: flatpak
 - Version 1.3.23

issues.title: Enhanced second_ringer.sh script for Linux and MacOS
issues.body:
This is a solution (for me) that solves the problem of Teams not ringing audible when earphones are plugged in.
My suggestion is based on the example from @93Sprinter, that was included in the release notes of version 1.3.21.

Here is my alternative script "second_ringer.sh" for use under Linux:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
export PULSE_SINK=1
/usr/bin/cvlc --play-and-exit --aout --intf=rc /usr/share/sounds/Oxygen-Im-Phone-Ring.ogg
```
This works under MacOS Sonoma for me, builtin speakers, homebrew under/usr/loca on a Intel Mac, may need tweaking with different headphones and/or installations:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# alternative path: /Applications/VLC.app/Contents/MacOS/VLC
# alternative path (brew M1/M2/M3): /opt/homebrew/bin/vlc
/usr/local/bin/vlc --play-and-exit --aout --intf=rc <your path to the soundfile>/Oxygen-Im-Phone-Ring.ogg
```

Explanations (Linux):

1. I have to user PULSE_SINK=1 since my speaker shows up as device 1 and my headset as device 0, ymmv
   * run `pacmd list-sinks` to determine you outputs
2. Switched from vlc to cvlc which is the CLI variant and does not open any window
3. Added the play and exit parameter in order to make vlc quit after playing the sound
4. My desktop environment is KDE Plasma 5 thus I can rely on already installed sound files, ymmv ;-)

Cheers,
Holger

**Describe the bug**

issues.title: Enable cookieEncryption fuse for electron
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

At the moment, cookies for teams-for-linux (access tokens etc) are stored in plain-text in a sqllite db on disk, this is the default for electron.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Enable the cookieEncryption fuse to make use of the built-in cookie encryption option.

https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/tutorial/fuses#cookieencryption

**Additional context**

The official MS Teams client had similar issues, which have since been mitigated.

https://www.vectra.ai/blog/undermining-microsoft-teams-security-by-mining-tokens

issues.title: Fix #992
Added three new events to activityHub.js (incoming-call-created,incom…
issues.body:
…ing-call-connecting,incoming-call-disconnecting).

These three events are handled inside the index.js of minAppWindow to run a command on an incoming call. The command can be specified as cli argument or inside a config.json as incomingCallCommand parameter. As an example this can be used to play a sound on a second speaker to simulate a second ringer.

Like so:

teams-for-linux --incomingCallCommand second_ringer.sh

second_ringer.sh:
#!/bin/bash
export PULSE_SINK=0
vlc -aout pulse -I dummy ring.mp3

issues.title: Whata about teams 2.0 and beyond
issues.body:
Hello !

This is more sort of a question rather than a query for action or something.  Its purpose is to understand what will be the teams-for-linux status now that [teams 2.0 is out](https://windowsreport.com/microsoft-teams-2-0-download).  Teams 2.0 is not electron based, and although they stopped supporting the electron Team for gnu+linux some time ago, the windows/mac desktop app was still electron.

The thing is that teams 2.0 is now based on webView 2.0 instead of electron, and react instead of angular.  The big part of it is not developing for electron:
- [Teams 2.0 Moves Away from Electron to Embrace Edge WebView2](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-teams/teams-2-0-moves-away-from-electron-to-embrace-edge-webview2/m-p/2484565)
- [Stop saying Microsoft Teams is being rewritten from Electron to React](https://blog.thoughtstuff.co.uk/2021/08/stop-saying-microsoft-teams-is-being-rewritten-from-electron-to-react)
- [Electron to WebView2 – What does it really mean for the Teams desktop client, and for a Client SDK?](https://blog.thoughtstuff.co.uk/2021/06/electron-to-webview2-what-does-it-really-mean-for-the-teams-desktop-client-and-for-a-client-sdk)

Now, there's hope to eventually, with really low priority webview2 gets support for gnu+linux, and there's a [linux support issue](https://github.com/MicrosoftEdge/WebView2Feedback/issues/645) about it.  Mac already gained support, and actually the windows report at the beginning shows that the recently release teams 2.0 includes Mac clients, and there's also a [mac support issue](https://github.com/MicrosoftEdge/WebView2Feedback/issues/1314#issuecomment-1751265475) on which it's mentioned the support for Mac is there.  All this just to say, as usual for MS stuff, gnu+linux is not part of their priorities.

Coming back to teams-for-linux, as it's based on electron, what's the status and plan?  Will teams-for-linux support only pre 2.0 teams technology, components and/or webapp?  I guess at some point being based on old versions on teams stuff might become difficult, given compatibility issues and the like, but can't really tell.

So just wondering what'll happen with teams-for-linux.  Not sure if we'll be able to use web browser (we should), but so far, at least for me, on Firefox, Librewolf and ungoogled chromium, there's no way to connect to meetings, whether something just fails (Firefox/Librewolf), or I get into an infinite loop without connecting (ungoogled chromium).  So I really enjoy teams-for-linux being available...

Greetings !

issues.title: Updated documentation
prevent arbitrary command execution via URL when using --defaultURLHandler
issues.body:
I've just seen changes in 1.3.16, and I think #996 points to a security flaw (which was there already, just happened to see it because of this change). In this version, when using `--defaultURLHandler favorite-browser`, opening an URL will execute a shell process ([`child_process.exec(...)`](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html#child_processexeccommand-options-callback)) to run the following command:
```sh
favorite-browser "THE_URL"
```
Although `THE_URL` has some URL-encoded characters, this still leaves plenty of potential for arbitrary command execution via a malicious URL in a Teams message. In particular, the following characters are not encoded in the path of an URL: `$`, `(`, `)`. 

Here is a way to exploit that: `https://google.com/$(sudo$IFS$(true)shutdown$IFS$(true)-h$IFS$(true)now)`
  - `$IFS` is substituted with some space characters
  - `$(true)` is substituted with empty string, it's used here to separate `$IFS` and whatever follows
  - `sudo shutdown -h now` is the resulting command...
  - `$(...)` executes this command in a sub-shell, and is substituted with its standard output (which is empty here)
  - `favorite-browser` will simply open `https://google.com/` (while the computer starts shutting down if `sudo` expects no password)

In this PR, I propose to use [`child_process.execFile(...)`](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html#child_processexecfilefile-args-options-callback) as a safer replacement for `exec(...)`. No shell involved, just a command name (`favorite-browser`) and its unaltered string argument (the URL string, as-is).

The drawback is that maybe someone somewhere has been successfully using something like `--defaultURLHandler "favorite-browser --some-browser-option"`, and this will not work anymore. IMHO, it's an acceptable trade-off. If you think it's not, then some alternative fixes could be:
- with `execFile(...)`: split `defaultURLHandler`, use first word as command and remaining ones as additional arguments before the URL
- with `exec(...)`: properly shell-escape the `details.url` string in the command (I _think_ escaping `$` as `\$`, plus the double-quotes which has already been added around the string, might be enough, thanks to the limited set of chars we get in URLs, but not 100% sure)

issues.title: Crashes (segfaults) on Wayland
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Ever since the last update (from v1.3.12 to v1.3.14), I am fairly frequently getting crashes when running on Wayland (often when I am in a call). Although the crashes can occur randomly, I can reproduce it fairly consistently using the steps below.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Launch in a terminal with the following command
   ```
   teams-for-linux --enable-features=UseOzonePlatform --ozone-platform=wayland
   ```
2. Quickly maximise the application before it has time to load fully.
3. It will crash and and I see this output in my terminal
   ```
   [ERROR] net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED
   [1]    52878 segmentation fault (core dumped)  teams-for-linux --enable-features=UseOzonePlatform --ozone-platform=wayland 
   ```

**Expected behavior**
No crash or segfault like in v1.3.12.

**Screenshots**
n/a

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 38 
 - Installation package: RPM
 - Version: 1.3.14
 - Desktop Environment: KDE Plasma 5.27.8
 - Kernel Version: 6.5.8-200.fc38.x86_64 (64-bit)
 - Graphics Platform: Wayland

**Additional context**
I've been experiencing a similar issue with VSCode which is also electron based under certain conditions (microsoft/vscode#184124) which has been blamed on this upstream electron issue (electron/electron#37531) and I think this might be the same issue. For now, I will stick with v1.3.12.

issues.title: Flag disable gpu
issues.body: Just adding a flag to disable GPU, it fixes #981  for me (and possibly for other people).

issues.title: Fix default url handler
issues.body:
URL is not passed correctly to handler if it contains special chars.
    
Example failing url: `https://mcas-proxyweb.mcas.ms/certificate-checker?login=false&originalUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fstatics.teams.cdn.office.net.mcas.ms...`
    
In the case above the url handler only receives `https://mcas-proxyweb.mcas.ms/certificate-checker?login=false` (the url is cut after "&").

issues.title: Password starting with special character not saving
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When using the app a password that begins with the special character ` (backtick) the user authentication is not preserved between session requiring re-authentication each launch.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set your Microsoft password to something beginning with ` (backtick)
2. Authenticate to teams-for-linux
3. Exit teams-for-linux
4. Relaunch and observe that you are asked to re-authenticate

**Expected behavior**
Password and/or login token should be preserved to avoid need to re-authenticate on each launch.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: archlinux
 - Installation package: flatpak
 - Version: teams-for-linux@1.13.3

**Additional context**
It's not the end of the world, especially since work has the paranoia settings cranked to the max on 2FA so i have about a 1 in 3 chance of needing to reenter 2FA on each launch of teams-for-linux anyway. But it seems a particularly odd bug...

issues.title: Screen sharing does not always consider window scaling
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I use Desktop scaling in Wayland (150%). Teams for Linux works fine with sharing my screen and also most windows. But, there are some issues when I try to share other Flatpack applications. It seems to only share the top left part of the window, cutting off from the right and the bottom. I also noticed some issues when rapidly switching the size of the shared window (with the Super+Left/Right Arrow short-cuts). But they recovered after changing the size of the window again.
I tested 3 different Flatpack applications to share (Teams-for-Linux, Signal and Discord), who all had the same issue. A bunch of native applications (Gnome Files, Firefox, Gnome Settings, ...) worked fine, apart from the fast size changing issue (which is a real edge-case and not important in my opinion).


**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour:
1. Set your display scaling option to 150% (have not tested other scaling) in Gnome.
2. Join or set up a Teams meeting using Teams for Linux.
3. Use the share functionality to share a window from a Flatpack installed program (i.e. Teams itself).
4. See the result on another device in the call.

**Expected behaviour**
Both native and Flatpack installed applications should be shared in the correct resolution without parts of the application cutted away.

**Screen-shots**
From sharing a Flatpack application window:
![Screenshot_20231023-124923](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/1598708/eec941a9-0499-49df-b6b3-528ce8da124b)
![Screenshot_20231023-124939](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/1598708/502d5aaa-1932-4fb5-964f-74167c1659b2)
From rapidly switching window size (as mentioned before, not really relevant):
![Screenshot_20231023-125114](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/1598708/14189669-a759-4ca1-86fd-23ec54f1ac6e)

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Fedora 38 (Wayland and Gnome 44.5)
 - Installation package Flatpack
 - Version 1.3.13

**Additional context**
From my limited knowledge of the underlying technologies, this could be an issue with Electron or Chrome screen sharing in combination with Wayland and desktop scaling, but I lack the knowledge, of addressing this at Electron or somewhere else with the adequate information.

#987

issues.title: Enhance zooming in and out controls
issues.body:
1. Since zooming in is `ctrl` and `+` key and `+` needs `shift` on many keyboard layouts so it make effectively` ctrl` + `shift` + `+` key for zoom in but only `ctrl` in case of zoom out. need to be consistent and make it work with or without `shift` like in real browsers
2. When reaching zoom limits (min or max) it visually stop zooming in/out but the actual zoom level keep on decreasing/increasing make it hard to recover from it unless you press `ctrl` + `0` or press the corresponding opposite key multiple times. so need to stop the zoom level when reaching the min/max
3. Add mouse wheel support for zooming in and out

issues.title: Disabled context isolation is a security hole/please enable
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
For #434 and further #492 context isolation was disabled. This is a security issue as it exposes
all electron api to websites running inside Electron.

If I understand correctly one has to implement contextBridges to be able to use ipcRender which
is required for WebRTC.
I've linked a few documentation pages around this below.

**Desktop :**
 - OS: any
 - Installation package: any
 - Version: >1.0.0.17

**Additional context**
- Related issue: #492
- Commit in question: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/6e8782b41016e628b69c73de8a4422d175105701
- [Electron on Context Isolation](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/tutorial/context-isolation)
- [Electron on contextBridge](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/context-bridge)
- [WebRTC-hid-SDK on Context Isolation](https://github.com/RibbonCommunications/webrtc-hid-sdk/blob/HEAD/docs/context-isolation.md)

issues.title: 3rd party app integration is not working as expected; example Jira integration
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When attempting to copy/transfer the contents of a chat to a Jira ticket it is possible to use a Jira plugin for MS Teams.
This requires a session token to be generated for the current Teams window to be able to make the appropriate REST API Calls against the selected Jira instance.
In order to do so MS Teams will trigger the "system web browser" where you can login using your MS Account.
The token will then be passed back to MS Teams and subsequent calls will use this token until it expires.

**To Reproduce**
1. Connect MS Teams with a Jira (Server/DC in our case) instance (done by our admin so I am doing a wild guess here) using this Jira app: https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1217836/microsoft-teams-for-jira?tab=overview&hosting=datacenter and the documented MS Teams App as well
2. Configure your personal account by obtaining the "Jira Server ID" (a guid) from your MS Teams admin
3. Click on the context menu item "..." -> "More Actions" -> "Create Comment" (or "Create Issue")
4. You may be prompted with a field requesting the above "Jira Server ID" from step 2
5. You will be asked to authenticate by clicking on a button "Sign in with Microsoft account" (sometimes needs to be triggered twice)
6. This opens a Tab/Window in your "system web browser" pointing to "https://jira-server.msteams-atlassian.com/login.html"
7. After a successful authentication the generated session token will be passed (should be passed) back to the MS Teams window
8. The dialog does not switch to the "next" page and simply keeps on displaying the "Sign in with Microsoft account" button

**Expected behavior**
You are able to reproduce this buy using the MS Teams Chrome PWA, this triggers a google chrome window from "itself" and that seems to work:
1. Connect MS Teams with a Jira (Server/DC in our case) instance (done by our admin so I am doing a wild guess here) using this Jira app: https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1217836/microsoft-teams-for-jira?tab=overview&hosting=datacenter and the documented MS Teams App as well
2. Configure your personal account by obtaining the "Jira Server ID" (a guid) from your MS Teams admin
3. Click on the context menu item "..." -> "More Actions" -> "Create Comment" (or "Create Issue")
4. You may be prompted with a field requesting the above "Jira Server ID" from step 2
5. You will be asked to authenticate by clicking on a button "Sign in with Microsoft account" (sometimes needs to be triggered twice)
6. This opens a Tab/Window in your "system web browser" pointing to "https://jira-server.msteams-atlassian.com/login.html"
7. After a successful authentication the generated session token will be passed (should be passed) back to the MS Teams window
8. A search dialog for Jira Issues or a create issue dialog should appear.
9. You are able to fill in the form an submit the text to either an existing or a new Jira ticket

**Screenshots**


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux
 - Installation package rpm
 - Version 1.13.3

**Additional context**

issues.title: access to microsoft's echo bot
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem?**
In the old world of original Teams from Microsoft itself, there was an audio echo service available via settings, by which one could test audio. The bot would say a few sentences, then wait for the user to speak for a few seconds, then play back whatever was recorded. Access to the echo service would be welcome.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A means to reach Microsoft's echo service. For example, there doesn't seem to be a way to search for this pseudo-user.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Currently, the only way to test audio is to start a conversation with another human, which is rather an imposition for personal testing purposes.

issues.title: Actual teams-for-linux-<VERSION>.AppImage doesn't have any update information
issues.body:
Currently the package **teams-for-linux-<VERSION>.AppImage** doesn't have any update information.

For instance:
```
$ ./appimageupdatetool-x86_64.AppImage -j teams-for-linux-1.3.12.AppImage
Could not find update information in the AppImage. Please contact the author of the AppImage and ask them to embed update information.
Error checking for changes!

$ ./appimageupdatetool-x86_64.AppImage -d teams-for-linux-1.3.12.AppImage 
Parsing file: teams-for-linux-1.3.12.AppImage
AppImage type: 2
Raw update information: <empty>
Update information invalid: | not found
Failed to parse AppImage. See above for more information
```

issues.title: rpm %postun scriptlet fails
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Upgrading or re-installing the rpm produces the following messages:
```
Running transaction
  Preparing        :                                                                       1/1 
  Reinstalling     : teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64                                       1/2 
  Running scriptlet: teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64                                       1/2 
update-alternatives is /sbin/update-alternatives

  Cleanup          : teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64                                       2/2 
  Running scriptlet: teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64                                       2/2 
/usr/bin/teams-for-linux has not been configured as an alternative for teams-for-linux
warning: %postun(teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64) scriptlet failed, exit status 2

Error in POSTUN scriptlet in rpm package teams-for-linux
  Verifying        : teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64                                       1/2 
  Verifying        : teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64                                       2/2 

Reinstalled:
  teams-for-linux-1.3.11-1.x86_64
```

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. update or re-install teams-for-linux via rpm

**Expected behavior**
It's expected that routine rpm updates would not produce errors

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
 - Installation package: rpm
 - Version: 3.1.11

**Additional context**
The postuninstall scriptlet is
```
#!/bin/bash

# Delete the link to the binary
if type update-alternatives >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    update-alternatives --remove 'teams-for-linux' '/usr/bin/teams-for-linux'
else
    rm -f '/usr/bin/teams-for-linux'
fi
```
and the problem is in the `update-alternatives` command. The last parameter should be the path to the binary (`/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux`) rather than the path to the alternatives link (`/usr/bin/teams-for-linux`). That is, the command should be:
```
   update-alternatives --remove 'teams-for-linux' '/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux'
```
But this seems problematic, as it runs _after_ the install. It should include a check to see that `/usr/bin/teams-for-linux` doesn't resolve to an executable (`[ -e /usr/bin/teams-for-linux ]`). Otherwise it will remove the perfectly valid, just-installed link.

Finally, in the course of writing this up, I'm starting to wonder if this is a problem with Electron rather than something in `teams-for-linux` itself. If nothing else, at least this documents it.

Release Notes for v24.8.2

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where child windows opened when the parent window is already fullscreen did not respect the child windows' fullscreenability and resizability settings. #39643 (Also in 25, 26, 27)
  • Fixed an issue where the Node.js assert module did not work in the renderer process. #39621 (Also in 25, 26, 27)

Other Changes

  • Security: backported fix for CVE-2023-4427.

    • Security: backported fix for CVE-2023-4430. #39647
  • Security: backported fix for CVE-2023-4572. #39688

electron v24.8.1

Fixes

  • Fixed a potential crash when calling BrowserWindow.moveTop() on modal child windows. #39526 (Also in 25, 26)
  • Fixed decorations for tiled windows on Wayland. #39567 (Also in 22, 25, 26, 27)
  • Fixed to regenerate thumbnail toolbar buttons when explorer is restarted. #39585 (Also in 25, 26)

Other Changes

    • Security: backported fix for CVE-2023-4353.
    • Security: backported fix for CVE-2023-4352.
    • Security: backported fix for CVE-2023-4351. #39558

electron v24.8.0

Features

  • Added support for several more Node.js cli flags in the main process. #39372 (Also in 25, 26)

issues.title: Fix online check with HTTP proxy
issues.body:
Fixes #933. This PR changes `httpHelper` to use the [Electron `net`](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/net) module as a replacement for Node.js `http`/`https` modules. The `net` module offers transparent support for web proxies, whereas Node.js `http`/`https` module requires complicated code to correctly issue a request through a proxy.

With this change, the online check "works for me" (on startup and wakeup from sleep), both when directly connected to internet and when on a corporate network with mandatory use of a proxy for internet access. Note that the `net` module requires the application being ready before it becomes usable, so I've had to delay `downloadCustomBGServiceRemoteConfig()` a bit (in `app/index.js`, I've moved the call into the `ready` event handler).

This PR also removes the unused `checkConnectivity` module; this dead-code removal is not strictly necessary (it's just me tidying code when I started looking into this issue), so if you prefer a PR without this commit I can amend it.

issues.title: Network never detected, app does not start
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
New feature "waiting for network" does not give expected result in my environment. The application never starts and always hangs on a blank page showing "no internet connection" in the title bar. I tried multiple times and cleared storage without success. 

Of course, I made sure network was available prior to filling this issue which is the case: I can browse any external site from my browser including the teams web site and downgrading the app to 1.3.3 works as expected for now.


**To Reproduce**
1. Start the app (either with or without clearing storage)
2. Watch the title bar showing "waiting for network"
3. Wait until the title bar changes to "no internet connection"
4. The app stays frozen on a blank page and does not start

**Expected behavior**
The app detect network correctly and starts normally

**Screenshots**
N/A

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package deb (via apt channel)
 - Version 1.3.5

**Additional context**
Possibly interfering:
- I run a local squid proxy forwarding requests to my company proxy. It runs on a local IP on port 8080
- I have 2 network interfaces enabled on my machine (although leaving only one enabled did not change anything).

issues.title: Issue 918
Automatic logoff/disconnect alert or automatic reconnect
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Every 3 of hours (or so), I am logged out of or am disconnected from Teams, which is a company policy that I mentioned here: [teams refuses to authenticate at all sometimes](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/740#issuecomment-1594475216)
The company policy just plain s*cks, but it's a reasonably large organization, and it's not something I can change.

The problem I'm facing is: I don't know when I've been thrown offline. The result is that people are trying to contact me, but they can't, and when I'm trying to use Teams myself, I have to remember to check whether I'm still online. Which is something you _will_ forget at some point, and you think you sent someone a message (or whatever), but it didn't really happen.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I would like one of these implemented:
- An auto reconnect when I've been thrown offline, or
- An alert after I've been thrown, so I can deal with it myself.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

I can't think of a better solution or workaround or ... . I would prefer a (configurable?) auto reconnect, as it takes the burden of reconnecting from me. However, I'd settle for an alert when an auto reconnect is unfeasible.

issues.title: App Reloading During Active Call due to Brief Internet Disconnection
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

The app reloads during an active call if the internet connection goes down for just a second.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Initiate a call.
2. Intentionally disconnect the internet connection.
3. Swiftly restore the internet connection.
4. Microsoft Teams successfully re-establishes the call without any complications.
5. Unexpectedly, after 10 seconds the reload mechanism is activated, resulting in the WebApp being reloaded. This occurrence is particularly annoying as the brief disconnections I experience do not interrupt the ongoing call.
https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/tree/develop/app/onlineOffline

**Expected behavior**

No reload if there's an active call or improved reload mechanism. (I actually don't know why we need this at all?). I guess there's a valid reason.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 37
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version 1.3.0

issues.title: using TZ=":/etc/localtime" Teams is off by 1 hour
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Not a big thing, but if I set the TZ environment variable to 
```
% ls -l /etc/localtime 
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 23 Aug  8 06:57 /etc/localtime -> /usr/share/zoneinfo/CET
% export TZ=:/etc/localtime
% teams-for-linux
```
(which is an extension of the GNU C library, see https://www.gnu.org/software/libc/manual/html_node/TZ-Variable.html), then the clock shown in Teams is off by one hour. For CET I get 5:21 instead of 6:21 . GMT is 4.21. DST is on.

Obviously Teams doesn't understand $TZ. In this case it should fall back to system time zone (eg. CET) or GMT.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Debian 12
 - teams-for-linux 1.3.1 debian package
 - 1.3.1

I understand that this is super-low priority.

issues.title: Teams APT repository does not work well with apt-cacher-ng
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I am using apt-cacher-ng at home to cache package downloads from multiple other machines. Unfortunately, apt-cacher-ng serves the old package version to its clients when the package is updated in the new Teams repo. This does not happen for other repositories.

Running `apt update && apt full-upgrade` on a machine behind the cache now yields an error message like this:
```
Get:1 http://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian stable/main amd64 teams-for-linux amd64 1.2.6 [68.8 MB]
Err:1 http://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian stable/main amd64 teams-for-linux amd64 1.2.6
  File has unexpected size (68775814 != 68757582). Mirror sync in progress? [IP: 10.0.0.89 3142]
  Hashes of expected file:
   - SHA512:e94a5b303715cac5b783fa9dfb2b1d1a9937e56a2ba473fea30fca726c3d7fa69c539de7834d6d9d2caef55f39d8511524db2c6c53258ad0cfff43d17d1d3f18
   - SHA256:a666a8c506728b1e8efa606b8190e07225cada31435303029e716466148e29d6
   - SHA1:49cf157560dc97ae6a463052264c1858520d2599 [weak]
   - MD5Sum:2a8814ff4f5232b1acfe9bcd8887c1ae [weak]
   - Filesize:68757582 [weak]
E: Failed to fetch http://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian/pool/main/t/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux_amd64.deb  File has unexpected size (68775814 != 68757582). Mirror sync in progress? [IP: 10.0.0.89 3142]
   Hashes of expected file:
    - SHA512:e94a5b303715cac5b783fa9dfb2b1d1a9937e56a2ba473fea30fca726c3d7fa69c539de7834d6d9d2caef55f39d8511524db2c6c53258ad0cfff43d17d1d3f18
    - SHA256:a666a8c506728b1e8efa606b8190e07225cada31435303029e716466148e29d6
    - SHA1:49cf157560dc97ae6a463052264c1858520d2599 [weak]
    - MD5Sum:2a8814ff4f5232b1acfe9bcd8887c1ae [weak]
    - Filesize:68757582 [weak]
E: Unable to fetch some archives, maybe run apt-get update or try with --fix-missing?
```

This can be worked around by stopping apt-cacher-ng on the caching server, deleting the cached package there and then starting apt-cacher-ng again.

I think this might be caused by the way the package is stored in the Teams APT repository - the package is always named `teams-for-linux_amd64.deb`. Files in other APT repositories have package version in their name. This way the cache cannot serve the old package when the new one is requested.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install apt-cacher-ng `3.7.4-1build1`/`3.7.4-1build1` from Ubuntu 20.04/22.04 to a machine in your network
2. Configure a test machine to use that apt-cacher-ng instance for downloading APT packages
3. Add the `http://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian/` repo (HTTP instead of HTTPS) to APT sources list on the test machine
4. Install the `teams-for-linux` package on the test machine
5. Wait for new `teams-for-linux` package release
6. Try to update the package via `apt update && apt full-upgrade`. The upgrade will fail when it tries to download the updated package.

**Expected behavior**
Ideally, apt-cacher-ng would cache every package version separately. This would require having the packages in the upstream APT repo named like `teams-for-linux_1.2.5_amd64.deb` and `teams-for-linux_1.2.6_amd64.deb`.

**Environment**
Client: Xubuntu 22.04, teams-for-linux 1.2.5
APT caching server: Ubuntu 20.04, apt-cacher-ng package 3.3.1-2build1; also tried to update to 3.7.4-1build1, but that didn't help

**Additional context**
From a pure HTTP standpoint, it looks like the Teams repo server handles things correctly (`Last-Modified` and `Etag` headers). However, I'm assuming that apt-cacher-ng assumes that files under `/pool/` never change.

issues.title: Provide SHA256 hashes for all downloadable assets
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I like to validate a downloaded asset against its hash.
I am using a script that downloads the AppImage asset and automatically also (prior to version 1.2) downloads  the file `latest-linux.yml` which always contained the AppImage's SHA256 file hash.
This is not the case anymore. As of version 1.2, it seems,  `latest-linux.yml` contains various file hashes, e.g. for `deb` or for `rpm` but always only one - changing from release to release.

This situation leads to an invalid checksum check on my side as I am downloading the AppImage but trying to check its SHA256 hash aginst some other asset's hash..

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I am missing a file that contains all the file hashes.
* Maybe also a hash file per asset.

That would be great and would benefit security in general.
Also it would improve automaion like the script I am using.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Because the hash, users want to check against, needs to be provided by the creator of the asset I can only imagine ignoring the checksum check, which I would really like to avoid.

**Additional context**

issues.title: Token/auth requests get sent to external browser
issues.body:
Intermittent requests to elements within teams (files, tasks, etc.) trigger some additional authentication (likely getting a service specific token). SOMETIMES this seems to happen transparently, but others it winds up opening in external browser, which presents this error.

![image](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/828492/d9d9157c-6fbe-47a7-b177-61cc435195ef)

It seems like there is maybe some additional logic needed for 'when to open internally' vs 'when to open in external browser'.

issues.title: Newly-connected BT audio devices are not noticed
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
TfL does not notice new bluetooth devices, so they do not become available selections. That is, if I start TfL when my BT headset is off, then TfL knows only the built-in audio devices. When I later turn on and connect my headset, TfL takes no notice of this so that I cannot choose a new configuration using the headset.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start TfL without a BT device active.
2. Note configuration available in Calls audio device selection shows only built-in devices (as expected).
3. Connect BT headset.
4. No new configuration becomes available.

**Expected behavior**
I would like TfL to take note of available devices whenever the audio device selector becomes visible. A simple re-scan of available devices would be in order.

**Screenshots**
This is how things look, after restarting TfL due to having connected headset. Without restart, the "custom setup" element is not  available.
![screenshot1](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/assets/7548920/edcafbc7-b898-4110-ab48-816fa162815b)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Fedora 37.
 - teams-for-linux-1.1.6.x86_64.rpm

I've filed this as a bug because I think it's something that should always have been available, but if you want to re-class it as a feature request, that would be fine.

issues.title: Popout window for chats
issues.body:
**Problem statement**
When participating in a presentation, reading and answering to chat messages from other channels requires minimising the presentation, writing in the other chat, then maximising the presentation again. This single-window approach is tedious when there is a continuous message flow.

**Desired solution**
The old, official client allowed to double click a chat and have it pop out as a separate window. Replicating this behaviour would streamline multi-channel communication.

**Alternatives**
Being able to launch multiple teams-for-linux windows (not necessarily instances) would also solve this problem.
`/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --help` doesn't indicate this capability, and running it just raises the existing window.
Right-click & "New Window" in gnome-shell also just raises the existing window.

**Additional context**
I hope I'm missing some obvious way to get separate/popout windows.

issues.title: libuuid is missing on openSUSE when imstalling rpm
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
libuuid is missing on openSUSE

**To Reproduce**
1. Add teams-for-linux repo via zypper
2. Install teams-for-linux with `zypper in teams-for-linux`

**Expected behavior**
Package should be installed without errors.

**Screenshots**
```zypper in teams-for-linux
Loading repository data...
Reading installed packages...
Resolving package dependencies...

Problem: nothing provides 'libuuid' needed by the to be installed teams-for-linux-1.1.6-1.x86_64
 Solution 1: do not install teams-for-linux-1.1.6-1.x86_64
 Solution 2: break teams-for-linux-1.1.6-1.x86_64 by ignoring some of its dependencies
```

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: openSUSE Tumbleweed
 - Installation package: rpm
 - Version: 1.1.6

**Additional context**
It's safe to install as libuuid is present in the `libuuid1` package.
So it has to be changed to `libuuid1` in requirements for openSUSE (or simply removed, as libuuid is mandatory for the system)

issues.title: Restored appdata.xml
No sound of the shared window
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I can share the screen using the application, but the sound of the shared windows doesn't go through. The official version of the Teams app for Windows had a toggle for whether to transmit the screen sound.

For example, if I play a youtube video in a browser window and share that window, the participant can see the video but the audio of the video is not shared. This is also the case for sharing the whole screen. Am I missing something here? Are there some settings that need to be tweaked for this to work?

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to the application/software
2. Click on 'Start a meeting.'
3. Share a window or the whole screen with audio playback.
4. The meeting can be recorded to see the error, or the meeting participant can be asked if they can hear the audio.

**Expected behavior**
Typically, the audio should also be shared when sharing the screen. An ideal option is to include the toggle, like the official client, whether or not to transfer the screen audio.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux (Arch with KDE)
 - Installation package: AUR
 - Version: 1.1.1-1

issues.title: Commit Style and Merges
issues.body:
Not directly a bug but: the current style of commit messages is hard to read, especially with the Github WebUi as if you call a commit #<bugnumber> you can only can click on the issue but not the commit itself.
The next issue with this is that the message isn't that descriptive, yes the issue explains the original intent somewhat but issues aren't always resolved in one big commit and a commit message should be more detailed.

The second issue I have is that almost every PR gets a merge commit even when the PR just had one commit. Creating merge commits when they aren't needed makes the history harder to follow and requires to rebase the branch that the PR is inside to have a clean history when a branch is behind master/the HEAD of the target branch.

issues.title: Change user-agent string on different domains to bypass CA policies
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Previously it was possible to bypass CA policies by implementing a custom user agent string in the `config.json` file under the `chromeUserAgent` property. Recent changes have meant that agents that work in bypassing conditional access policies work to do that, however are unable to render the teams interface due to an unknown browser type (presumably due to the custom user agent). 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Currently the `config.json` file is configured as such 

```json 
{
...
"chromeUserAgent":"custom user agent here",
...
}
```

A solution would be to define user agents based on domain like so:

```json
{
...
"chromeUserAgents": {
  "_default_":"default user agent to use here or use app default if not present",
  "microsoftonline.com": "Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; U; Android 4.0.3; ko-kr; LG-L160L Build/IML74K) AppleWebkit/534.30 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Mobile Safari/534.30",
  "login.microsoftonline.com": "Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; U; Android 4.0.3; ko-kr; LG-L160L Build/IML74K) AppleWebkit/534.30 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Mobile Safari/534.30"
},
...
}
```

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The current work around is to define the custom UA, start teams, authenticate, pkill teams, remove UA, start teams.

issues.title: Allow custom URLs for loading custom backgrounds.
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
With the changes added to fix #591 you can now use custom background images to replace the background during video calls. However, the URL for loading custom images is hard coded to "http://localhost". This requires that you run a web server on every server that you use teams-for-linux and that the web server runs as root so that it can open port 80.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
At a minimum I'd like to be able to configure a port number other than 80 to run the web server on. That way the web server wouldn't have to run as root. Even better would be able to fully customize the URL so that I could run the web server wherever I wanted, possibly with TLS support and some sort of header/token authentication.

Extra credit would be the ability to load the custom_bg.json file from a web server as well.

Extra extra credit would be the ability to point to multiple custom_bg.json files around the web so that people could potentially share cool (openly licensed of course) backgrounds.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I've patched my local installation with a custom URL but it would be less fragile if this was a supported configuration option.

issues.title: Feature: Disable Notification Sound if not Available
issues.body:
This adds functionality to disable notification sounds if status is not "Available".

If the user is in a meeting/call, or has set their status to "Busy", "Away", etc. - basically anything other than "Available" - then notification sounds won't be played.

issues.title: Teams gets unloaded after suspend
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Whenever I raise my system from suspend the teams website gets unloaded: no notification badge, blank window -> a reload is required to use teams.
This behavior is confusing especially when I miss some important notifications because teams is not loaded and I don't recognize it.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open teams-for-linux
2. Hide it to the tray
3. Suspend your machine
4. Wake up your machine e.g. one day later
5. The teams app stops working
6. After reloading, everything backs to normal

**Expected behavior**
The app should reload teams on system resume (like Ferdium does), so no manual reload would be necessary.
Ferdium also allows a setting for idle time (default is 10 minutes).

Or another possible solution would be to somehow detect when teams is not loaded and automatically reload it if necessary.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch Linux x86_64
 - Installation package: AUR (tar.gz)
 - Version: 1.0.52

issues.title: feat: Disable hardware media key handling
issues.body: Fixes IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux#716

issues.title: Making calls grabs multimedia keys
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Making a call in Teams binds the multimedia keys (play/pause/etc) to Teams so that they no longer work with media applications (e.g. Spotify).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Open a media application (e.g. Spotify) and start playing music
2. Press the play/pause multimedia key to make sure the key works with the media application
3. Open Teams for Linux
4. Make a call in Teams
5. End the call
6. Press the play/pause multimedia key and observe that the key no longer has any effect on the media application

**Expected behavior**

Since Teams isn't a media application, it should have no impact on the multimedia keys

**Screenshots**

N/A

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 1.0.49

**Additional context**

Electron seems to bind to the multimedia keys by default.

It seems that this can be disabled relatively easily:

> ```js
> app.commandLine.appendSwitch('disable-features', 'HardwareMediaKeyHandling');
> ```

(https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/18253#issuecomment-589542934)

issues.title: Click on link to external meetings is ignored
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

I have some teams meetings that are scheduled by an external organisation. I accept the meeting in outlook and see it in teams calendar.
The entry has no "Join Meeting" Button.


Normal Teams meeting scheduled in our organisation:
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/186753/226926936-474ccaa7-0644-4ff5-9eaf-7f7489d2cf60.png)

External Teams meeting:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/186753/226927106-4a6574e1-2248-4dfb-9575-dcf3d35c8e80.png)

The external meeting has a link to the actual meeting:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/186753/226927347-e8736d54-89e3-4115-b25b-bc25598f457a.png)

When I click on the link, nothing happens. When I right-click the link and click "copy", nothing is copied (clipboard has still the previous content).

When I inspect the link, it looks like a normal a-href html link:

![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/186753/226927860-4f4eec27-5ead-464a-b9ba-0d62d2972891.png)

When I copy the link address, post it in teams to myself and then click the link in the message history, the meeting opens in teams-for-linux (so the link actually works, that's my daily workaround).

When I click the link in outlook or in teams web (office.com), it opens the browser (so the link in the invitation is in general clickable, just not in teams-for-linux).

**To Reproduce**

See above, reproducible for all my external meetings

**Expected behavior**

External meeting should open when clicking on the link.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: archlinux
 - Installation package: aur
 - Version 1.0.49

This is most likely not an issue of teams-for-linux but rather Microsoft's web client implementation. However maybe somebody has encountered the same thing and/or has even a workaround or fix?

This week mouseover actions on comments (emojis, the "..." submenu) have stopped working. The hover menu appears, but when I click on something, nothing happens.

I have experimented with this a bit, and it has turned out to only occur under only one window manager / desktop environment. I am using NsCDE (a retroish setup based on fvwm). I have tried running the applicatin with plain fvwm, gnome, WindowMaker and plasma, everything's fine there.

I haven't knowingly made any changes to my setup, it just "stopped working", and this across reboots, logout/login, restart of the application. Really weird.

issues.title: Patch: Support for multiple Teams Accounts at the same time
issues.body:
I'm always frustrated when I have to use my three different Teams Instances and managing them is quite difficult.
I usually have my team instances managed via teams-for-linux, one with my browser, and one with the incognito tab of my browser.
With the provided code one can run multiple teams-for-linux Instances at the same time and only has to create a different .desktop file for each of them.

issues.title: Patch: Support for multiple Teams Accounts at the same time
Add option to load client certificates for corporate auth
issues.body:
Example in `~/.config/teams-for-linux/config.json`:

```
{
    "clientCertPath": "/path/to/my-certificate.p12",
    "clientCertPassword": "my-password"
}
```

This will use `my-certificate.p12` for corporate authentication, allowing copy/paste, attachments management and possibly other blocked features.

issues.title: Screen sharing on Tumbleweed does not work
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Screen sharing does not work on Tumbleweed.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start meeting.
2. Share screen (FHD, full screen)
3. No screen is shared

**Expected behavior**
Screen sharing should be initiated..

**Screenshots**
Not applicable.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: OpenSUSE Tumbleweed 20230226
 - Installation package: rpm, courtesy of Thaodan
 - Version: teams-for-linux-1.0.47-51.1.noarch
 -  CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-3740QM CPU @ 2.70GHz

**Additional context**
1. When starting from command line, the following warning/error is displayed:
christo@lenovo-t530:~> MESA-INTEL: warning: Ivy Bridge Vulkan support is incomplete
failed to open /usr/lib64/dri/hybrid_drv_video.so
Not using hybrid_drv_video.so
[978:0228/160044.142008:ERROR:nss_util.cc(349)] After loading Root Certs, loaded==false: NSS error code: -8018

2. When starting screen shared, debug shows the following:
0.2-angular-jquery.min-14969ec.js:114 2023-02-27T09:31:53.829Z [screenSharing][control] screenSharingControlService: unexpected, cannot find the call, teamsCallId [1]

0.2-angular-jquery.min-14969ec.js:114 2023-02-27T09:31:56.629Z callingAgents: calling-stack [JS.TsCalling.SignalingAgent] ffffffff: CSA[24634c42-1821-4d36-a0c5-93f7d8d9ddbf][Connected]/Broker _handleSubscriptionFailure failed, re-subscribing, {"response":16,"status":498,"statusText":"RequestAborted"}

3. Screen sharing works with Edge PWA, but not with Chromium PWA.  Might be related?

issues.title: MacOS Apple Silicon build
issues.body: **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** Currently launching the app on MacOS m1pro/max processors we experience super slow app startup. Teams-for-linux helper renderer eats out 130% of CPU on start. On tapping chats/selecting team/opening messages or traversing through menus - it does that super slow and renderer loads CPU up to 100% and more **Describe the solution you'd like** Is it possible to make m1 apple silicon optimized build please? Screenshots:

issues.title: Mac keyboard shortcuts are shown
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

I hesitate to mention this because it's so minor, and something I can personally live without, but in the interest of making this more polished for other users I thought I'd report it ...

It looks like the MacOS keyboard shortcuts are being shown instead of the Linux ones. Maybe it's due to whatever browser user agent is being used?

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Start a meeting
2. Mute yourself
3. Blow into the microphone
4. Observe that the message that pops up shows the MacOS keyboard shortcuts

**Expected behavior**

I'd expect Linux (or I guess Windows would be fine too?) keyboard shorctuts

**Screenshots**

![mac-keyboard-shortcuts](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/346123/220422829-2e4bc45b-4fe1-4717-b4ab-887f000b8c3d.png)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version 1.0.49

**Additional context**

Thanks!

issues.title: Back/forward navigation buttons
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

No

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Hi,

The official Teams app for Linux has back/forward buttons that are very useful navigating back and forth quickly between chats. Would it be possible to add them?

Thanks!

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

For the time being, I'm able to use the back/forward navigation buttons on my mouse, but I don't have those with every mouse I use.

**Additional context**

This is what they look like in the official Teams Linux app (I don't really care what they look like, just thought a screenshot would be helpful):

![teams-navigation-buttons](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/346123/220420052-3eeba71f-55d2-450e-a4ea-2a7b25eabf4f.png)

issues.title: Flatpak Out Of Date (1.0.45 in flathub, 1.0.49 latest release)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
In the official flathub remote of Flatpak, **teams-for-linux** is out of date.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install flatpak (instructions vary per distro)
2. Add flathub to flatpak if not already: `flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo`
3. Search for **teams-for-linux**: `flatpak search teams-for-linux`
4. Observe the version is **1.0.45** at time of this report

**Expected behavior**
The version in the output of `flatpak search teams-for-linux` should be **1.0.49** at time of report.

**Screenshots**
![teams-for-linux-search](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/284931/219795685-bc3c30bd-ecaf-4d54-8918-0015cdb0deed.png)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch Linux

issues.title: Use CSD and disable desktop window
issues.body:
At the moment teams-for-linux looks like this.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/572924/218971226-7c6eee8c-0de0-4a1d-94f8-f6f71aa38289.png)

This wastes a lot of space for a window frame that doesn't add anything useful.

However, the official Microsoft Teams client has this window.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/572924/218970858-fbb31318-b091-48ea-9bc7-558ed9586a73.png)

It would be very interesting to support (at least as an option) to be able to use CSD instead of having a system window that wastes space.

Is it somehow possible to emit the current presence state via dbus? It would be awesome to use this and maybe other events (calender notifications, ...)
my current usecase is to update the state of a connected USB busylight - but dbus is also a standard way to send Desktop Notifications.

issues.title: Call for help: No longer able to open meeting invitations in app
issues.body:
It looks like Microsoft have changed their teams.microsoft.com site's behaviour: while before, when opening a meeting invitation in a browser one would get prompted to either open in the browser or in the app, the site now immediately redirects into the teams webapp in the browser, no way to open the meeting in the app. 

![teams-screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/383271/216048308-97bb1ef0-d544-4793-bff0-7af949aff917.png)

The above screenshot shows what I can see for a few milliseconds before the redirect takes place (and nothing on the right side is linked to anything). The native dialogue asking me which app I want to open the link with no longer appears.

All my Linux-using colleagues have had the same problem since this morning.

This is of course not a problem of teams-for-linux, but since MS may not be willing to change this (annoying) behaviour, I'd like to ask around whether anyone has found a workaround for this?

[edit] This behaviour is triggered by a "Linux" in the user agent.

issues.title: OpenSUSE Buildservice webhook
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

No. I want to provide packaging based on the OpenSUSE buildservice. To make updating the package automatically we can add a webhook to trigger the service when ever you tag a new release.

The service can be used for any distro as long as packaging is provided e.g. Debian, Arch Linux etc.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add openSUSE webhook to this repository. I would setup anything else but that you have to add the url to this repository.
If possible it would make sense to store the spec files in this repository too.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manual updates 

**Additional context**
OpenSUSE docs:
https://openbuildservice.org/help/manuals/obs-user-guide/cha.obs.scm_ci_workflow_integration.html

**Describe the bug**
When I'm sharing screens or windows in Teams, sometimes it works perfectly and sometime it crashes immediately with a segfault. Usually, from fresh start of Teams, I can screen share without any troubles and then when I try to share again after stopping the first one, it crashes.

**To Reproduce**
1. Make a call
2. Start sharing a screen or a window
3. Stop sharing
4. Start sharing again

**Expected behavior**
A stable screen sharing feature that works everytime.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04.1
 - Installation package deb
 - Windowing system: Wayland

**Additional context**
dmesg:
`traps: teams-for-linux[262696] general protection fault ip:7f0f55425014 sp:7fff822b1cd8 error:0 in libwayland-client.so.0.20.0[7f0f55423000+6000]`

Thanks

issues.title: Add electron spell checker option
issues.body: As mentioned in [this issue](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/385#issuecomment-1378521745), I've added a new flag/option named `spellCheckerLanguages` with type array. This array will contain the language codes that we want to use for the spell checking offered by Electron. This is enabled only if the option is passed when running teams-for-linux.

issues.title: Minimized feature
issues.body: This commit aims to fix minized feature adding call to "window.hide()" during startup

issues.title: Option to set a custom tray icon
issues.body:
First thanks for the app - really useful. 

I am using teams for two organisations for which teams is really bad as I can only be logged in to one organisation at a time which means that I am only getting notifications for one organisation and I need to always authenticate  when I switch. So my idea is to use two instance of this app (simply installing one with apt and one with snap) and use two different tray icons but I couldn't figure out a convenient way to do this. With `apt` I tried replacing all the images with: `sudo cat /var/lib/dpkg/info/teams-for-linux.list | grep png | sudo xargs -n1 cp ~/Downloads/my-icon.png` but in the tray still the default icon has been used.  The only way which worked for me was building it my self and replacing the icons in the `assets` dir...

issues.title: Have a starting argument to switch to the old Teams UI
issues.body:
When in a meeting, Teams used to show the following floating box:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38435343/208399640-b9ab98e9-5a77-40f1-b104-e2b148603eb9.png)

which, not only would disappear when there was no mouse activity, it also had an option to have a true full screen (that is, without borders, bars, etc.)

At some point, Microsoft switched to a new UI which is now unable to do a true full screen. The following bar is always at the top:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38435343/208400122-a0eef9e7-d428-4da2-b499-453668ff04c9.png)

and there's also a bar at the left side of the screen. In other words, the screen other participants are sharing is always resized to a smaller size to make room for the permanent bars.

The official Linux Teams still uses the old UI, and the old UI also was used in teams-for-linux up until 2 months ago. 

I'm wondering if there's some option that teams-for-linux can pass to the server in order to load the old UI, which I prefer a lot more.

issues.title: Uploading a file from a non root location fails
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Whenever I try to upload a file not on the root drive, it fails once it gets roughly halfway. File upload works fine if I upload a file from the root system drive.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to a direct message.
2. Find a file that isn't on the system root drive and try to upload it.
3. It should fail with "Something went wrong while sharing. Let's try again."

**Expected behavior**
The file should upload, show up in the "Files" tab, and be accessible to whomever I send it to.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.04
 - Installation package: snap store
 - Version 1.0.47

**Additional context**
I assume this is some quirk with snaps since they're normally containerized. I will have to install the "deb" version and see if I can reproduce the issue there.

issues.title: Devices submenu in Settings
issues.body:
In the Settings menu, the Devices submenu is missing. While in a call, I can access/set the device settings, it would be nice to set these correct without actually needing to make a call. The "old" linux client had this present, together with the "secondary ringer" option. The current web interface on gnome does not though ...
So, if at all possible: bring back the Devices menu (and the secondary ringer option).

issues.title: Snap package often asks for changing "default uri scheme handler"
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
On nearly every startup (at least once a day) the app asks to change the default uri scheme handler (msteams) to teams app

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
- Install the app from snap
- Open the app

**Expected behavior**
The app should start without the question / dialog box once the question was accepted

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Ubuntu 20.04.1
 - Installation package: Snap
 - Version: 1.0.45

issues.title: `Realtime AudioW` trap int 3 (complete crash, needs restart of program)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Randomly(?) Teams decides to close itself, requiring a full restart of the program and a full re-login. The logs reveal a signal trap causing the crash.

**To Reproduce**
Inconclusive. I've been sitting in a meeting for a long time and experienced multiple crashes. The issue started happening when Teams's UI changed to the newer style (in synch with what Windows people are having) where the menu is on the top. As long as the menu was in the middle of the meeting screen (old-style UI), these crashes were not prevalent.

**Expected behaviour**
No crashes, or at least a meaningful log.

**Screenshots**
```
Dec  2 10:57:27 hostname kernel: [ 8209.886646] traps: Realtime AudioW[8627] trap int3 ip:55c4ffd1cd3f sp:7f407ffd9a90 error:0 in teams-for-linux[55c4fc83c000+7187000]
Dec  2 12:46:38 hostname kernel: [14761.026328] traps: Realtime AudioW[22448] trap int3 ip:564d7c9bfd3f sp:7fea90f9caa0 error:0 in teams-for-linux[564d794df000+7187000]
---- reboot ----
Dec  1 10:27:01 hostname kernel: [ 9657.947124] traps: Realtime AudioW[38730] trap int3 ip:55a88f3e7d3f sp:7f660c91fa90 error:0 in teams-for-linux[55a88bf07000+7187000]
Dec  1 12:28:03 hostname kernel: [16919.336019] traps: Realtime AudioW[55243] trap int3 ip:556f3528ed3f sp:7fa845e2da90 error:0 in teams-for-linux[556f31dae000+7187000]
Dec  1 14:46:15 hostname kernel: [25212.058606] traps: Realtime AudioW[102887] trap int3 ip:55703603dd3f sp:7f3f7f0b3a90 error:0 in teams-for-linux[557032b5d000+7187000]
```

In every case, the symbol offset is the same, so I'd guess the issue is in the built binary. The ip and sp registers and the map location of the executable are different, but that is very likely due to ASLR.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 20.04.5 LTS "Focal Fossa" (with KDE)
 - Installation package: [DEB from GitHub releases page](http://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/tag/v1.0.45)
 - Version: [1.0.45](http://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/tag/v1.0.45)

**Additional context**
I have a wireless headset that is mounted with the A2DP (audio sink) profile, i.e. it only acts as a speaker. (Instead of HDP, where mono audio is connected in both directions with the wireless device.) For a microphone, I use a USB microphone.

Could you please advise what logs I could gather and from where to help debug this?

issues.title: Pen support for OneNote under Wayland
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When run with flags to enable Wayland, pen input is not registered.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start the app with `flatpak run com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux --enable-features=UseOzonePlatform --ozone-platform=wayland --enable-features=WebRTCPipeWireCapturer`
2. Open a OneNote notebook.
3. Try to draw something with a pen. There is no input.

**Expected behavior**
Proper pen support with pressure sensitivity and a functional eraser.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 22.10
 - Installation package: flatpak
 - Version: 1.0.45

**Additional context**
Without the Wayland flags pen input works, but there is no pressure sensitivity and both the eraser and the secondary button do not work. The pen is fully functional in other apps, e.g., in Xournal++.

issues.title: Bright flash when starting the app even with dark theme enabled
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When I start the Teams, it starts loading with full white background, which kills eyes. This might probably considered both as a bug and as a feature improvements. The problem should be in one out of three places (or in several places): Teams code itself, Teams-for-Linux and/or Electron. Either way, doesn't matter where the bug is originating, I think this can be fixed with a CSS styling or blocking of showing any content before the app fully loads a page (I would be happy with both approaches).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set up teams-for-linux in dark mode.
2. Fully close teams.
3. Open teams-for-linux.
4. See bright flash with fully white background for 5 seconds (sometimes more) while app loads the resources etc.

**Expected behavior**
Dark theme should be dark at all times.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/575925/198957846-43ca2564-6bc5-46f6-911a-4a131664ba6d.png)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Garuda Linux Soaring Talon Linux rog-strix 6.0.5-arch1-g14-1
 - Installation package: teams-for-linux from AUR: https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/teams-for-linux
 - Version: 1.0.45-1

**Additional context**
First, fully white background appears for about 2 seconds. Second, Microsoft logo appears for about 2 seconds as well, also on fully white background. Ideally make them both black or remove both of them while app loads.

issues.title: upgrade to electron 21.0.1 breaks app
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
On my gentoo system, 1.0.39 is the latest working official release. Any subsequent release will launch, show the initial popup, perform the login (if required) and then will close without any error message. This happens even after clearing not only the cache but the entire .config/teams-for-linux folder.
A git bisect shows that the "culprit" is the upgrade of electron. After ending the bisect and restoring (only) the electron dependency to 20.3.0 (latest currently available 20.x) on top of the latest commit in develop branch, the app starts working again (via yarn start).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Launch app
2. (Login, if token has expired)
3. App closes

**Screenshots**
luca@E0557546 ~/Documents/external/teams-for-linux $ git bisect bad
7a8f3b85b822d9a5f6f2e2a4d00d3f79ecad1412 is the first bad commit
commit 7a8f3b85b822d9a5f6f2e2a4d00d3f79ecad1412
Author: Jijo Joseph <jijojosephk@hotmail.com>
Date:   Thu Sep 29 01:16:19 2022 +0530

    Electron version upgrade to 21.0.x

 package.json |  4 ++--
 yarn.lock    | 20 ++++++++++----------
 2 files changed, 12 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
luca@E0557546 ~/Documents/external/teams-for-linux $

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux, gentoo, 64bit
 - Installation package: bug experienced via AppImage, reproduced via source, then bisected

issues.title: Prevent screensaver while in a call
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When I'm in a call, I often do not move the mouse or tap the keyboard for a long time. After 5 minutes (OS setting), the screensaver kicks in, which is annoying.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Prevent screensaver/standby while in a call.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually moving my mouse, but that's not a practical solution.

**Additional context**
It is working in the official Teams client.

issues.title: Option closeAppOnCross not working
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The config option **closeAppOnCross** is not working.

OS: Fedora Linux 36 (Workstation Edition) x86_64  
Kernel: 5.19.10-200.fc36.x86_64  
DE: GNOME 42.5  

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Tried
```bash 
teams-for-linux --closeAppOnCross true
teams-for-linux --closeAppOnCross=true
teams-for-linux --closeAppOnCross="true"
```
Also tried by creating a "~/.config/teams-for-linux/config.json" with:
```json
{
    "closeAppOnCross": true
}
```

3. Click on 'X' to close Teams or "Teams for Linux > Quit"

4. See process still running:
``` bash
ps auxf | grep teams
``` 

**Expected behavior**
Teams should quit and no teams process should be running after clicking on "X" or "Quitting" the app.

**Screenshots**
The same app process is there after the app is started and is closed/quit.
![Screenshot from 2022-09-26 19-05-28](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8213310/192238112-e6b13495-cc16-4140-bcec-50199d66139c.png)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 36
 - Installation package [ rpm ]
 - Version [ 1.0.38 ]

**Additional context**
Gnome 42.5 has no "tray" and enabling it to be able to quit is just not an acceptable solution.

**Workaround**
Forcefully kill the app
```bash
pkill -f teams-for-linux 
```

issues.title: Screensharing does not work on Sway / wlroots with Flatpak installation
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Screensharing does not work on the Flatpak installation with Sway 1.7 (more specifically xdg-desktop-portal-wlr 0.6).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install a Sway desktop environment with xdg-desktop-portal-wlr..
2. Install teams_for_linux flatpak
3. Run, start meeting, try to share screen
4. Nothing happens, error messages fill the terminal log.

**Expected behavior**
On screenshare, the desktop portal prompts for the desktop to share, then the desktop is shared.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch Linux, Sway 1.7, xdg-desktop-portal-wlr 0.6, Flatpak 1.14.
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version 1.0.35 and updated 1.0.36

**Additional context**

 The error is a replica of [this issue](https://github.com/emersion/xdg-desktop-portal-wlr/issues/227). The cause is that a newer version of Pipewire is needed than the one in the Freedesktop 21.08 platform. Bumping to Freedesktop 22.08 fixes the issue, but causes problems elsewhere, as reported in #584. An alternative might be to keep Freedesktop 21.08 but bundle Pipewire 3.40 (as done by e.g. OBS Studio). However, this may well cause the same problem as #584.

(This bug report was requested in #584)
