issues.title: No window decorations when running in Wayland
issues.body:
I noticed earlier today that by default, Teams seemed to be running in xwayland. I modified the command line to have the same flags as what I use for, ex, VSCode. Unusually, while VSCode gets a title bar, Teams will not.

I'm starting Teams using: `/usr/bin/flatpak run --branch=stable --arch=x86_64 --command=teams-for-linux --file-forwarding com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux --enable-features=WaylandWindowDecorations --ozone-platform-hint=auto` which works for VSCode and Signal perfectly well. It's not the biggest deal in the world, I can still move the window around with Super+LMB, though I can't resize the window at all.

My system is running Fedora 36 on Gnome 42.4

issues.title: Allow enable-features on commandline
issues.body:
Electron 20 seems to work quite well in native wayland mode. Unfortunately the electron process only allows the enable-features switch to be supplied once and since the app is appending this switch itself (when running on wayland) it overrides the switch the user has specified on the command line. This change allows any user specified features to be appended with those added by the app.

With this patch I can run with "--ozone-platform=wayland --enable-features=WaylandWindowDecorations" to run in native wayland mode and it seems to work pretty well for me (on Fedora/Gnome).

issues.title: feature: option to hide show with a left click on the tray icon
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

I usually only use the tray menu to hide and show teams. Currently a left or right click will get this same result, and I can find the hide and show there. Although in practice I'm more likely to use my window manager short-cuts as this is a bit cumbersome.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

With an option (probably) I'd like to make a left click on the tray icon click toggle teams visibility, instead of showing the menu.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

There's plenty of alternatives, including just using the power of my window manager. This is simply a nice piece of potential polish. If there's some other alternative I'd be interested to hear it.

**Additional context**

I'd just say this is purely extra shine, really this isn't a burning need so I don't mind if this idea goes on the rubbish tip. However I do think this would be a pretty snazzy option, and would give a bit more power rather than left and right click having the same effect.

issues.title: Leaks X11 client connections, eventually causing screen sharing failure
issues.body:
I'm on X11 and screen sharing works well in general. However, every time I start and stop screen sharing, an X client connection is leaked. You can verify it with
`lsof +c0 /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libX11.so 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f1  | grep teams-for-linux  | wc -l`
(credits go [here](https://bbs.archlinux.org/viewtopic.php?pid=1611548#p1611548)). The connections counted with `wc -l` increases with every screen sharing start.

There may be further "leaks", because right after starting teams-for-linux, there are already 11 client connections by it, which is unfortunate, because I sometimes even hit the hardcoded MAXCLIENTS limit of Xorg. In this case I see the following in the output of teams-for-linux, when trying to start screen sharing:
`Maximum number of clients reached[2:0525/093349.717641:ERROR:shared_x_display.cc(38)] Unable to open display`

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start teams-for-linux
2. In a terminal, run `lsof +c0 /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libX11.so 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f1  | grep teams-for-linux  | wc -l`
3. Be surprised about the high number being reported (should ideally be close to 1)
4. Start screen sharing
5. Run lsof again, number will have increased by 1
7. Stop screen sharing
8. Run lsof again, number is still initial + 1

**Expected behavior**
The number of X11 client connections should ideally close to 1, but at least not increase over time.

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Debian sid, KDE
 - Installation package: AppImage, used with firejail
 - Version: 1.0.24

Thanks a lot for providing this! :heart:

issues.title: Unable to share screen while on Fedora Silverblue 36 with wayland session

While using Fedora Silverblue 35, I was able to share my screen or window without issues. Since rebasing to Fedora Silverblue 36, still with Wayland, I can't, but with X11 session it is possible.

There a similar issue, I think, with the [Ubuntu distribution.](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/526)

 - OS: Fedora Silverblue
 - Installation package: Flatpak 
 - Version: 1.0.24

issues.title: spellChecker issue / request
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
spellcheck option not working:
[](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5880102/158060677-b5eb0e0c-ff70-4703-85a5-383e6b4187a4.png)program version:
teams-for-linux-1.0.24.AppImage

command line arguments:
"--rightClickWithSpellcheck=true"

configFile = { closeAppOnCross: false, rightClickWithSpellcheck: true }

With this configuration, right click does nothing.
_
Other test scenario:
program version:
teams-for-linux-1.0.24.AppImage

command line arguments:
"--rightClickWithSpellcheck=false"

configFile = { closeAppOnCross: false, rightClickWithSpellcheck: false }

Result: same behaviour, right click does nothing .
Also MS Teams options doesn't allow activate / deactivate spell check:
_
I know for sure the config file is being read because of debug info on terminal, and because "closeAppOnCross" works properly.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Enable / disable spell check option.

**Additional context**
In the index.js
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5880102/162574561-c75a5b36-852c-409b-995b-41404ccb9e8f.png)
Problem is that it could be better worded:
- enableSpellCheck
and
- enableRightClick
would be the options.
_
And in this part, an if should be added to isMisspelled:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5880102/162574601-451a8930-83fb-46e6-b777-4a23886e7521.png)
if (!spellcheckenabled) {
    return false;
}

issues.title: teams crashes at startup on newest sway (wayland) compositor
issues.body:
When starting teams-for-linux with --ozone-platform=wayland with sway 1.7, it doesn't start, with this error message:
  INFO: Running under Wayland, switching to PipeWire...
  interface 'wl_output' has no event 4
  Trace/breakpoint trap

This is due to a bug with electron, fixed upstream, where it binds to whatever is the newest wayland interface available, rather than the latest one it knows how to use. The fix backporting issue is ://github.com/electron/electron/issues/32487 and hasn't been backported yet, as far as I can tell, but hopefully will be soon.

issues.title: Teams often segfaults on native Wayland (electron#32436)
issues.body:
This is technically an [Electron bug](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/32436) that's already resolved upstream (fix available in Electron 17), but I've been wishing to open up an issue about it for anyone else stumbling upon it.

Basically, there's a race condition related to certain leftover X11 code that causes the app to crash while running as a native Wayland client.

It happens mostly on start, therefore it may segfault twice or thrice before actually starting succesfully, but from then on it mostly works.
I say mostly as certain ui interactions still cause it to segfault every now and then, but it's pretty uncommon and either way it's most likely all due to that upstream issue.

I just spam restart it till it pushes through, but for anyone else experiencing it that's less patient about it, using Teams through Xwayland should work fine until there's an Electron 17 update.

issues.title: Unable to install binary release rpm on openSUSE Tumbleweed where nothing provides 'libuuid'
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
teams-for-linux asks for a `libuuid` dependency while this package is named `libuuid1` on openSUSE, which makes it harder to install there

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
```bash
wget https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/download/v1.0.24/teams-for-linux-1.0.24.x86_64.rpm
sudo zypper in ./teams-for-linux-1.0.24.x86_64.rpm
```

**Expected behavior**
rpm package is successfully installed on openSUSE, without any override

**Screenshots**

Output for `sudo zypper in ./teams-for-linux-1.0.24.x86_64.rpm`
```
[sudo] password for root: 
Retrieving repository 'Packman Repository' metadata ....................................................................................................................................................................................................................[done]
Building repository 'Packman Repository' cache .........................................................................................................................................................................................................................[done]
Loading repository data...
Reading installed packages...
Resolving package dependencies...

Problem: nothing provides 'libuuid' needed by the to be installed teams-for-linux-1.0.24-1.x86_64
 Solution 1: do not install teams-for-linux-1.0.24-1.x86_64
 Solution 2: break teams-for-linux-1.0.24-1.x86_64 by ignoring some of its dependencies

Choose from above solutions by number or cancel [1/2/c/d/?] (c):
```

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux openSUSE Tumbleweed
 - Installation package rpm
 - Version 1.0.24

**Additional context**
https://github.com/BoostIO/BoostNote-App/issues/1063 is the same issue for another electron app fixed in https://github.com/BoostIO/BoostNote-App/pull/1228, while https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-installer-redhat/pull/134 seems to be a proper fix upstream, though I couldn't find any reference to electron-installer-redhat in teams-for-linux's yarn.lock.

Forcing install like the following works (please note the lack of signature, but that would be another issue) and teams-for-linux runs fine as libuuid1 is installed
```
sudo zypper in ./teams-for-linux-1.0.24.x86_64.rpm 
Loading repository data...

issues.title: Black window on wayland
issues.body:
Fedora 35 (Nvidia driver 510.47)
Installed from flatpak, version 1.0.23
When launched in Gnome Wayland session, only a black window is visible.
Application works normally in Gnome Xorg.
Application works in Wayland when started from command line with "--no-sandbox" argument.

issues.title: Background effects possible in Chrome
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
In Chrome/Chromium (not tested with other browsers) it's possible to change background effects (blur/pictures/...) since a time. Would it be possible to add the same functionality to this client?

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Clicking on the three dots it should show the show background effects menu.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The feature is working with chrome and using teams in web browser

issues.title: Fix: #505 , removed extra logic which filters list of available resolutions
issues.body: removed extra logic which filters list of available resolutions based on the primary monitor.

issues.title: Issue 506
issues.body: Reverted changes for 506 which did not work.

issues.title: Electron v16 update
issues.body:
Bump electron to v16
- Refactor calls to desktopCapturer.getSources since this is now [deprecated in the renderer](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/breaking-changes#deprecated-desktopcapturergetsources-in-the-renderer) and will be removed in v17
- Bumped user agent to chrome 96 to match electron version.
- Added use-fake-ui-for-media-stream flag on wayland to fix permission error I was getting during screen share (didn't seem to be an issue until v16).

For me this seems to enable virtual/blured background support which is nice to have. It may just be the user agent bump that did this.

Also this partially resolves bug #504. With electron v16 you can launch with --ozone-platform=wayland and the main window loads  but you end up with no titlebar/decorations. This seems to be an outstanding [electron issue](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/27016). A work around would be to implement server side decorations similar to the official teams app (#441).

issues.title: Electron16 update
issues.body:
Bump electron to v16
- Refactor calls to desktopCapturer.getSources since this is now [deprecated in the renderer](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/breaking-changes#deprecated-desktopcapturergetsources-in-the-renderer) and will be removed in v17
- Bumped user agent to chrome 96 to match electron version.
- Added use-fake-ui-for-media-stream flag on wayland to fix permission error I was getting during screen share (didn't seem to be an issue until v16).

For me this seems to enable virtual/blured background support which is nice to have. It may just be the user agent bump that did this.

Also this partially resolves bug #504. With electron v16 you can launch with --ozone-platform=wayland and the main windows loads  but you end up with no titlebar/decorations. This seems to be an outstanding [electron issue](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/27016). A work around would be to implement server side decorations similar to the official teams app (#441).

issues.title: Wayland flags, bypass streamSelector on wayland
issues.body:
Fix Wayland flags to allow pipewire capture.

Bypass streamSelector on wayland to avoid spamming user with multiple requests to select a source stream (native pipewire dialog already allows for stream selection).

issues.title: Wayland: Fix the Wayland specific runtime flags. Fixes #455.
issues.body: The Wayland specific runtime flags are separated by a semicolon rather than the required comma. This PR fixes that. Furthermore, fixing the feature flags also resulted in the Wayland/Ozone backend actually being picked up. However, for me Teams would remain completely blank, and for another user (https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/455#issuecomment-1007458946) the title bar was gone preventing you from resizing or moving the window. Removing those flags for now.

issues.title: docs(readme): add link to pacstall package
issues.body: Add link to pacstall package in README.MD as discussed in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/510

issues.title: Request for Pacstall Support
issues.body:
[Pacstall](https://github.com/pacstall/pacstall) is a community-driven AUR-like package manager for Ubuntu. We have an ongoing [pull request](https://github.com/pacstall/pacstall-programs/pull/603) to add teams-for-linux to our repository.

Would you like to maintain the pacscript (similar to a PKGBUILD) yourself? We can maintain it for you if you decide not to.

issues.title: Does not terminate related processes after closing app
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
After clicking x for closing the app, the related processes continue running. The official app terminates all related processes correctly.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Click on close window to terminate, and not go to background.
2. open system monitor and search for teams
3. wonder why the processes are still there!

**Expected behavior**
After closing the window the processes should terminate, and no go to the background, or at least have the possibility to choose it like the official app does, in settings

**Screenshots**
No applicable

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 35 - Gnome
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version: 1.0.20

**Additional context**
The option to terminate all related processes running seems to be missing from settings. Is this by design?

issues.title: Cannot share screen nor window with snap
issues.body:
I have been using the snap in the past successfully. For a while I was using the official client, but yesterday I switched back.

**Describe the bug**

It seems somewhere along the road screen sharing got broken. 

I cannot share a screen or window. Once it fails to share something I cannot reactive the camera.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start a meeting
2. Activate camera (if not done automatically)
3. Open the dialog for screen sharing
4. Select a screen or window (does not matter which one) to share
4. Camera gets disabled by Teams
5. Problem: Screen/window is not shared
6. Problem: When going back to step 3, step 4 does not offer a choice which screen/window to share
7. Problem: Clicking on the Camera button does not re-enable the camera

**Expected behavior**

* The chosen screen/window gets shared
* Sharing can be stopped
* Camera can be reactivated

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 20.04.3 LTS

issues.title: Popup when opening application: Allow snap "teams-for-linux" to change "default-url-scheme-handler" subproperty "msteams" to "teams-for-linux_teams-for-linux.desktop"
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Every time we open this snap on Ubuntu 20.04, a popup appears with the following message:

> Allow settings change?  
> Allow snap "teams-for-linux" to change "default-url-scheme-handler" subproperty "msteams" to "teams-for-linux_teams-for-linux.desktop"

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. `snap install teams-for-linux`
2. open Teams

**Expected behavior**
Could prompt for that window, but only once

**Screenshots**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1264761/138115711-85be4bc6-2acc-4a55-aec5-b47e9488bd3b.png)

**Desktop:**
 - OS: Ubuntu 20.04
 - Installation package: `snap`
 - Version: `1.0.10`

**Additional context**
This was also asked here:  
https://askubuntu.com/questions/1334691/how-to-prevent-microsoft-teams-allow-settings-change-dialog-on-startup

The provided solution is to use `teams` or `teams-insiders` snaps released by `msteam`. 

I don't expect this to really get fixed since Readme says:

> This project is in maintenance mode only.

I only created this issue to document this bug and bring visibility :)

issues.title: Bump electron from 13.1.2 to 13.3.0
issues.body:
Bumps [electron](https://github.com/electron/electron) from 13.1.2 to 13.3.0.
Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v13.3.0

Release Notes for v13.3.0

Other Changes

  • Security: Backported fix for 1228036. #30639
  • Security: Backported fix for 1231134. #30637 (Also in 11)
  • Security: Backported fix for 1233564. #30636 (Also in 11)
  • Security: Backported fix for 1234009. #30635 (Also in 11)

electron v13.2.3

Release Notes for v13.2.3

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where the TouchBarScrubber crashes when showArrowButtons is enabled if items is an empty array. #30680 (Also in 14, 15)
  • Fixed titlebar showing under simple fullscreen mode. #30706 (Also in 14, 15)

electron v13.2.2

Release Notes for v13.2.2

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue present in webView where the leave-html-full-screen event is not emitted if the user exits fullscreen with esc instead of by clicking into the webView. #30560 (Also in 14, 15)
  • Fixed an issue where background color would not be correctly applied to BrowserViews on Windows when either the x or y coordinate is negative (off-screen). #30540 (Also in 14, 15)
  • Fixed an issue where toggling documentEdited status on macOS with titlebarStyle: 'hiddenInset' inadvertently moves the traffic light location. #30603 (Also in 14, 15)

Other Changes

  • Security: Backported fix for 1227933. #30583 (Also in 12)
  • Security: Backported fix for 1231950. #30584
  • Security: Backported fix for 1234764. #30587
  • Security: Backported fix for 1234770. #30586
  • Security: Backported fix for 1234829. #30585

electron v13.2.1

Release Notes for v13.2.1

Fixes

  • Fixed potential crash when programmatically closing a draggable frameless child window. #30572

electron v13.2.0

Release Notes for v13.2.0

Features

  • Added support for the des-ede3 cipher in node crypto. #30453 (Also in 11, 12)

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue on Mac where an application could not capture its own window using the desktop capture or getMediaSourceId APIs. #30523 (Also in 14, 15)

... (truncated)

Commits
  • 5771f38 Bump v13.3.0
  • a55bc6a chore: cherry-pick fix for 1231134 from chromium (#30637)
  • dcb311d chore: cherry-pick fix for 1228036 from v8 (#30639)
  • e5935ea chore: cherry-pick fix for 1233564 from chromium (#30636)
  • d927f1d chore: cherry-pick fix for 1234009 from chromium (#30635)
  • ca1cae6 fix: remove ipc wrapper for nativeImage.createThumbnailFromPath (#30736)
  • 69df216 Bump v13.2.3
  • 2917eb1 docs: explain the null webContents case in permission checks (#30686)
  • ec282ff fix: titlebar and buttons state under simple fullscreen (#30706)
  • 5d98e5e fix: crash when using TouchBarScrubber arrow button (#30680)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

Changing my account password breaks the Teams app, giving a message saying the tab needs to be refreshed. Doing so by pressing the button eventually brings it back to the same point.

Presented with initial log-in screen, as on first use.

Ubuntu 21.04, latest snap package

Deleting the contents of `~/snap/teams-for-linux/` solved the problem, but should not be necessary of course.

issues.title: Missing tray icon
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Teams successfully opens and a tray icon spawns, however, the tray icon is completely transparent with no visual icon. It can still be hovered over and clicked for a context menu.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Run teams
2. See the tray icon with no visual icon; context menu is still working

**Expected behavior**
Tray icon should have the Microsoft Teams logo displayed

**Desktop**
 - OS: Arch Linux
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version: 1.0.0 (As reported by Flatpak, was installed today; perhaps the Flatpak is still an older version?)

**Additional context**
This has been an issue for a while (about 1-2 months as a guess), but, I've only recently starting following projects and reporting issues for them.

issues.title: teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb contains a data.tar.xz that Debian 8/10 utilities can't cope with
issues.body:
According to Debian utilities (both Debian v8 and Debian v10), release deb teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb's content is "corrupt", although I'm able to access its contents just fine using 7-Zip on Windows.  It looks to me as though the deb's content may be triggering some obscure bug in Debian utilities (ar/ar-equivalent?), or perhaps there is some difference in handling of a sparse file(?).

Strangely, 7z.exe (x64) version 19.00 on Windows says that there is absolutely nothing wrong with teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb (md5sum 86d5d264543c094c45f92a4e9d35ea0e, size 62136904 bytes) and can extract a data.tar.xz from it (md5sum cf7dac63e3fd8648d210af335f67934c, size 62133448 bytes) that everything I try agrees is perfectly fine.  However, if I use any Debian dpkg utility (e.g. dpkg or dpkg-deb) or ar (from binutils) on the exact same deb file, the data.tar.xz that is extracted has a different checksum and the utility declares it to be "corrupt":

```
# dpkg --version
Debian `dpkg' package management program version 1.17.27 (amd64).
This is free software; see the GNU General Public License version 2 or
later for copying conditions. There is NO warranty.
# dpkg -i teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb
Selecting previously unselected package teams-for-linux.
(Reading database ... 31960 files and directories currently installed.)
Preparing to unpack teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking teams-for-linux (1.0.9) ...
dpkg-deb (subprocess): decompressing archive member: lzma error: compressed data is corrupt
dpkg-deb: error: subprocess <decompress> returned error exit status 2
dpkg: error processing archive teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb (--install):
 cannot copy extracted data for './opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux' to '/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux.dpkg-new': unexpected end of file or stream
Processing triggers for mime-support (3.58) ...
Processing triggers for hicolor-icon-theme (0.13-1) ...
Errors were encountered while processing:
 teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb

# dpkg-deb --version
Debian `dpkg-deb' package archive backend version 1.17.27 (amd64).
This is free software; see the GNU General Public License version 2 or
later for copying conditions. There is NO warranty.
# dpkg-deb -x teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb /tmp/foo
dpkg-deb (subprocess): decompressing archive member: lzma error: compressed data is corrupt
tar: Unexpected EOF in archive
tar: Unexpected EOF in archive
tar: Error is not recoverable: exiting now
dpkg-deb: error: subprocess tar returned error exit status 2

# ar --version
GNU ar (GNU Binutils for Debian) 2.25
Copyright (C) 2014 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
This program is free software; you may redistribute it under the terms of
the GNU General Public License version 3 or (at your option) any later version.
This program has absolutely no warranty.
# ar x teams-for-linux_1.0.9_amd64.deb data.tar.xz
# unxz --version
xz (XZ Utils) 5.1.0alpha
liblzma 5.1.0alpha
# unxz -t data.tar.xz
unxz: data.tar.xz: Compressed data is corrupt
# md5sum data.tar.xz /tmp/data.tar.xz
1f3100e6cbbf8295b92d56889a031c8d  data.tar.xz
cf7dac63e3fd8648d210af335f67934c  /tmp/data.tar.xz # <- extracted from same deb on Windows
```

labels.description: This issue depends on external projects

issues.title: Open a Teams Meeting using x-scheme-handler
issues.body:
To open a meeting from the browser I pointed .config/mimeapps.list to teams-for-linux.desktop:
`x-scheme-handler/msteams=teams-for-linux.desktop`
and in the .desktop file I set the Exec entry to:
`Exec=teams-for-linux %U`

That way, the Meeting URL gets opened using teams-for-linux. In there I have to click "continue in browser" to open the meeting itself (oposed to just getting routed to the meeting directly).

Is it possible to further route the provided URL so that it gets directly opened as a meeting? Also, can the changes I made to mimeapps.list and the .desktop file be shipped by default? I copied the settings from the official teams client.

issues.title: Multi-tenant feature
issues.body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/discussions/436
Originally posted by **jijojosephk** June 25, 2021 I've personally suffered from missing this feature on official teams client. What are the problems? - It only works with one organisation - Well you can log out and login but there's a terrible bug. After log out when you try to login with another domain, it just does not work. This bug exists for a long time. Anyway.. - I work with 3 different Teams Organisations everyday. Absolutely not possible with the official client. - I'm still unsure on why can't they do it. What I've tried is to have multiple snaps with different names when installing. One instance itself works slowly. I imagine how it would be when there are 3. Then, I started my own personal experiment and created a multi tenant electron wrapper. Currently I enjoy using it. I had no intentions to make it public rather I consider it as a work around for the troubles I had faced. What are your suggestions about this feature? Is it worth?

issues.title: ¿MacOS compilation?
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Nope

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I like to have a macOS binary

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Teams web and/or teams native app

**Additional context**
The problem here is that on my company, only windows PCs are allowed, so we can't use MS teams on our macs nor linux. We've tested this repository binaries inside our linux computers and we're able to connect to Teams!! So I suppose that if we could use this electron app also on macos... maybe we can access to. What do you think?

labels.name: not an issue
labels.description: Not really an issue or not much we can do here

issues.title: Install Warnings on Debian
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

On debian, two warnings are issued during the installation:

```
chmod: cannot access '/opt/teams-for-linux/chrome-sandbox': No such file or directory
/var/lib/dpkg/info/teams-for-linux.postinst: line 10: update-desktop-database: command not found
```

They do not seem to affect the functionality or behavior of teams.

**To Reproduce**


1. Download the [debian package](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/download/v1.0.6/teams-for-linux_1.0.6_amd64.deb)
2. As root, `dpkg -i teams-for-linux_1.0.6_amd64.deb `

**Expected behavior**

Those warnings should not be issued if they do not affect team's behavior.

**Screenshots**
N/A

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Debian 4.19.0-16-amd64 SMP Debian 4.19.181-1 (2021-03-19) x86_64 GNU/Linux
 - Installation package: teams-for-linux_1.0.6_amd64.deb 
 - Version 1.0.6

**Additional context**
The second warning may be related to https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/issues/4341

labels.name: wontfix
labels.description: This will not be worked on

issues.title: Font issue when saving attachments
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When you want to save an attachment to your computer, a popup of the file manager opens which allows you to select the saving destination. The font on the file manager is "corrupt".

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to any message with an attachment
2. Tap the download button to save it on your computer
3. See the font issue

**Expected behavior**
A window of the file manager should popup with readable text.

**Screenshots**
Dolphin file manager when saving the attachment:
![Teams Download](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10143926/112725781-353c5480-8f1a-11eb-8dbd-7f2e6152af88.png)

What my actual Dolphin file manager looks like under default circumstances:
![Dolphin](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10143926/112725958-302bd500-8f1b-11eb-9919-240084fe146c.png)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Manjaro
 - DE: KDE Plasma 5.21.3 
 - Installation package Snap
 - Version 1.0.6
 
**Additional information**
This happens under any system selected font. I tested it with the default Noto Sans and Apple's SFPro Text fonts.

**Description**

don’t know what the problem is as I can access all type of speakers, that is switch to builtin audio, headphone if plugged in, and also to raop sinks. The notebook (N15_17RD_RD2 also know from Tuxedo) here installed with openSUSE Leap 15.2  has hardware keys which can also control the volumen and mute/unmute state of the speaker but this also has no influence on the teams flatpak app.

The app org.pulseaudio.pavucontrol also works out of the box as well as the local pavucontrol and paprefs

**To Reproduce**

1. Start teams with ``flatpak run --verbose com.microsoft.Teams``

issues.title: building with podman / docker
issues.body:
Since I have to provide many project with many different node versions, I try to encapsulate my build processes in podman commands (should be the same in docker)
Today I built the latest version with:

podman run -it --rm --volume .:/var/mnt:z -w /var/mnt/ node:12 /bin/bash -c "apt update && apt install -y rpm && yarn install && yarn run dist:linux"

issues.title: YATY: Yet Another Thank You
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
As a user I would like to point out the TREMENDOUS work done by @IsmaelMartinez on this project. I would like to thank him on behalf of hundreds of developers using linux as a dev environment at La Poste (French post office) who where in the dark and have been able to see the light during the last months. I just wish Microsoft had a different strategy and had been able to overtake this magnificent piece or software to build upon it rather than re-inventing the wheel.

Meanwhile (we knew this moment had to come....), the latest bug on screen sharing forced me to look (for the 1000th time...) into the official client and see they had **FINALLY**(tm) fixed the SAML login bug, that I could access my account and that this was more or less functional, at least enough to do my job.

I might come back since I am not totally convinced the official client is totally stable. Thanks again a million and all the best for the future.

An impressed and delighted user

issues.title: Changing user agent to a lower version and removing now redundant browser settings
issues.body:
# What

Reducing the browser user agent to version 71 from 77. Also removed some redundant overwrite of settings.

# Why

Because MS did include screensharing support from version 72, but is now breaking our workaround for screensharing, and the settings that we use to overwrite, aren't needed anymore.

This fixes/closes #406

issues.title: Bump electron from 4.2.8 to 9.4.0
issues.body:
Bumps [electron](https://github.com/electron/electron) from 4.2.8 to 9.4.0.
Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v9.4.0

Release Notes for v9.4.0

Fixes

  • Added Electron DLLs like libGLESv2.dll to symbol server. #26967 (Also in 10, 11, 12)
  • Fixed systemPreferences.effectiveAppearance returning systemPreferences.getAppLevelAppearance(). #26881 (Also in 10, 11, 12)
  • Fixed an issue where event.reply could sometimes not deliver a reply to an IPC message when cross-site iframes were present. #26928 (Also in 10, 11, 12)
  • Fixed an issue where some buttons were un-clickable in some BrowserViews with draggable regions enabled. #26745 (Also in 10, 11)
  • Fixed an issue whereby a corrupted async_hooks stack would crash the renderer when throwing some errors in the renderer process. #26748 (Also in 10, 11)
  • Fixed an occasional crash on Windows related to NativeViewHost::SetParentAccessible. #26950 (Also in 10, 11, 12)
  • Fixed usage of --disable-dev-shm-usage for apps using --no-sandbox on linux. #26806

Other Changes

  • Backported the fix to CVE-2020-16024: Heap buffer overflow in UI. #26831
  • Security: backport fix for 1150649. #26897
  • Security: backported fix for 1137603. #26893
  • Security: backported fix for 1141350. #26895
  • Security: backported the fix to CVE-2020-16014: Use after free in PPAPI. #26856

  • Security: backported the fix to CVE-2020-16022: Insufficient policy enforcement in networking. #26861

electron v9.3.5

Release Notes for v9.3.5

Fixes

  • Fixed <webview> render-process-gone event dispatch. #26576
  • Fixed LC_ALL environment variable getting changed in Electron. #26508 (Also in 10, 11)
  • Fixed debug.log files being created under working directory on windows. #26267 (Also in 10)
  • Fixed draggable regions stops working when devtools is opened on macOS. #26506 (Also in 10, 11)

Other Changes

  • Backported the fix to CVE-2020-16017: Browser UAF when detaching a provisional frame. #26477
  • Fixed value of getSystemVersion() on Big Sur. #26430
  • Security: backported fix for 1143772. #26400
  • Security: backported fix for 1144489. #26397
  • Security: backported fix for chromium:1133527. #26412
  • Security: backported fix for chromium:1137608. #26409
  • Security: backported fix for chromium:1139398. #26406

Unknown

  • Re-enable Rosetta on Apple Silicon devices. #26572 (Also in 7.3, 8, 10, 11)

electron v9.3.4

Release Notes for v9.3.4

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where Hover Text on macOS Catalina did not work without VoiceOver also being enabled. #26244 (Also in 10, 11)

Hi, I have a strange behavior here: I tried to join a meeting organized outside my oy organization and I was admitted to the waiting room. Once the organizer let me in, the call was interrupted. Teams printed the error message, that something went wrong with joining the call.
When I switched to the official MS client this did not happen. Any idea what could be the cause for this?

snap           2.48.2-lp152.1.1
series         16
opensuse-leap  15.2

issues.title: Screensharing stopped working
issues.body:
Some when over the weekend - between Friday, January 8th, 14:00 UTC and Monday, January 11th 08:00 UTC, screen sharing stopped working for me. When I share a screen, the app behaves like if it would work, but other participant in the call only see my persona icon, instead of the shared screen.
I uninstalled/reinstalled teams-for-linux already, that didn't fix the issue.
I also tried the official Teams client from MS, but that app shares only whole screens instead of individual applications, like Ismael's teams-for-linux.
This applies to the RPM release 1.0.5, `teams-for-linux-1.0.5.x86_64.rpm`.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start teams-for-linux 
2. Start a call
3. Click on the "Open share tray" button
4. Select a screen or application to share

**Expected behavior**
Other participants on the call should see my shared application or screen.

**Screenshots**
None, as nothing is to be seen.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora release 33 (Thirty Three) running with XOrg (not Wayland)
 - Installation package teams-for-linux-1.0.5.x86_64.rpm
 - Version 1.0.5

issues.title: Use native file dialog on Qt-based desktop environments
issues.body:
Currently, when downloading or uploading a file in teams-for-linux, the gtk file selector dialog turns up, which looks rather out of place in KDE Plasma (or LXQt). It would appear more native if KDialog / xdg-dektop-portal-kde could be used instead.
However, it's electron's fault; see [this thread](https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/2911).
Perhaps https://github.com/codebytere/native-kde-file-open could be used as a workaround until the upstream issue is fully resolved?

issues.title: Electron 5.0.x?
issues.body:
Hi Ismael,

We at ThinLinX Pty Ltd (an embedded Linux thin client startup specialising in Raspberry Pi) are very appreciative of the work you've done, which remains the only practical way to get MS Teams working on most of our platforms (which include i386 and armhf).

We have an - admittedly obscure - problem with 1.0.5 in that it pulls in a binary build of electron 4.2.8 that will not work with old Debian/Raspbian releases (specifically Debian 8, Jessie) that we use for our "long term stable" release, because its GLibC and GLib dependencies are much too high.  At the time, Electron maintainers were publicly rather unsympathetic about this issue (https://github.com/electron/electron/issues/16205), but they appear to have nonetheless corrected it in Electron 5.0.x armv7l builds.  Teams-for-Linux 1.0.5 doesn't appear to be compatible with Electron 5.0.x, however.  We tried Electron 5.0.13 and got the following:

Uncaught Exception:
Error: onBeforeRequest parameter 'filter' must have property 'urls'.
    at App.onAppReady (/opt/teams-for-linux/resources/app.asar/app/mainAppWindow/index.js:30:40)

Is there any chance you could update Teams-For-Linux to use a higher Electron baseline?

P.S. I admit to being a Yarn/NPM/Javascript-generally dunce.  Including some build instructions with your source code would be very helpful for (older) people like me, who have no clue what to do with a "package.json" and no Makefile!  I think I figured most of it out eventually, but it was a struggle.

- Paul Whittaker

issues.title: Cannot switch to a different org view
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
My Teams account is a member of my main organization. But I have recently been added as a guest to a different organization. 

There is a drop down in the top right near my profile icon that I can click to get a list of all my orgs (just 2 atm) and it seems like I should be able to click on the other org (that I am a guest in) to change my view over that org and be able to see all of my chats and other activity. But when I click on the org it opens a new tab in my browser (not in the Teams local application wrapper). The tab in the browser does correctly show the context for the org that I clicked. But my local application window is still stuck in my main org, it did not change contexts to the one I clicked.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Load Teams with an account that has access to 2 or more organizations
2. Click on the dropdown arrow next to the profile icon
3. Click on any org that does not have the check mark by it
4. new Tab opens in browser, but local app remains in the same org context

**Expected behavior**
Local application should switch to the context of the org that I clicked on, instead of opening a new tab in the browser. 

**Screenshots**
This is the dropdown I clicked: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2406189/99694124-b148fc00-2a51-11eb-953b-0fa072d0c8b9.png)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Pop! OS
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version 1.0.0

**Additional context**
I know that microsoft has a more official teams app for linux now, but the call / video quality when I am in meetings is way worse in the offical app, whereas it's just fine in this one so I have continued to use this one.

Usage question: Problem with joining meetings from the calendar (no enhancement request or bug report ;-))
issues.body:
I do encounter a problem with joining meetings from the calendar within the teams client. Does anyone else here encounter such problems? In those cases the client jumps directly to the "Teams" tab. 
Opening the appointment in the browser gives me a chance to submit a chat message and from the chat in the client I can then join the meeting.

issues.title: Grid/tile view missing
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Microsoft added up to 7x7 grid view of video call participants to their Teams client in May 2020. See [Grid View in Microsoft Teams](https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/education_ms/forum/all/grid-view-in-microsoft-teams/22111e01-57d6-485b-907c-8bf543fe74dc) on answers.microsoft.com.

Is this something that should show up in the `teams-for-linux` client automatically or does it need to be implemented separately?

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like to have a grid view automatically enabled in `teams-for-linux`

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Trying out the proprietary Linux Teams client from Microsoft to see whether grid view works there. I haven't done that yet.

**Additional context**
Huge thanks @IsmaelMartinez and contributors for keeping the `teams-for-linux` client updated and working. When Microsoft's own Linux client came out, I evaluated both clients and found `teams-for-linux` to be more reliable and work in a way I preferred. Besides, it's valuable to have an Open Source option alive even if the proprietary one works well.

issues.title: Google Drive Integration Doesn't Work
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When I go to the Files section and select Connect to Google Drive, it'll open up a new tab in Firefox for accounts.google.com to authorize, then it closes really quickly. Afterwards, the integration just loads forever and doesn't do anything. Trying to tell it to disconnect and reconnect again just opens up a Firefox tab super quickly, and then closes it soon after. I assume this means it's just going through accounts.google.com and since I already authorized it just closes. Anyway, the same issue occurs. It just spins, and I can't access my files. The same thing happens if I set my default browser to Chrome.

In the normal Teams app, it works fine. Opening teams.microsoft.com in Firefox also works just fine. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Files
2. Click on "Add cloud storage"
3. Sign in to Google in the browser window
4. See how it endlessly loads

**Expected behavior**
I expected to see my Google Drive files.

**Screenshots**
https://imgur.com/a/8IzFPnc

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Pop!_OS 20.04
 - Installation package: deb, flatpak
 - Version: 1.0.5

issues.title: Write plain-text chatlogs
issues.body:
Microsoft, in their infinite wisdom, made it damn near impossible to get useful textual chatlogs from Teams. Admins might be able to do it, but users can't (even copy-pasting entire conversations doesn't work, and for years, copy-pasting more than one message at a time from a conversation wasn't possible...).

I'm wondering whether the open-source client could be enhanced to write text-based chat log files, like almost all IM clients do. That would be quite the killer feature.

Ideally, there'd be one log file per chat, with one line per message, prefixed with a timestamp and the name of the sender.

I realize that this may not be easy, since the Electron client is essentially just a browser, but hey, one can dream.

Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v7.2.4

Release Notes for v7.2.4

Fixes

  • Fixed Promise timeout issue when running Electron as Node. #23324
  • Fixed a use-after-free error that could happen if a Tray was destroyed while showing a custom context menu. #23182
  • Fixed an issue where windows without nativeWindowOpen: true could invoke the non-native-open path. #23224
  • Fixed memory leak when using contextBridge with sandbox=true. #23232

Fixes

issues.title: Streams with pred_weight_table unsupported.- trashing logs
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The bug is that we see allot of logs printing this "Streams with pred_weight_table unsupported." 
it trashes the systemd log stream during a ms teams call, 
here's a related issue
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/24005714/why-am-i-getting-unsupported-format-errors-reading-h-264-encoded-rtsp-streams 

**To Reproduce**

1. Go to Teams cleint
2. Click on 'Click on calander, and locate the meeting you want to join'

issues.title: Show app window after it was minimized to tray
issues.body:
On GNOME Shell 3.34.3 (preinstalled with ubuntu)
Starting teams-for-linux from taskbar or from gnome activities, if it isn't already running, opens it in window mode. However if it is running and is in tray then you cannot open it in window mode from neither taskbar nor activities, you have to navigate tray icon menu. And so the only thing you get by typing "teams-for-linux" in terminal is response "App already running", when it should just bring it back to window mode from tray.

I use Super + N hotkey to open taskbar items but with t4l it doesn't werk. Seeing how t4l doubles as instant messenger, inability to open it in a window from tray is damaging.

Whenever I try to sign in either web or app, it says that we couldn't sign you in, please try again later on UBUNTU 16.04 desktop

I am having issue in running MS teams in my linux as I cannot sign-in. I am able to sign-in using WINDOWS OS and my android , so it's not the issue from my service provider or admin. I am able to sign-in using UBUNTU 18 desktop, the issue persists on Ubuntu 16.04

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Download .deb file from Git
2. when option pops-up select using "Software launcher"

2. Sign-in to your office 365

I tried to check if I can debug it but couldn't.

issues.title: screen share - individual display/screen on multiple output setting
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
My workspace consists in two display devices.
When I need to share screen, I only get to choose one of the following options

1. Share a single window, which gives me problems when having sub-windows prompted
2. Share full desktop (both displays) which means not much readability.
---
**Describe the solution you'd like**
Teams (including Linux version), gives the option of sharing one of the screens
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1073243/79311378-a1869980-7efd-11ea-8f7b-47d448f7860d.png)

---
**Describe alternatives you've considered**
My current workaround is use option 1, with constant checkup with my colleagues, or just remove one of the displays while in the meeting.

---

issues.title: Please request auto-connection to camera for the snap
issues.body:
As you know, the teams-for-linux snap doesn't have access to the camera by default. Users have to turn this on manually, which is not great user experience. There is a process to request auto-connection for this interface, however.

Similar snaps such as Discord have been granted auto-connection to the camera. As an example, their request is here: https://forum.snapcraft.io/t/camera-plug-auto-connect-for-discord/2730

More info about permission requests: https://snapcraft.io/docs/permission-requests

Requesting this auto-connection is relatively easy and it improves user experience a lot. Let me know if you have more questions about this.

issues.title: Teams opens meeting link 8 times
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I experience similar issues like #322 (looking at the log of #322 it tries 8 times). I click the meeting link in Evolution or Firefox, teams-for-linux opens 8 new instances, even though it is already running. If I click `Continue in this browser` I can join the meeting anonymously, I am not logged in. Using `Open with Microsoft Teams` it displays the `not found or moved` error.
If I copy&paste the link like mentioned in issue #318, this behaviour is not shown. It just joins the meeting (and the user is still logged in).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Generate meeting link
2. Click on meeting link
3. Watch teams-for-linux open 8 new instances
4. Choose one of them and click `Continue with this Browser` (or similar, topmost button) - Success!
5. User is not logged in and joined anonymously - Partial Success...

**Expected behavior**
Teams opens links in existing window, not 8 new ones.
Teams logging the user in before joining meeting.

**Screenshots**
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45227795/78759819-83122280-7980-11ea-8394-fc9655c8d035.png)
As you can see, teams-for-linux didn't open the meeting with the existing instance (rightmost icon in top panel). Instead it opened 8 new ones. The 8 new instances aren't logging me in, I join the meeting anonymously (notice the missing avatar).
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45227795/78760507-8fe34600-7981-11ea-89ba-d0faa4654c67.png)
When I end the call, the login button (german `Anmelden`) is displayed.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Manjaro Linux (up to date)
 - Installation package: Flatpak
 - Version: 1.0.0

labels.name: duplicate
labels.description: This issue or pull request already exists

issues.title: Audio problems with Fedora 31
issues.body:
For some reason neither teams nor sudo teams has sound input or output under "teams" except for the test call. The test call works fine.. But during a meeting the device settings shows the output as being muted and the microphone shows no levels.. It eventually says there is something wrong with my mike.

I maxed out things using pavucontrol and alsa mixer. Still nothing.. I tried running it as root, nothing..

I even have a webcam plugged it to see if it performs better.. Nope, same lack of input and output.

This is a Acer Nitro 5:
$ aplay -l
**** List of PLAYBACK Hardware Devices ****
card 0: Generic [HD-Audio Generic], device 3: HDMI 0 [HDMI 0]
  Subdevices: 1/1
  Subdevice #0: subdevice #0
card 1: Generic_1 [HD-Audio Generic], device 0: ALC255 Analog [ALC255 Analog]
  Subdevices: 1/1
  Subdevice #0: subdevice #0
[turgut@tklaptop ~] $ 

$ arecord -l
**** List of CAPTURE Hardware Devices ****
card 1: Generic_1 [HD-Audio Generic], device 0: ALC255 Analog [ALC255 Analog]
  Subdevices: 1/1
  Subdevice #0: subdevice #0
card 2: USB20Camera [USB2.0_Camera], device 0: USB Audio [USB Audio]
  Subdevices: 1/1
  Subdevice #0: subdevice #0

I tried the --try-supported-channel-layouts  trick, no change.
what shall I try?
This laptop records and plays just fine.. any of the other tool can use the mike and speakers without issues..

issues.title: Blank screen on startup, removing ~/.config/teams-for-linux doesn't help
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

I hesitate to report a "known issue", but I don't completely understand the mention of "on login".
In case that is different than "on startup", here is my report.

Initially I get just the icon in the status bar, but if I right click that and click run, I get a blank window titled "teams-for-linux".

Installing the snap with "snap install --channel=edge teams-for-linux" seems to work, although I don't know if that is built from the same source.

What's different?
1) I don't make it as far as a login screen
2) Removing ~/.config/teams-for-linux does not change anything.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. git checkout d7f3647ad30161
2. sudo apt install yarnpkg rpm
3. yarnpkg run dist:linux
3.  start teams-for-linux, e.g. with "yarnpkg start"
4. Right click on icon, select "open"
5. see blank screen.

**Expected behavior**
I expect to see the teams login box

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Debian testing
 - Installation package: source
 - Version:  d7f3647ad30161

**Additional context**

yarnpkg is probably called yarn other places

I'm running the tiling window manager i3

issues.title: Help Wanted: Is there a way to change user agent?
issues.body: I'm new to this project and wanted to know if there is a way to change user agent. My company recently migrated us to teams but does not allow linux. (Well it seems they have't done it intentionally, but aren't willing to look into it and configure it properly either). Anyway, I'm able to login to teams via browser by changing my user agent. Sadly, screen share doesn't work. Is there a way to change UA here to Windows and still get every thing to work (audio/video calls and screen share)?

issues.title: Timezone is always displayed in UTC
issues.body:
Today I have upgraded to the 1.3 version and the timezone is always displayed in UTC. e.g. a scheduled meeting for 10.30 localtime is displayed as 8.30 as we are in UTC+2 currently.

Local timezone is configured correctly and time is also correct.

issues.title: Window empty blank on Ubuntu 18.04 Xubuntu XFCE
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
After starting the teams-for-linux the window popup, but it's white, blank and empty. The usual CTRL+R isn't working.
Bevor it was working on Ubuntu 18.04 Gnome Desktop, but after login with Xubuntu session, which is an XFCE desktop, the application window is always empty. MS-teams is working in Firefox or Chrome.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start teams-for-linux from commandline or XFCE Application menu
2. see blank window

**Expected behavior**
First the login should be requested and then the MS-teams application content should be shown.

**Screenshots**
A blank window. Just that. No error output on terminal, when starting from commandline.

**Desktop**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.04LTS, Xubuntu, XFCE session
 - Installation package deb
 - Version 1.0.0

**Additional context**
It's somehow related to my network connection. It's working if I'm directly connected to the internet. If I'm connected to a network with firewall restrictions, where I need a http proxy to access the internet, it's not working in XFCE session. In Gnome session it worked in both.

issues.title: Logs me out of everything in chrome, if I open a link from chat, without having a browser open first.
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I have a weird issue when I click on links in chat, and it took a while until I realized why chrome keeps asking me to re-authenticate every now and then; and this only happens from teams for me.
Without a running chrome instance, the next chrome that gets opened, if i click on a link in chat, is completely resetting all logins, and I have to re-authenticate with every service I use browser wide.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have Chrome installed (I have a syncing google account set up)
2. Have it not running
3. Click on a link in a chat, which opens chrome
4. Check if logins (facebook, etc.) still work or require re-authentication
4a. another hint is, that the current profile in Chrome is on "sync paused" and stays there. once you have that browser open, closing it and reopening it is already too late. the profile is somehow reset.

**Expected behavior**
Should not do this. 
Clicking on links in the official microsoft teams works as expected (so do other apps), so it must be the way the browser is called.
If the browser is already running, behaviour is fine (new tab is opened)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux, KDE Neon (based on Ubuntu 18.04 bionic)
 - Installation package deb
 - Version: tried 0.6.6, 1.0.1

**Additional context**
Might be related to chrome, or electron, or kde, of course.
Is definitely low priority, as there is a workaround (simply have a browser open before clicking links)
And it is a minor hassle (one can simply log back in)
However, it would interest me, how this can happen; so mainly I would be happy to investigate how links are opened differently here.
I used the official teams as comparison, which does not have this issue (however is unusable for me due to it's excessive CPU usage)
I will also check other electron apps.

Yes I know this is a somewhat weird issue, but maybe someone else is wondering why he gets random re-authentications. It probably happens since forever.

Basically, what happens is that the content inside teams-for-linux window flashes for a few times and then teams-for-linux ends with:

`The resource you are looking for has been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable.`
**Expected behavior**
A screen saying something like "Waiting for someone to allow you to get in"

**Screenshots**

issues.title: GNOME Notifications don't display when active on another workspace
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When Teams for Linux is open on another workspace and a chat is selected, notifications do not show up.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
0. Start Teams for Linux with `enableDesktopNotificationsHack`
1. Open any chat with a person
2. Move to another workspace
3. Have that person send a message
4. No notification

**Expected behavior**
The notification should display and the red icon should show up. If you leave the chat, and say, go to your calendar before switching workspaces, the notification displays.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.04
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 1.0.0

issues.title: Clicking on URLs or card buttons doesn't do anything
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Clicking on a link or card button doesn't do anything; in previous versions it opened the link in the default browser. (Control-click, middle-click, and right-click all work as expected.)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Click on a link or button in a chat or team discussion.
2. Observe that the link is not opened in an existing browser (if running) or new browser (if not).

**Expected behavior**
The link should have been opened in the default browser.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 31
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version 1.0.0

**Additional context**
`xdg-open [link]` opens in the correct browser.

issues.title: difference electron client <--> webclient, customizing the electron client
issues.body:
Hello, i am copying my question asked via gmail + answer for future reference:

----------------

>>Hi,
>>
>>First of all, thx for your work. i am trying to integrate ms teams into
>>our linux-based product, which works basically when using an up-to-date
>>chrome browser, running the web application

>>- (plz forgive me if this is a stupid question:) i dont really understand the difference between >>your client and the Ms Teams webclient. from my first perspective, running
>>https://teams.microsoft.com in my chrome v78 looks the same as your
>>client...?

The client uses Electron that runs internally a version of chromium (69 I think). Running chrome v78 will mostly look the same as the client. Chromium is only a part of Chrome (mostly just the renderer) so there are quite a few (electron) caveats.

>>- would it be possible to customize or remove certain elements from within your clients.
>>for example, remove the menu on the left, hide certain button, insert custom buttons,... ?

You can hide elements using CSS. There are a few templates in the project that can give you something to play with.

issues.title: Allow the middle click on URLs to open in the default browser

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

issues.title: Login to trello app inside teams doesn't work
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When I try to add a trello board to my team, I'm not able to login to it so I can see the board.

**To Reproduce**
When I try to add a trello board to my team, it will give me a button to the first login to trello as shown below:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/279828/66704379-c5201b80-ecf1-11e9-9cda-f4c73cc47260.png)

When I click on "Log in with Trello", a chrome browser will open to this link: `https://trello.com/login?returnUrl=%2Fintegrations%2Fteams%2Ftab-config-login` and will close itself automatically 1-second latter.

Nothing will happen then and I will be still logged off from trello and unable to add it.

Note that I can log in, add the board and visualize it if I use teams directly from the browser, but I will still not be able to use it in teams-for-linux with the above issue.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
I'm using the latest (0.7.0) version in Gentoo linux

issues.title: Teams for Linux snap application will not start
issues.body:
(This issue has also been [posted on askubuntu](https://askubuntu.com/questions/1177634/teams-for-linux-snap-application-will-not-start?noredirect=1#comment1966638_1177634).)

I recently installed Ubuntu 19.04 on my work laptop, and installed Teams for Linux snap for work purposes. Everything seemed to work perfectly at first, but at some point it stopped working. I can find the shortcut icon under Gnome's applications list, but nothing happens when I click it. Right clicking and selecting "Show details" returns the error message `Could not find "teams-for-linux_teams-for-linux.desktop"`. I tried to start the application from terminal to see if it returns any error messages, but absolutely nothing happens. When executing startup command, it simply returns to command prompt as shown below.

```
~$ teams-for-linux
~$
```

I've tried adding "verbose" (`--verbose`, `--v`, `-v`) and "help" tags (`--help`, `-h`) to see if this will return any feedback, but the result is the same. It simply returns to command prompt. issues.title: No mic input during meetings
issues.body:
No audio input during Teams meeting.  Even though the mic is functional for other applications (Skype, for example).  I am using a Koss USB headset.

**Steps to reproduce the behavior:**
1. Go to 'Calendar'
2. Click on 'Join' button in the event
3. Ensure that mic is not muted (i.e. mic icon is not crossed out)
4. Join the meeting

**Expected behavior**
Participants should hear me when I speak.

**System**
- Desktop: Fedora 30, KDE spin
 - OS: Linux 5.2.11-200.fc30.x86_64
 - Installation package rpm
 - Version: 0.7.0

I have _sometimes_ been able to achieve success by:
1. Opening the system's audio settings
2. Switch the default input from the USB device to some other input - typically the webcam
3. Apply the change
4. Reopen the system's audio settings
5. Change the default input back to the USB headset
6. Restart Teams for Linux

Repeat the above once or twice more.

I highly suspect this is more distro related than it is app related.  Nevertheless I would appreciate any advice you can offer on how I can troubleshoot the problem more effectively.

labels.name: not an issue
labels.description: Not really an issue or not much we can do here

issues.body:
Currently in our company we use teams. The application is good, however last month we started to have CA certs for our proxy and the application no longer works. What happens is it only shows a blank screen upon starting.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to the linux console
2. Execute teams-for-linux
3. Error will show

configPath = /home/xxx/snap/teams-for-linux/62/.config/teams-for-linux
Failed to get the config file, using default values
configFile = {}
Error org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.Failed: cannot use setting "default-url-scheme-handler": not allowed
Unknown cert issuer for url: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize?response_type=id_token&client_id=5e3ce6c0-2b1f-4285-8d4b-75ee78787346&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fteams.microsoft.com%2Fgo&state=ba6078e1-05e0-4ffa-86cd-7d899e4c0816&&client-request-id=fc900571-3f82-4298-b8be-7c104e3101f5&x-client-SKU=Js&x-client-Ver=1.0.9&nonce=331b9d76-8edb-43e2-a73a-47dff910e581&domain_hint=
Issuer Name: XXX
The unknown certificate fingerprint is: sha256/XXXX

**Expected behavior**
I am not sure how to fix the issue, but the expected behaviour is to have a way to load the SSL certs to be trusted and make the application work.

 - OS: Ubuntu
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version: 0.7.0

**Additional context**
None.

issues.title: When running multiple app instances, only one provides "Calls" feature
issues.body:
I need to participate in several teams simultaneously. That could be achieved by using native-packaged application (rpm in my case) by overriding XDG_CONFIG_HOME env variable, so teams-for-linux uses different config directories for different instances.

Unfortunately, only first-spawn instance gets access to sound equipment (it shows both "Calls" tab and "Calls" menu in Settings.
All other copies do not show any of them.

Also, once instance missed its sound, you need to sign-out and sign-in again in order to get sound back (assuming no other instances run at that time) - just restarting application does not help.

It would be really nice to get sound in all instances.

System is Fedora30 with pulseaudio.
 --- Solution:
Adding this to the code should be as easy as adding:

app.commandLine.appendSwitch('--try-supported-channel-layouts');
around https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/app/index.js#L10

maybe someone can create a PR with that change.

labels.name: ready
labels.description: There is a fix in develop branch (and snap edge). Soon to be released

issues.title: Download to ~/Downloads, without file save pop-up
issues.body:
I often need to save a bunch of images or attachments supplied via Teams for local manipulation. Having to (1) find the relevant dialog and (2) click "Save" is a pain. (And half the time I lose the original file name in the process too.) Being able to click Download and have files silently appear in the relevant folder would be awesome (and consistent with other incarnations of Teams).

Update: as per [this Electron documentation](https://github.com/electron/electron/blob/master/docs/api/download-item.md), it should be possible to achieve this behaviour without relying on a third-party package.

labels.name: ready

issues.title: "No available camera found" error after upgrade to 0.7.0 (snap and deb)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
In chat or while attending a scheduled meeting, the video camera icon appears that allows the user to either enable or disable video.
After I upgraded to 0.7.0 release using either snap or deb, this icon is disabled all the time.
**However, if I use the 0.7.0 AppImage, then the camera is enabled.**
So I believe something is broken in teams-for-linux snap and deb versions.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Upgrade to version 0.7.0 using either snap or deb package.
2. Open "Microsoft Teams for Linux".
3. Start a chat with someone.
4. Notice the video camera icon at the top right is disabled all the time.

**Expected behavior**
The video camera icon should be enabled, so that the user can start a video call.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4309910/63838470-b10c9e80-c97d-11e9-82e9-f020272356f1.png)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.04.3 LTS.
 - Installation package: Teams for Linux Snap package.
 - Version: 0.7.0 snap and deb packages.

When I close the main window the process is still running in the background making it impossible to reopen the app later without having to kill it myself.

After my first login, after I either reboot or restart teams-for-linux, Teams takes several minutes to load, and then eventually fails, culminating in Teams returning "We're sorry - we've run into an issue." 

Reinstalling teams-for-linux allows me to use it again, but the issue reoccurs after the first login.

issues.title: Teams registers as HTML file handler
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The application registers itself as handler for HTML files i.e. to be used when in Caja (MATE's fork of Nautilus) an HTML is double-clicked.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set HTML file in Caja (MATE fork of Nautilus) to open in e.g. Firefox (if not already the case)
2. Start Teams
3. Open an HTML file in Caja via double-click: The Teams window gets the focus, instead of the HTML file being opened in the configured browser. The Caja settings also show that now Teams is registered as handler for HTML files.

**Expected behavior**
Teams shouldn't touch the handler setting for HTML files (or any other file type, if applicable).

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu MATE 16.04 AMD64
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version: 0.6.0

# Fix

This is now fixed in all releases. Apologies again and thanks to @jef79m for the PR. Without it, I will be still preparing the fix release.

for anyone using snap, the following command should fix this issue:

`sudo snap refresh teams-for-linux` 

Clean your application cache before starting:
https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/KNOWN_ISSUES.md#blank-page

If you still get a blank screen, clean your config location:
https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/KNOWN_ISSUES.md#config-folder-locations

issues.title: Fixes issue 220. Electron's getLocale, and Chromium's l10n_util, can …
issues.body: Fixes issue 220. Electron's getLocale, and Chromium's l10n_util, can return the language code in the IETF BCP 47 format (en-US) instead of the ISO 15897 format (en_US). Hunspell, the dictionaries included with this project, and the JavaScript code here assumes the ISO 15897 format, so this hotfix converts what getLocale returns.

Enable it!

Doesn't apply here.

issues.title: No spell check occurring for English in 0.6.0 Snap or RPM
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When I misspell words in a text window, they are not red underlined, and right clicking them does not bring up any corrections.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to a chat screen.
2. Type a misspelled word in a text box.

**Expected behavior**
The word should be underlined red, and right clicking the word should show spelling corrections.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15981736/62881819-a48e1200-bcfe-11e9-9e0e-75fd1d067cac.png)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 30 (Workstation Edition)
 - Installation package: Snap and RPM
 - Version: 0.6.6 (Snap) and 0.6.0 (RPM)

**Additional context**
My system language is en_US.UTF-8 (from /etc/locale.conf and $LANG). I have hunspell-en-US installed. /usr/share/myspell/en_US.aff and .dic exist. I've confirmed that this bug exists for me in both the Snap and the RPM packages. I've tried clearing both ~/snap/teams-for-linux/current/.config/teams-for-linux and ~/.config/teams-for-linux .

I've looked at known issues and past bugs for this project, and they seem to apply to other languages and previous versions of teams-for-linux which used different spell check methods.

I've tried running teams-for-linux from command line and didn't see any relevant errors. I'm not specifying the "--rightClickWithSpellcheck" option. I haven't figured what what "yarn start" means, yet.

issues.title: Debian package uses deprecated gir1.2-gnomekeyring-1.0
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Installing the .deb package under debian testing (bullseye) or stable (buster) fails due to not installable dependency to gir1.2-gnomekeyring-1.0 . 
libgnome-keyring is deprecated and it's use strongly discouraged [1]; hence gir1.2-gnomekeyring1.0 was removed in debian buster.
The debian and GNOME teams recommend using libsecret/gir1.2-secret-1 [2] instead.

I am reporting this as a bug as version 0.4.0 does not require gir1.2-gnomekeyring-1.0

**Steps to reproduce the behavior**
1. use either debian buster/stable or debian bullseye/testing 
2. dpkg --install teams-for-linux_0.6.0_amd64.deb
3. apt --fix-missing install
4. Get error message that gir1.2-gnomekeyring-1.0 is not  installable

**Expected behavior**
No deprecated libraries should be used.

**Desktop**
 - OS: Debian GNU/Linux
 - Installation package: teams-for-linux_0.5.0_amd64.deb, teams-for-linux_0.6.0_amd64.deb
 - Version: 0.5.0 and later

**References**
[1] https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/libgnome-keyring/commit/6a5adea4aec93
[2] https://wiki.gnome.org/Projects/Libsecret

issues.title: Better handle external requests in new windows

Here we use a counter to track the requests that are slated to open in a new window and have a URL of `about:blank`. We then filter all requests and proceed normally when the counter is less than 1.

Closes #182

issues.title: Release 0.5.0
issues.body:
# General notes

_The __change #194 might require to clean your configuration. Check the https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/KNOWN_ISSUES.md#no-history for information about what needs to be removed.

This release its been mostly concentrated in stability issues/fixes. Microsoft did surprise us again by changing the implementation of the remote desktop. 

# Detail changes

* #180 Snap not running from Wayland Session (PR #181) (Thanks to @zigford ) 
* #183 The client now prints the location for the config.json file. This solves the issue where clients will not know where to put the config.json file (part of #186)
* #184 Fixing the issue where the app stopped working after suspension was trigger.
* #186 Refactoring the index.js and adding some console.log messages to ease issues debugging (PR #189)
* #187 Fixing an issue where Fedora couldn't open the app again.
* #194 Fixing the Screensharing not working anymore. (PR #195 )

The packages versions has been updated a bit, as we are still block for moving to version 5 of electron (#156).

# Thanks

To everyone involved from people reporting issues, finding work around, testing solutions and implementing them. Again, you all make this possible. 

# Notes on version number.

Releasing as 0.5.0 as, at the moment, I don't consider this 1.0.0 until we can move to electron 5/6.

issues.title: Allow for pasting text without markup
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When copying and pasting text, like 
```
Issue: Feature request
Suggest an idea for this project. If this doesn’t look right, choose a different type.
```
which i found right above this text area and paste it into teams, the resulting text has the same formatting like this website. 
While this MIGHT be handy sometimes i mostly find it very annoying. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I can imagine many solution but i am unable to overview possible side effects

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Maybe possible Solutions:
- add a setting to disable formatting at all - so incoming, formatted text would be made raw, too
- add a warning when pasting text that contains markup and offer a button to paste it without markup
- Add a button in the compose box "Remove all formatting"

**Additional context**
We found out, that the resulting, formatted text is dependend of the SENDER'S theme. Is that a bug in `teams-for-linux` oder in the original code? Is it a bug at all?

issues.title: Make animated gifs stop moving and collapsable

Next they should be collapsble like minimizing the gif (and other images) so they don't  use that much space in threads.

Perhaps electron cannot use native wayland, so disabling it in the snap

Tested by building snap locally on my machine.

issues.title: Snap does not run from Wayland Session
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Snap package immediately exits if run from within a wayland session. Additionally, if you have had a wayland session running, then switch to Xorg, the same problem exists as described [here](https://forum.snapcraft.io/t/electron-apps-and-wayland/12315)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install the snap
2. Ensure your logged into a Wayland session over Xorg
3. Attempt to run snap
4. Nothing happens

**Expected behavior**
The app opens

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Gentoo linux
 - Installation package snap
 - Version [0.3.0] and [0.4.0]

**Additional context**

**Workaround**
A workaround is to set env variable `DISABLE_WAYLAND=1 snap run teams-for-linux`
This forces the electron app to fall back to XWayland

Possibly the cause of #151
