issues.title: Multiple Teams icons in dock (fix proposed)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
After opening Teams for Linux in elementary OS, with its default dock (an elementary-patched version of Plank), a second, low-resolution icon appears in the dock in addition to the main high-resolution icon (which remains if pinned, and disappears after a few seconds if not).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
- Install Teams for Linux (snap or deb) in elementary OS
- Launch it from the dock or Applications Menu

**Expected behavior**
One (high resolution) icon that hosts all Teams for Linux windows.

**Screenshots**
![2019-07-18 at 00 21 27 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2563783/61417280-fd55cf00-a939-11e9-84d9-bd282431da4f.png)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: elementary OS "Juno" (based on Ubuntu 18.04+Gnome)
 - Installation package: I've tried both deb and snap
 - Version 0.3.0

**Additional context**
I've identified the following issues / solutions:
- The .desktop file distributed with Teams for Linux includes `StartupWMClass=teams-for-linux`, but according to `xprop` the WM_CLASS of each window is actually `"teams for linux" "Teams for Linux"` (note the spaces instead of hyphens).
- Manually updating the .desktop file with `StartupWMClass="teams for linux"` doesn't work (not with Plank, anyway--I'm not sure if other docks / window managers work better with spaces in WM_CLASS). 
- After investigating further, the incorrect WM_CLASS is being emitted as a result of the `"productName": "Teams for Linux"` entry in [package.json](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/package.json). I confirmed this by removing the `productName` and rebuilding; the `"name": "teams-for-linux"` entry took precedence, WM_CLASS was set to to `"teams-for-linux" "teams-for-linux"`, and the dock's window matching issue was resolved.
- The downside of this is side-effects like `$HOME/.config/Teams for Linux` becoming `$HOME/.config/teams-for-linux` (meaning users will lose their configuration). It may be preferable to set WM_CLASS manually in some other way.

Locally, I've returned to stock 0.3.0 and worked around the problem by creating a user-specific desktop file (at `~/.local/share/applications`):

```
[Desktop Entry]
Name=Microsoft Teams for Linux
Comment=Unofficial client for Microsoft Teams for Linux
Exec=/bin/bash /home/lina/Code/random-scripts/linux/start-teams-for-linux.sh %U
Terminal=false
Type=Application
Icon=teams-for-linux
StartupWMClass=teams-for-linux
MimeType=application/sip;
Categories=Chat;Network;Office;
```

And here's the Bash script:

```bash
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

"/opt/Teams for Linux/teams-for-linux" "$@" &

TEAMS_PID=$!

while ps -p "$TEAMS_PID" >/dev/null; do

    sleep 5

    TEAMS_WINDOWS=($(xwininfo -tree -root | grep -E '\("teams for linux" "Teams for Linux"\)[ 0-9+x-]*$' | grep -Eio '\b0x[0-9a-f]+\b')) || continue

    for TEAMS_WINDOW in "${TEAMS_WINDOWS[@]}"; do

        xprop -id "$TEAMS_WINDOW" -f WM_CLASS 8u -set WM_CLASS "teams-for-linux"

    done

done
```

Even this is imperfect, as Plank still associates the app's windows with the system-wide .desktop file, so you can't "pin" your user-specific launcher. Always launch from the Applications Menu.

```2019-07-15 16:29:18 ➜  Apps teams-for-linux                                                      
Error org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.Failed: cannot use setting "default-url-scheme-handler": not allowed

**Describe the bug**
Snap app does not appear to respect yargs

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Fresh install of Kubuntu
2. Install teams-for-linux snap from KDE Discover
3. Run `teams-for-linux --closeAppOnCross true` from terminal
4. Try to close teams-for-linux by closing window
5. Closes to system tray but app stays open

**Expected behaviour**
If yargs are not supported in snap app, then running from terminal should return "unexpected parameter" error message.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Kubuntu 19.04
 - snap
 - 0.30

**Additional context**
I'm not 100% sure whether yargs are only used when the app is installed from source but I assume that's the case. Either way it would be nice to be able to specify launch parameters for the snap or debian apps. Unless I'm doing something wrong?

Ideally, it would be great to have a simple preferences dialogue that lets novice users configure the launch options from there.

issues.title: Webcam is not automatically connected in snap
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When installing the snap (on ubuntu 19.04), the camera is not automatically connected, so the webcam is not available for usage during calls.

**To Reproduce**

1. snap install teams-for-linux
2. start the app

```
➜ snap connections teams-for-linux 
Interface               Plug                                    Slot                     Notes
browser-support         teams-for-linux:browser-support         :browser-support         -
camera                  teams-for-linux:camera                  -                        -
desktop                 teams-for-linux:desktop                 :desktop                 -
desktop-legacy          teams-for-linux:desktop-legacy          :desktop-legacy          -
gsettings               teams-for-linux:gsettings               :gsettings               -
home                    teams-for-linux:home                    :home                    -
network                 teams-for-linux:network                 :network                 -
opengl                  teams-for-linux:opengl                  :opengl                  -
pulseaudio              teams-for-linux:pulseaudio              :pulseaudio              -
screen-inhibit-control  teams-for-linux:screen-inhibit-control  :screen-inhibit-control  -

issues.title: App window fails to re-open after closure
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
In ElementaryOS 5 (I can't speak for other distros) if the Teams window is closed the app window won't re-open again (when the icon is clicked absolutely nothing happens). If an app launch is attempted from the command line the following is output is sent (and the app window still fails to open)

```
mickeymouse@home:~/abc$ teams-for-linux
Gtk-Message: Failed to load module "pantheon-filechooser-module"
Gtk-Message: Failed to load module "canberra-gtk-module"
Gtk-Message: Failed to load module "canberra-gtk-module"
App already running

```

The app can be reopened if all the 'teams-for-linux' processes are killed. After killing them  'teams-for-linux' can be used successfully from the command line to start the app however note that the Gtk warnings appears then too (using the Teams icon will open the app too)

**To Reproduce**

From a clean boot:

1. Open the Teams for Linux app
2. Close the app using the standard window-close X in the window title bar
3. Try and restart/reopen the app by clicking the Teams icon or running 'teams-for-linux' from the command line
4. The app will fail to start/open

**Expected behaviour**
Each time the app icon is clicked the app window opens and Teams is running and functioning within it

**Desktop**
 - OS: Elementary OS 5.0 Juno
 - Installation package: snap
 - Version: 0.3.0

teams-for-linux to run with electron 5.x

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
-

**Additional context**
This is been develop under the branch called `feature/electron-5-migration`. At the moment the following problems have appeared:
* Screenshare is not working.
* Spellchecker is not working.

issues.title: Spellchecker related improvements
issues.body:
The following improvements has been done in order to tackle #149 

* There is now the option to disable the spellchecker. That also disables the rightClick menu as they are pretty much one for each other. You can use the config option `rightClickWithSpellcheck` to disable it. By default is enabled.
* I have re-implemented the spellchecker removing the implementation from electron-spell-check-provider.

In principle, with the second part, the issue of things going pretty slow should be fixed. In any case, there is now the option to disable the spellchecker menu.  

this fixes also #123

issues.title: Join meeting as Guest by using URL
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I currently use windows virtual machine in order to join teams meeting as a Guest/Anonymous using a web browser. Tried the solution in #47 but that did not work (pasted the link to a chat, then tried clicking or ctrl+clicking - got a new teams windows which was forever initializing), but the point is that I'd like to join the meeting as guest instead of my teams account.
I've tried passing the url as a command like parameter (teams-for-linux -url https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_ID%40thread.skype/0?context=SOME_CONTEXT) - the teams-for-linux opened with sign in page instead of with a 'Join on the web instead' button.


**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like to be able to join a meeting using url as a guest without needing to sign-in to Microsoft account. Preferably url should be entered using UI, but command line works too.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Changing user-agent in firefox gives me audio, but I can't see video - what others are screen sharing, so I though maybe teams-for-linux would give me video, but guest joining doesn't work so can't test that..

**Additional context**
This is how the process looks like in browser.
1. Open the url and see the screen which allos to open desktop app or join on the web (since this is firefox in ubuntu with modified user-agent it shows only one button):
![Screenshot from 2019-06-17 11-58-16](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19952587/59591842-71ad1080-90f7-11e9-9528-b201a31ec6a5.png)
2. By pressing join on the web, you are presented with Guest/Anonymous join window, were you enter your name and set audio/video parameters:
![Screenshot from 2019-06-17 11-58-52](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19952587/59591906-9903dd80-90f7-11e9-82c9-ac5c624a14b4.png)
And after pressing that join now button you are in the meeting.

issues.title: Typing becomes really slow on 0.3.0
issues.body:
![Capture d’écran de 2019-06-14 10-51-19](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44399944/59496741-67d6a380-8e92-11e9-966b-ccb9a8cdfd84.png)
![Capture d’écran de 2019-06-14 10-49-37](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44399944/59496654-3362e780-8e92-11e9-8f4d-e4b68eecdba6.png)
**Describe the bug**
When typing a message to someone, the more words not recognized by the dictionary I type, the slower the app becomes (100 %CPU after ~20 words)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Conversation
2. Start writing a message to someone
3. Type words that are not recognized by the spell checker 
4. After  a dozen of non-recognized words, the app begins to hit 100 % cpu and gets slower

**Expected behavior**
Spell Checking should not have such an incidence on the typing speed. The issue doesn't occur on the web client.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.04
 - Installation package deb
 - Version 0.3.0

**Additional context**
The issue started when I upgraded form 0.2.0 to 0.3.0. 
I haven't installed any dictionary or stuff related to the spell checker for this app, so the default language is english

This is quite annoying, since I type both in french and english, and typing more than two sentences in french becomes really hard.
EDIT : Teams seems to recognize french, so the issue occurs just because I type too many misspelled words, like without accents.

By the way, a lot of thanks for this app !! :)

issues.title: Permission denied errors
issues.body:
I keep having to remove and reinstall this snap package.  Everything will be fine for a few days and then all of a sudden, usually in the middle of a video conference, because that's my luck, things will stop functioning (i.e. selecteable/clickable items aren't responding).  I will restart the client but it fails to launch.  Launching from the command line yields:

`/home/local/DOMAIN/USERNAME/snap/teams-for-linux/15: Permission denied`

Everything worked fine when this was in the beta channel and using the `--classic` option when installing.  Now I get this:

`Warning: flag --classic ignored for strictly confined snap teams-for-linux`
`teams-for-linux 0.3.0 from Julian Alarcon (alarconj) installed`
`Channel beta for teams-for-linux is closed; temporarily forwarding to stable.`

This may be a snap issue and not an issue with this project but I'm brand new to the snap game.  My apologies if this is not the right avenue for this.

I'm running Linux Mint 18.3 if that makes any difference.

Cheers and thanks for your time spent on this project.

issues.title: Need to kill the process after closing the window
issues.body:
In version 0.3.0, we found on our system we need to kill the process after closing the window.  If we don't kill the process, we can't restart Teams for Linux from the launcher.

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

- Open the app by: Go to Unity launcher, search 'teams', click on 'Teams for Linux'
(note Teams for Linux opens)
- log in
- Click on red x icon in the window title bar to close Teams for Linux
(Note that the app window closes)
- Open the app again by: Go to Unity launcher, search 'teams', click on 'Teams for Linux'
(Note that nothing happens.  No window opens.)
- Open terminal and enter:

`ps -x | grep teams`

(Note that 5 related processes are found)
` 3457 ?        Sl     1:36 /snap/teams-for-linux/15/teams-for-linux`

`3600 ?        S      0:00 /snap/teams-for-linux/15/teams-for-linux --type=zygote --no-sandbox`

` 3857 ? Thank you for all the great work. I use the teams for linux daily.

Do not get any sound notification for incoming messages, meeting invites etc. I have sound notifications enabled.

To have sound notifications in the incoming messages. Notification sound be played.

Not available

issues.title: Tray icon is missing padding
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Tray icon is missing some padding, so it looks larger then the rest of tray icons and also sticked to the neighbors.
**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add some padding to the tray icon

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3274951/58451626-cab40500-8146-11e9-9f9c-482c89420642.png)

# General notes

This release is been mostly focus on automating the release to snapcraft. 

Also, now the spellchecker works with a local version of hunspell installed (if located under /usr/share/hunspell/)

The icon has been also updated to the latest MS one.

A couple of new features added to be able to start the app minimised and having the webDebug option in the tray menu.

# Detail changes

* #92 Using gitflow from now on for releases (having master and develop branches) (Thanks to @julian-alarcon)
* #74 automating the snap release (Thanks to @julian-alarcon and @benyanke)
* #121 Fixing snap and camera not working together (Thanks to @julian-alarcon)
* #118, #113 and #119 Unpacking the spellchecker dictionaries and adding the ones installed in hunspell (Thanks to @vadimi and @noderat )
* #134 Updating the MS icon (Thanks to @julian-alarcon)
* #91 removing the apparmor=DENIED messages in syslog for snap build (Thanks to @julian-alarcon)
* #137 Adding the --minimized option to start the app minimized (Thanks to @didster)
* #135 Adding webDebug to the tray menu options (Thanks to @Fmstrat)

Also:
* #109 At the moment the propagation of 'about:blank' links has been disabled to minimise annoyance from this issue.

And the packages versions has been updated a bit, as we got a blocker for moving to version 5 or electron.


# Thanks

To everyone involved from people reporting issues, testing solutions and implementing them. You all make this possible.

issues.title: icon in Unity app switcher not found
issues.body:
In version 2.1, when switching apps in Unity (alt+tab), the icon shows as a question mark.  This was fixed by changing the below line.

File: /var/lib/snapd/desktop/applications/teams-for-linux_teams-for-linux.desktop
From:
`StartupWMClass=teams-for-linux`
To:
`StartupWMClass=teams for linux`

issues.title: msteams URIs are not opened (for instance for online Microsoft Teams meetings)
issues.body:
msteams URIs - which are, for instance, produced by calendar invites for Microsoft Teams meetings - are not "opened". 

One example of such an msteams URI is
msteams:/l/meetup-join/19:meeting_MmQ5NmViYjktOGU5Zi00Mjc3LWFlNDAtYWZkMmZmZjM3MmE1@thread.v2/0?context=%7B%22Tid%22%3A%228947597c-56ab-40dc-8bc2-yyyyyyyyyyyyy%22%2C%22Oid%22%3A%224b885135-a86e-45d9-acdc-zzzzzzzzzzzz%22%7D&anon=true&deeplinkId=xxxxxxxxxx-5035-4a1e-a907-fe766d6a7161&launchAgent=join_launcher&type=meetup-join&directDl=true&msLaunch=true&enableMobilePage=true&fqdn=teams.microsoft.com

I extracted this URI from Chromium's internal handling of the calendar request, with some random redacting (xx, yy, zz) in the above sample.

Going through Firefox on Linux, and a (redacted) calendar event (for the above) of https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_MmQ5NmViYjktOGU5Zi00Mjc3LWFlNDAtYWZkMmZmZjM3MmE1%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%228947597c-56ab-40dc-8bc2-yyyyyyyyyy%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%224b885135-a86e-45d9-acdc-zzzzzzzzzzz%22%7d I get the "open link with ..." xdg-open request.
Directing this request towards teams-for-linux (RPM, 0.2), brings the Teams client to front correctly, but does not switch to the online meeting session.

issues.title: Basic HTTP auth popup dialog cannot be completed
issues.body:
Hi,

I have another issue that just started manifesting itself. 

The issue is that I am working in a corporate environment where we have an internal authentication server doing basic HTTP auth followed by requiring O365 2FA which has been working flawlessly until now (since it was fixed like a year ago).

What is happening is that I do get the basic auth popup in the client and can fill in username and password but nothing happens when I try to submit.  I do not remember if the OK button in the pop up should give visual feedback when being clicked but it certainly does not now.

I can work around this by logging in outside the corporate network using the normal O365 login procedure complete with 2FA.

Best regards,
/Erik

issues.title: Audio/video calls suddenly unsupported
issues.body:
Hi,

First of all, thank you for working so hard on the Teams client.  I have been using it for quite some time and have been very happy to see the development done on it.

The last days I did however discover a strange thing:  When trying to call someone in the chat view, I get the "this feature is not yet implemented, please download the desktop app" message.  At the same time it works fine to join a teams meeting with audio and video.

Version is 0.2.1 installed from a deb built from git sources yesterday (i did not work on the previous build I had either).  Host is running Ubuntu 18.10.

I do not know if electron has dependencies on the system Chrome browser but it struck me today that I recently uninstalled a home brew chromium build installed from deb and replaced it with a development version supplied as a snap package. However, uninstalling the snap did not improve things and the regular Chrome browser i have had installed from the official Chrome repo has not been changed.

I also tried deleting ".config/Teams for Linux", should it contain anything that did not agree with new components to no avail.

I guess that Microsoft may have changed something as well but as I do no longer have the old built debs I cannot go back to verify.

Best regards,
/Erik

issues.title: AppImage of 0.2.0 does not terminate cleanly, ever
issues.body:
The AppImage of 0.2.0 does not terminate cleanly, ever, hangs dead on shutdown.

This was on Fedora 29 and KDE, but also applies to Fedora 30 and KDE.

Steps:
* download AppImage
* open terminal in location where AppImage is located
* chmod u+x
* ./<the>.AppImage <- launch the AppImage from the terminal
* (proceed to successfully use the AppImage)
* click on "X" button in upper right corner of (main) window
//exp: AppImage terminates cleanly, terminal prompt reappear
//act: AppImage never terminates cleanly; terminal is blocked for entry by the AppImage

At this stage, note that process resources still linger (ps -ef from other console)

* Press Ctrl-C on terminal where AppImage hangs on shutdown
-> AppImage processes terminate, resources get cleaned up
-> terminal becomes unblocked for entry

I am using a terminal above, because that makes the effect much more observable; it seems to happen, too, if / when integration with the desktop environment is used (launch through KDE app launcher / menu)

labels.name: not an issue
labels.description: Not really an issue or not much we can do here

issues.title: AppImage of 0.2.0 occasionally hangs on startup.
issues.body:
The AppImage of 0.2.0 very occasionally hangs on startup, with this lovely blueish background screen that Teams has.

Note that my account on Microsoft Teams is protected by mandatory two-factor authentication (I have no idea whether this applies to all Teams accounts). My second factor is Microsoft Authenticator on Android, where I typically respond within a second or two to the authentication request. The hang takes place _after_ I successfully confirmed with the second factor.

If the process hangs, terminating the process and simply restarting the AppImage will ask for the second factor again, will then proceed to success.

After that, it seems to be very smooth sailing.

Steps:
* download 0.2.0 AppImage; chmod u+x
* start AppImage
* enter credentials (email, password) for account on Microsoft Teams
* get asked for authentication for second factor (on Microsoft Authenticator on Android)
* successfully approve with second factor
* AppImage hangs

Terminating the AppImage and starting again so far seems to always have succeed so far (with the exact same steps after startup - credentials, 2FA)

issues.title: calling does not work when run from source
issues.body:
**BUG**
When running the latest from source, the app appears.  I can login, chat, and do everything I would do from web app in Chrome on Linux.  However, initiating a chat produces the exact same messages as when run from Chrome on Linux:

```
Try a different browser
This feature isn't available yet for your browser. Try the web app with Microsoft Edge or Google Chrome, or switch to the desktop app.
```

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. clone teams-for-linux repo
2. Install missing npm modules:
```
npm install --save electron-window-state
npm install --save yargs
npm install --save electron-native-notification
```
3. Run from source:  `yarn start`
4. After logging into Teams from newly opened app window, select a buddy in "Chat" and click on "Call" (telephone) icon.

**Expected Behavior**
To make calls with audio & video using my web-cam.

**Desktop**
 - OS: Gentoo (current)
 - Installation package: source
 - Version:  a895dfc
- yarn:  1.15.2
- electron:  v4.0.7

**Additional**
I verified that my web-cam works using a native Linux app, Kamoso.

issues.title: Crash error using electron 5.0.0 on snap build
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
I'm trying the new electron version, 5.0. Has new Chromium, Node 12 and V8 engine.
https://electronjs.org/blog/electron-5-0

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour:
1. Remove yarn.lock file to generate a new one
2. Change electron version on package.json to 5.0.0
3. Build the snap package and install it
4. Run it on from a terminal, it will crash and show this message:
```
julian@mypc:~/Code/teams-for-linux$ teams-for-linux 
[31893:0502/075604.540153:FATAL:setuid_sandbox_host.cc(157)] The SUID sandbox helper binary was found, but is not configured correctly. Rather than run without sandboxing I'm aborting now. You need to make sure that /snap/teams-for-linux/x1/chrome-sandbox is owned by root and has mode 4755.
Trace/breakpoint trap (core dumped)
```

**Expected behavior**
It should run with no issues

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.04/1904
 - Installation package snap
 - Version 0.2.1

**Additional context**
Build on .deb works with no issues

issues.title: snap can no longer connect to camera
issues.body:
Previously the snap version would see my camera as I had done this after first installing:
sudo snap connect teams-for-linux:camera core:camera

However, teams-for-linux no longer sees my camera and the command now produces this error:

`error: snap "teams-for-linux" has no plug named "camera"`

I wondered if commit 226f21a had something to do with it, as 'camera' is no longer a child of the plugs property on snapcraft.yaml

issues.title: #74 deploying to snap store
issues.body:
This should implement #74 

Push commits to develop will trigger a deploy to the snap/edge store.
Push commits to master will trigger a deploy to the snap/stable store.

-- Note that the push to stable can, and will, only be tested in the next release.

Also... all commits will be squeeze into one as there are far too many.

This might also solve #96 , as electron-builder should have taken care of it.

Leaving here open for a few days so @julian-alarcon and/or @benyanke can review it.

issues.title: Unpack hunspell dictionaries during the build
issues.body:
It will keep `hunspell_dictionaries` in `app.asar.unpacked` directory which allows spell checking to work correctly.

Fixes #118

issues.title: Spell checking issue
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Spell checking only works when running app from sources. When I build it using `yarn dist` and run it spell checking is not highlighting misspelled words. I'm using default en_US language.  

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Enter any misspelled text into the message box

**Expected behavior**
en_US spell checker is working

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Xubuntu
 - Installation package: deb
 - Version 0.2.0

**Additional context**
I was able to debug it a bit. Basically spell checker is looking for dictionaries in the following directory: `......../teams-for-linux/dist/linux-unpacked/resources/app.asar.unpacked/node_modules/spellchecker/vendor/hunspell_dictionaries`. But `app.asar.unpacked` does not exist, so it falls back to `app.asar, and it cannot load dictionaries from app.asar.

issues.body:
Where the user account is a guest account there is no dialog for the 'Account verification code'.  Instead the authentication goes to the password entry dialog.  This is no use because the account has no password.

(Note. Microsoft bizarrely does not allow work email addresses to be used to create accounts, so it is not possible to register the account and set a password outside of teams).

**To Reproduce**
Login as new user or user with no Azure AD account.
Password dialog is displayed

issues.title: Messages marked as read when window is not focused
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Lets say I send a message to Frank and change the focus to another app (leaving ms teams on frank's chat). When Frank replies, there will be no icon badge or notification. This also happens in team rooms.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Send a message to Someone.
2. Change the focus to another app.
3. Receive reply from Someone.
4. No red dot on the app's icon or notification.

**Expected behavior**
A red dot should appear on the app's icon and a notification.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.10
 - Installation package: deb, snap
 - Version: 0.2

**Additional context**
It looks like it is simply not aware that the window lost focus, so it marks chats as read. If I change to another chat before unfocusing, it works. Also, if I "hide" the window using the tray menu, it works. Minimizing the window also solves the issue.

labels.name: wontfix
labels.description: This will not be worked on

issues.title: Unable to Authenticate using NTLMv2
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
The company I work for has disabled NTLMv1 and requires NTLMv2 only.
Since this change, I have been unable to authenticate in teams-for-linux 0.2.1, but am able to login using Chromium 73.0.3683.103

I've attempted manually running **teams-for-linux --ntlmV2enabled true** and have tried enabling the setting in **.config/teams-for-linux/config.json**.  No success in either case.

Attempts to authenticate with teams-for-linux locks out my account.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Enter the username/password when prompted
2. Hit 'Submit'
3. The teams-for-linux interface refreshes, and prompts for credentials again

**Expected behavior**
Should be able to login successfully after entering credentials

**Screenshots**
Nothing useful via screenshot

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Arch Linux 5.0.7-arch1-1-ARCH
 - Installation package https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/teams-for-linux/
 - Version 0.2.1

issues.title: Electron starting args in config.json not honoured
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

Our linux users must declare a proxy with basic authentication in their system settings to work conveniently in our internal environment.

Using such settings with team-for-linux is not expected to work as Electron does not allow basic auth for proxy in the url. (Note: this causes teams-for-linux to enter an infinite respawning loop for which I might open an other ticket, or reopen the existing one for the same kind of issue).

To overcome this, we have a working solution which is to override proxy settings in Electron to use the enterprise provided proxy pac file.

There is absolutely no issue using this from the command line:
```bash
teams-for-linux --proxy-pac-url=https://pac.server.com/file.pac
```
However, putting this parameter in the config.json file for a fresh install is not taken into account (I get stuck in infinite respawning loop).

To be noted: once TFL has been started successfully once from the command line (and has created its partition, etc...), next start without params (e.g. from the start menu) is ok

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

Note: this will unfortunatelly need a proxy *really* requiring authentication + a proxy pac file available to be tested (a very simple local squid install + a fake proxy pac file answering always "DIRECT" should do)
1. Cleanup config dir `rm -rf /home/user/.config/teams-for-linux/!(config.json)`
1. Make sure you have a `proxy-pac-url` param in `/home/user/.config/teams-for-linux/config.json`
    ```json
    {
        "proxy-pac-url": "http://proxy.pac.com/file.pac"
    }
    ```
1. Make sure your system proxy settings are pointing to a proxy-url with basic auth required
1. Start teams-for-linux normally (e.g. by clicking its icon in applications)

Result => TFL enters an infinit respawning loop which means that the proxy-pac-url was not taken into account.

**Expected behavior**
TFL start correctly taking into account the proxy-par-url parameter in config.json

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu bionic 18.04
 - Installation package: reproduced on both deb package and from source
 - Version: 0.2.0

labels.name: not an issue
labels.description: Not really an issue or not much we can do here

issues.title: Allow single screen sharing for multi-monitors
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Under windows or mac with the desktop application, it is possible to share a single opened application, the entire desktop or, if you have multiple monitors, a single monitor. This is not possible in teams for linux.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

If this is not a limitation of the web version, I'd like to have the choice of sharing the entire desktop or only one of the monitors in a multi-monitor setup.

issues.title: Unable to use GitHub connector buttons
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Clicking buttons on posts made by the GitHub connector has no effect. 

Using [Ctrl] + click opens in a new mini browser window with no addressbar instead of opening in my default browser.

Right-clicking opens a context menu with options like "Copy" but clicking "Copy" does not copy the URL (or anything) to the clipboard. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to a channel with posts from the GitHub connector
2. Click on any button
3. Watch nothing happen

Opening the console and performing the above shows lots of errors like `Authorization error: Nonce in profile is not the same as whats in storage`. 

**Expected behavior**
1. Go to a channel with posts from the GitHub connector
2. Click on any button
3. See GitHub page in default browser

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Fedora 29 GNOME
 - Installation package: rpm
 - Version 0.2.0

issues.title: Merge pull request #104 from IsmaelMartinez/develop

issues.body:
# General notes

From this release onward, we are following SemVer. Also, we have unfork and tidy up a bit the project to ease contributions.

There are a few other changes in this release detailed next.

# Detailed changes

* #81 Fixing an issue when clicking on the "Open" in the tray menu did not focus the window. Thanks to @alexeiskachykhin for fixing it!
* #94 Adding the ability to provide certificate fingerprints for self signed certs. Thanks to @zeitounator and @charlycoste for their work. That fixes #93.
* #77 Adding some default custom CSS templates. Available customCSSName templates are in the [config readme](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/app/config/README.md) file and you can get them to run with `teams-for-linux --customCSSName compactLight`
* #80 Fixing an issue where the yargs was not picking up the config file. Now it is possible to have a config.json file with the config changes that you need. Refer to the [config readme](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/app/config/README.md) for more info.
* #83 Added a wee screen that shows what you are sharing. It is quite dump but does the job.
* #98 Adding the ability to stop the onlineOfflineRefresh introduced in version 0.1.13 as it seems to produce an infinite loop in some scenarios. `teams-for-linux --onlineOfflineReload=false`
* #100 Adding a few extra browser tweaks to try to solve the issue with the microphone and screen sharing not working if not having a webcam.
* #78 Decided to go down the SemVer route.
* #87 Community decided to unfork.
* #95 Created a code of conduct (thanks to #87)

The main README.md file has been also clean up and re-structure to split the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md), [HISTORY.md](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/HISTORY.md) and [KNOWN_ISSUES.md](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/develop/KNOWN_ISSUES.md)

Updated versions of electron and the other packages that we have in the package.json file.

And last, but not least, added a bug and feature request templates in order to ease that process.

Thanks to everybody involved!

issues.title: Not able to Screen Share with Audio only (without having a webcam)
issues.body:
---
name: Bug report
about: Create a report to help us improve
title: 'Screen Sharing not working simultaneously with audio'
labels: ''
assignees: ''

---

**Describe the bug**
In 1 on 1 calls, I am unable to use screen sharing while also using the same call to talk to the other person. I am unable to use screen sharing at all in group calls.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
I have been able to reproduce the error in the snap --edge install, running from source on v0.1.17, master, and develop.

For 1 on 1 calling:
1. Start a call using "Start Screen Share"
2. Select window I want to screen share (All options reproduce the behavior)
3. The person being called receives the call immediately and is able to see the screen. The caller doesn't join the call until about 30 seconds later and the call screen is shown.
4. If I change nothing at this point, the called person is able to see screen, but no audio from either side. The call screen tells me to unmute in order to start talking.
5. If I click the unmute button, screen sharing turns off but audio now works.

Additionally, I cannot start screen sharing from inside the call. The option tells me my browser is incompatible and prompts me to download teams.exe.

For channel calling:
1. Start channel call (there is no option to start a channel call with screen sharing)
2. Screen sharing option is greyed out.

**Expected behavior**
I am expecting to have screen sharing to work with audio in both 1 on 1 and group calls.

**Screenshots**
Let me know what you'd like screen shots of. I am unsure what to grab that will be useful to you.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Ubuntu 18.04
 - Installation package: snap, from source, deb
 - Version: v0.1.17, master, develop

**Additional context**
1. Am I doing something wrong?
2. How are you using screen sharing?
3. Does screen sharing work in channel calls?
4. Are there potentially any dependency differences I may be running in to?

issues.title: Blank page on saml login with self signed certificate
issues.body:
Hi,

First of all thank you and all other forks members for this work.

OS: Ubuntu 18.04 (bionic) x86_64
TFL version: 0.1.17
Installation method: deb package downloaded from releases page

I currently work for a customer using teams linked to O365 implementing it's own external authentication mechanism. When I launch teams-for-linux, I can enter my organisation email and get the normal message "We are redirecting you to you organisation's login page" (which I have traced to be https://idpriam.groupe.extra.laposte.fr/auth/dologin.jsp in case it really matters). But the screen is desperately blank, I'm never offered a chance to enter my credentials.

This totally works as expected if I launch teams in a browser (either chrome or firefox).

Around the coffee machine, I've learned that several of my colleagues have tried team-for-linux on the same (or nearly identical) configuration in the past months and are facing the exact same issue.

I have tried the remedies that were proposed in #18 but it did no help.

I'm brand new to your app and I didn't yet take any time to look at the app contents or to go further than reported above. Meanwhile, my job implies debugging lots of different things so I definitely can capture more info. Though, I would appreciate a small initial assistance to put myself on track.

The customer I mentioned is a 200k+ people company with an echosystem of several thousands developers/techs/pms. My team is very activelly pushing (officially and underground) to let all these people having AT LAST the ability to choose their OS. Having teams working correctly is the last rock in our shoe to be fully connected with the enterprise information system and deploy at large for some hundred devs desperately waiting.

Looking forward to cooperate, cheers !

**Edit**: it turns out the real problem is not the enterprise login but a self signed certificate delivered only when connecting from the company network. Logins from outside the site are ok (with a fully trusted ssl certificate).

issues.title: Use gitflow to organize contributions and separate develop from releases
issues.body:
Hi @IsmaelMartinez @benyanke 

I'm checking the current flow and I think that it's better to use a **develop branch** where things can break, and focus on getting releases branches when we think that a new release is needed (bug fixes, new features, etc).
So the idea will be:
All most all commits on **develop** -> Release branch e.g. **0.2** release.
When *release is done* tag it and then *merge to master* (so master will always have the latest stable release, *every commit on master is a new release* by definition).

If someone want to contribute, or even us (feature/bugfix), the branch to use will be **develop** always.

I more detailed explanation can be found here: https://nvie.com/posts/a-successful-git-branching-model/

I already created the develop branch from master, what do you think about this?

issues.title: Support opening meeting links via xdg-open
issues.body:
Visiting a teams meeting link in my browser doesn't automatically open it in `teams-for-linux`. Issue #47 provides a workaround to this, but it'd be much better if simply clicking the link worked.

`xdg-open` provides a mechanism for this, and I believe the "download the app or open in browser" page that you see when you first visit the link already attempts to open the official Teams app via a similar mechanism (though I could be mistaken). If this assumption is correct, it's simply a matter of `teams-for-linux` registering itself via `xdg-mime` or `xdg-settings`.

issues.title: Unfork or continue as it is?
issues.body:
Hi,

Time has arrive to decide if to unfork or continue as we are.

Please tell me what you thing.
👍 for unfork
👎  for keep as we are.

The main reason for the unfork are:
* almost a year since last message from ivelkov. James repo is also archived now.
* this repo has now not much in common with ivelkov one nor with James one.
* there are a few features in github that are only available for main projects (no forks)

And reasons to keep as we are:
* there are a lot of links in Internet pointing to ivelkov and then, indirectly to our repo.
* Once MS does the Linux client, this repo will become obsolete. So what is the point of the extra “work”.
* unexpected consequences... in principle, by using the same name, there should not be issues with regards links to the repo, etc, but experience tell me  expect issues in any change.

In any case, I will keep the history section on this repo, but probably move it to a separate HISTORY.md file (just to keep things tidy.)

issues.title: Improve the UI in capture selector screen
issues.body:
The current UI in the capture selector is far from great. 

Improvements that I think can be made are:

* Change the https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/master/app/browser/desktopShare/captureSelector.js#L6 to be a frameless window.
** That implies adding, at least, a close button that will close the child window.
* Create a UI similar to the one of chrome screen selection:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/430992/54954951-df57ff00-4f43-11e9-9d13-a5443cb5384d.png)
Note: Ignore the "chrome tab"

Information on how to create a frameless window is in here:
https://electronjs.org/docs/api/frameless-window

issues.title: Ability to see what is been shared
issues.body:
It will be great to be able to see what it is sharing. 

Similar to what Skype does (small view in the corner).

Technically that requires the following:
1. Under https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/tree/master/app/browser/desktopShare , create a new child view similar to the captureSelector.html. That will have a video tag for the video to stream to.
2. Modify the https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/master/app/browser/desktopShare/chromeApi.js#L9 to also stream the video to the page created in point 1. An example can be found in the desktop-capturer electron page https://electronjs.org/docs/api/desktop-capturer

Note, it might be needed to modify the captureSelector to also return the source type (desktop/screen).

issues.title: Implemented reliable tray updates
issues.body:
Hey @IsmaelMartinez this pull request addresses the problem that I observed on Ubuntu 16.04.5 where a tray icon wouldn't update on unread messages. This implementation relies on Teams API to get notified instead of obtaining unread notification based on page title change.

I tested it on Ubuntu 16.04.5 and Ubuntu 18.04.2.

issues.body:
Hello Ismael.

Your application is great!!, i've been using the ivelkov version for a while and i've discover today yours. You have done a great job.

The only thing i miss is, when i'm in a multi video call the ivelkov version shows all the members of the conversation, your version only shows the one who's talking, i think is much better the multi view option. 
Maybe this is related with the user agent, is there any way to change this behaviour?.

Thanks in advance for your work!.

issues.title: Low quality in screen sharing on 1 to 1 calls
issues.body:
When using screen sharing between only 2 peers the quality of the screen is pretty low.

If the screen sharing is between 3 or more people the quality is great with no issues. If you are in a one and one  screen sharing and invite to someone else the quality wont be improved, you have to cancel and reshare the screen to fix the quality issue.

I'm using Snap build on Ubuntu 18.04 with Xorg (no Wayland) using version 0.1.16 of teams-for-linux.

Attached is an screenshot that show the issue.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30119472/54383772-d1ab9b00-4660-11e9-9745-f1effd891979.png)

issues.title: Huge Huge thank you!
issues.body:
Ability to share screen should help me to keep using my sweet Linux as a work laptop instead of that darn mac.
It's hard to overestimate how pleased I am. 
Thank you, sir. really really appreciated.

First off, thank you for working on this. I really do appreciate it.

Unfortunately with both v0.1.15 and master one-on-one calls to other users results in the message;

> Try a different browser
> This feature isn’t available yet for your browser. Try the web app with Microsoft Edge or Google Chrome, or switch to the desktop app.

Unfortunately, I think the fix for #21 is causing the problem. I have verified that adding the missing `angular.element(document).injector().get('callingSupportService').oneOnOneCallingEnabled = true;` from #21 does not fix the issue, but I've not yet had the time to dig in further.

I'll try and see if I have time at some point in the next few days. Figured I'd create the issue just incase someone's already on it?

issues.title: Custom CSS
issues.body:

issues.title: Electron spellchecker
issues.body:
Adding a basic spellchecker and right click menu functionality by using the following libraries:

* [electron-spell-check-provider](https://www.npmjs.com/package/electron-spell-check-provider)
* [electron-editor-context-menu](https://github.com/mixmaxhq/electron-editor-context-menu)

This is to fix #28 . The functionality is fairly basic. We get the locale from the OS using app.getLocale()

This is until electron implements a proper hook to the OS spellchecker or chrome adds a public API for their spellchecker capabilities.

issues.title: Refactoring
issues.body:
1st pass of the refactoring.

A part from moving things around and improving the documentation, the following have been modified:

* The parameter displayDestopNotifications is now displayDesktopNotificationsHack , as it is a hack.
* I have invert the changes from version 0.1.10 and now all windows open in an external browser. If you need to open a window in the electron browser, you can use 'Crl+Click'.
* I have added also a very basic eslint 
* Removing the two levels app as it was just a nightmare to update the versions and we don't really need two levels.

Version have been increase to 0.1.11 but it might need to wait for a few days in order to be released as I might accommodate more changes (index.js file still looks a bit messy IMO)

issues.body:
* Fixing issue #12 where you couldn't drag and drop images anymore.

issues.title: Double notifications
issues.body:
For every message I get two desktop notifications, one is personalized (with sender's photo, message, etc), the other one is generic useless one.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1177900/47846886-b9319700-ddc9-11e8-85ac-812a45fda446.png)

I tracked the generic notification to this piece:

https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/blob/99621c5f8e9942aabdc36a96bc7a7e9585da4361/app/lib/index.js#L65-L78

Commenting out that entire block solves the issue for me, now I just have one beautiful notification.

Arch Linux, dunst notifications manager, running with ` yarn start --partition persist:teams-for-linux`

issues.title: drag and drop not working
issues.body: Drag and drop is not working after changes for version 0.1.8.

* Adding ntlmV2enabled and authServerWhitelist options to the configuration and parameters. More information in the README.md file.
* Implementing issue #10 . Because of it, it is not needed nor recommended to be able to setup the userAgent as an argument. It should be now possible to use video and mentions with the app.

issues.title: @mentions to work with video/edge
issues.body:
Mentions only work with chrome userAgent. Unfortunately you need edge as userAgent to be able to have video calls. 

The following [issue number 8](https://github.com/JamieMagee/teams-for-linux/issues/8
) from JamieMagee repository, indicates that it is possible to have it both working together.

I believe I have seen it temporarily working what it will mean it could be a difference between electron 3 and 1.8 (probably also 2).

Any work related to this, will be added to this thread.
trying to fix #6 by following the recommendation from https://github.com/yargs/yargs/blob/master/docs/api.md#argv
